the last chapter has ended,
but the next one hasn't started.

You're standing on the page in between —
blank, no words, no one telling you what's supposed to go there.

For the first time,
no syllabus,
no deadline,
no "next semester will be better" to lean on.

That page is yours to write.
Slowly is fine.
Getting it wrong is fine.
Just don't leave it blank too long.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's adrift after graduation, or post as a story — names the in-between feeling that nobody warned you about, while still pushing gently forward
variations: You're not lost. You're just on the page that hasn't been written yet.
The hardest part of graduating isn't the new chapter. It's the blank page in between that no one teaches you how to live in.

items.sourceNote: 受短caption「Plot twist: I graduated」啟發，擴寫成內心OS版

zh-tw.content:
劇情大轉彎：

那個翹早八翹到教授記得他名字的人，
那個期末前一天還在問同學「我們這學期上過什麼」的人，
那個從大一就在說「我可能讀不完」的人——

畢業了。

而且還拿了學位證書，是真的，不是 P 的。

所以人生請相信一件事：
再廢的人，都有走到終點的可能性。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照 IG caption 神器，特別適合那種「我自己都不敢相信自己畢業了」的人，最後一句把搞笑變成意外的勵志
variations: 如果連我都畢業了，你也一定可以。
證明一件事：低空飛過也是飛過。

en.content:
Plot twist:

The one who skipped 8am classes so often the professor remembered his name,
the one who asked his classmates the day before finals "wait, what did we cover this semester?",
the one who said since freshman year "I'm probably not finishing this" —

graduated.

And has the diploma to prove it. Real one. Not Photoshopped.

So trust this:
even the messiest plot still gets to the ending.
en.editorNote: Perfect IG caption for graduation photos — especially the 'I genuinely cannot believe I made it' energy, and the last line turns the joke into a quiet reassurance
variations: If I graduated, you definitely will.
Confirmed: barely passing still counts as passing.

items.sourceNote: 受2026流行短caption「In my graduated era」啟發，擴寫成有故事感的內心獨白

zh-tw.content:
正式進入：「我畢業了 era」。

這個 era 的特徵包括：
履歷投了 47 份還沒回音、
爸媽問你「然後呢」的時候會突然手機沒電、
以及偶爾在凌晨三點打開 LinkedIn 又默默關掉。

但沒關係，
每個 era 都有自己的主題曲，
我這個 era 的主題曲是——「再給我一點時間」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 完美 IG caption 給那些剛畢業還在迷茫期的人，把流行的「era」用法配上現實感，自嘲又療癒
variations: 我現在在「畢業了但還在加載中」這個 era。
新 era 解鎖：每天起床都要問自己今天想做什麼人。

en.content:
Officially entering: my graduated era.

Features of this era include:
sending out 47 resumes and hearing back from zero,
mysterious phone battery deaths whenever parents ask "so what's next?",
and occasional 3am LinkedIn opens followed by quiet closes.

But every era has a theme song,
and mine right now is —
"give me a minute."
en.editorNote: Drop-in IG caption for the post-grad limbo crowd — leans into the 'era' trend but adds real-life texture, equal parts self-deprecating and comforting
variations: Currently in my 'graduated but still buffering' era.
New era unlocked: waking up every day and deciding who I want to be.

items.sourceNote: 受B.B. King「The beautiful thing about learning is that no one can take it away from you」啟發，擴寫成更個人化的版本

zh-tw.content:
他們會收回宿舍鑰匙。
會收回學生證、收回圖書館借書權限、
甚至連你最愛的那個自助餐廳也不再讓你刷卡進場。

但有一樣東西他們收不回去——

你這幾年學會的所有事。

包括怎麼念那一科你以為會被當的科目、
包括怎麼跟難相處的組員合作、
包括怎麼在凌晨四點還相信自己。

那些東西，是你的。
沒有人有權力收回去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮致詞或寫給好友的卡片，用「收回」這個動作做反差，把『學到的東西帶得走』講得不老套
variations: 證書會放抽屜，但你學會的能力，會跟你一輩子。
畢業之後你會發現：最有用的不是成績，是你逼自己撐過去的那些夜晚。

en.content:
They'll take back the dorm key.
The student ID, the library access,
even the meal card you used at your favorite dining hall.

But there's one thing they can't take back —

everything you actually learned.

How to study for the class you were sure would fail you.
How to work with the group member who never replied.
How to keep believing in yourself at 4am.

That stuff is yours.
Nobody has the right to take it.
en.editorNote: Works for a graduation speech or a card to a close friend — frames 'what you got to keep' through what they're taking away, so the sentiment lands without feeling cliche
variations: The diploma goes in a drawer. The skills you built getting it stay with you for life.
After graduation, you realize the grades weren't the point — the nights you got through were.

items.sourceNote: 受「saying goodbye to college」主題啟發，重新寫成具體場景版的告別

zh-tw.content:
最後一次走出那間教室。
最後一次經過那個總是壞掉的飲水機。
最後一次跟管理員阿姨說「謝謝」。

奇怪的是，這些「最後一次」當下你都不知道。
你只是像平常一樣走出去，
以為明天還會再來。

後來才發現——
人生很多重要的時刻，
都是事後才認得出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後回首校園的那種惆悵感，適合配畢業照或最後一次走出校門的照片，會讓人想起自己的「最後一次」
variations: 我們以為的「平常的一天」，後來都變成回憶裡最珍貴的那幾天。
重要的事情，常常是離開了才知道有多重要。

en.content:
The last time walking out of that classroom.
The last time passing the water fountain that never worked.
The last time saying "thanks" to the front desk lady on your way out.

The strange thing is — you didn't know they were the last times.
You just walked out like always,
assuming tomorrow would bring more of the same.

It's only later that you realize:
the most important moments in life
usually only get recognized in hindsight.
en.editorNote: Captures the bittersweet 'last walk through campus' feeling — pair with a final-day photo and it'll make people think of their own quiet last times
variations: The days that felt ordinary turn out to be the ones we miss the most.
Sometimes you only know it mattered after it's already gone.

items.sourceNote: 受家人寫給畢業生的卡片祝福啟發，重新寫成更口語、更有畫面感的版本

zh-tw.content:
謝謝你們，
從來沒有逼我「一定要」做什麼。

謝謝你們在我說「我想轉系」的時候，沒有翻白眼。
謝謝你們在我說「我可能讀不下去」的時候，沒有罵我。
謝謝你們在我大三那年崩潰打電話回家的那個晚上，
陪我講到我睡著。

我畢業了。
但那張證書上面，
其實也應該有你們的名字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在畢業典禮後傳給爸媽，或寫進感謝家人的小卡片，把「謝謝你們」具體化成幾個真實場景，比制式祝福有感
variations: 我畢業的不只是學校，還畢業在你們的耐心裡。
這張證書是我的，但能拿到它，是因為有你們在後面撐著。

en.content:
Thank you
for never forcing me to be anything in particular.

Thank you for not rolling your eyes when I said I wanted to switch majors.
Thank you for not yelling when I said I might not finish.
Thank you for that night junior year when I called home crying
and you stayed on the phone until I fell asleep.

I graduated.
But your names should really be on the diploma too.
en.editorNote: Send to your parents after the ceremony, or use it inside a thank-you card to family — grounding the gratitude in real moments hits harder than a generic message
variations: I didn't just graduate from school. I graduated through your patience.
The diploma has my name, but I'm only holding it because you held me up.

items.sourceNote: 受『Plot twist: I graduated』短句啟發，重寫成更有故事感的版本

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉。

那個大一被當三科、大二抱佛腳到天亮、
大三懷疑人生、大四交報告交到懷疑學校的人，

居然，
畢業了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搭配畢業照貼IG，把『畢業』包裝成一個爆冷結局，比直接寫『我畢業了』更有畫面、更好笑
variations: 劇透：那個你以為會延畢的人，是我。畢業的，也是我。
本來以為這是悲劇，沒想到結局是喜劇。

en.content:
Plot twist.

The one who flunked three classes freshman year,
pulled all-nighters sophomore year,
had an existential crisis junior year,
and turned in papers questioning if this school was even real senior year —

actually graduated.
en.editorNote: Pair this with a graduation photo on Instagram. Framing the diploma as a shocking twist ending lands better than a plain 'I did it'
variations: Spoiler: the one you thought would be stuck another semester? Yeah, that was me. The one walking the stage? Also me.
Thought this was going to be a tragedy. Turns out it's a comedy.

items.sourceNote: 受 Ursula K. Le Guin 關於不確定性的觀點啟發，重寫成更口語、更貼近現代畢業生焦慮的版本

zh-tw.content:
有人問我畢業後要幹嘛。

我說我不知道。

他用一種「你完蛋了」的眼神看著我。

但說真的，
如果我現在就知道接下來十年會怎樣，
那這場人生不就只剩走流程？

不知道，
才有空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被親戚問『畢業後要幹嘛』時拿出來反擊，或貼在IG限動回應那些焦慮——把『不知道』寫得有底氣，比強行樂觀真實
variations: 我不知道接下來要去哪，這代表我哪裡都還可以去。
迷茫不是失敗，是還沒被劇透。

en.content:
Someone asked what I'm doing after graduation.

I said I don't know.

They looked at me like I was doomed.

But honestly —
if I already knew exactly how the next ten years would go,
what would even be left to live?

Not knowing
is where the room is.
en.editorNote: Use this when relatives interrogate you about post-grad plans, or post it as a quiet flex on Instagram — owning 'I don't know' beats faking a five-year plan
variations: I don't know where I'm going next. Which means I can still go anywhere.
Being lost isn't failing. It just means the story hasn't been spoiled yet.

items.sourceNote: 受『感謝Google和Quizlet』類型的搞笑感謝句啟發，重寫成更在地、更有畫面的版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業感謝名單：

感謝那個願意分享筆記的同學。
感謝那個讓我抄考古題的學長。
感謝那家凌晨還開的便利商店。
感謝Wi-Fi從來沒在交報告那刻斷線。
感謝我自己——
至少最後那幾週，真的很拚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後貼IG或限動很合適，把搞笑感謝名單具體化成幾個學生才懂的場景，比官方致詞接地氣一百倍
variations: 我要感謝的人很多，那就謝天吧——還有便利商店、咖啡因，跟願意載我去考試的朋友。
畢業不是我一個人的功勞，是整條街的便利商店一起完成的。

en.content:
Graduation thank-you list:

Thanks to the classmate who shared their notes.
Thanks to the senior who let me copy past exams.
Thanks to the 24-hour convenience store.
Thanks to the Wi-Fi for never crashing the exact moment I hit submit.
Thanks to me —
for actually trying, at least in those last few weeks.
en.editorNote: Post this after the ceremony — turning the cliche 'thank-you speech' into specific, student-coded moments lands way better than a generic shoutout
variations: There are too many people to thank, so let me just thank caffeine, late-night snacks, and whoever drove me to the exam.
I didn't graduate alone — the whole block of 24-hour shops helped.

items.sourceNote: 受畢業典禮上『下一章』類型語錄啟發，重寫成更個人、更有溫度的版本

zh-tw.content:
今天合上的這本書，
不是被讀完。

是被我，
用了好幾年的時間，
慢慢寫到這裡。

下一本還沒有書名，
第一頁也還是空白。

但握筆的人，
已經跟剛入學那天的我，
不一樣了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業前夕的反思貼文，把『畢業』比喻成『書寫到一個段落』而不是『被讀完』，能引起對成長過程有感的人共鳴
variations: 我不是合上一本讀完的書，我是寫完了一本只有我自己看得懂的書。
下一頁還是空白的，但這次我比較不怕了。

en.content:
The book that closed today
wasn't one I finished reading.

It's the one I spent years
slowly writing,
until I got here.

The next one doesn't have a title yet.
The first page is still blank.

But the person holding the pen
is no longer the person
who walked in on day one.
en.editorNote: A reflective caption for the night before graduation — reframing the diploma as a chapter you wrote, not a book you finished, hits anyone who actually felt themselves change during these years
variations: I didn't close a book someone else wrote. I closed one only I could read.
The next page is blank again. This time I'm less afraid of it.

items.sourceNote: 受『畢業仍在 buffering』類型搞笑語錄啟發，重寫成更生活化的版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業典禮結束的那一刻，
我以為會聽見一個聲音說：
「恭喜你，從這裡開始你會知道自己在幹嘛。」

但什麼也沒發生。

只有風吹過，
還有同學在後面喊：
「等下要去哪吃？」

喔，原來人生就是這樣繼續下去的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮當天或之後幾天的反思貼文，把『畢業頓悟』寫成『其實什麼也沒發生』，反而更貼近真實感受
variations: 我以為畢業會像關卡破了一樣有提示音，結果只有肚子餓的聲音。
畢業最大的衝擊不是離別，是發現原來沒有人會告訴你下一步要怎麼走。

en.content:
When the ceremony ended,
I thought I'd hear a voice say:
"Congratulations — from here on, you'll know what you're doing."

But nothing happened.

Just wind,
and a classmate behind me shouting:
"Where are we eating?"

Oh. So this is just how life keeps going.
en.editorNote: Post on graduation day or in the days after — captures the truth that the 'big moment' doesn't actually feel like the movies promised it would
variations: I thought finishing school would come with a level-up sound. Instead I just heard my stomach growl.
The real shock of graduation isn't the goodbyes. It's realizing nobody is going to tell you what to do next.

items.sourceNote: 受『畢業是新章節開始』類型語錄啟發，重寫成更聚焦在『改變的那個人是你自己』

zh-tw.content:
畢業不是你拿到了什麼，
是你變成了什麼。

你不是同一個人，
你只是用同一張臉走出去。

四年裡發生的所有事，
那些撐過去的、撐不下去又撐過去的、
以為很重要後來不重要的——

全都變成了你身上看不到的零件，
但只有你自己知道，
它們確實在那裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業前的長文反思，重點不放在『離別』而是『你已經變了』，能引起對成長有感的人深度共鳴
variations: 你以為你只是讀完了學位，其實你是默默被自己重新組裝了一次。
別人看見的是你拿到證書，只有你自己知道，這四年是怎麼把你變成現在這個你的。

en.content:
Graduation isn't about what you got.
It's about who you became.

You're not the same person —
you just walk out wearing the same face.

Everything that happened in those years,
the parts you survived, the parts you almost didn't, the parts you thought were huge that turned out tiny —

they all turned into pieces inside you,
invisible from the outside,
but you know they're there.
en.editorNote: A long-form reflection caption — works for the night before graduation or the morning after, focusing not on the goodbye but on the quiet rewiring that happened along the way
variations: You think you finished a degree. Really, you got quietly rebuilt by yourself, piece by piece.
Other people see the diploma. Only you know what these years actually did to make you the person now holding it.

items.sourceNote: 受『感謝清單』類型搞笑畢業語錄啟發，重寫成更具體、更個人的版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業致詞如果讓我來講，
我會這樣說：

感謝那個願意跟我交換筆記的同學。
感謝那個從來沒當掉我的教授。
感謝那杯凌晨三點的咖啡。
感謝Wi-Fi沒在我按送出的那一刻斷掉。
感謝我自己——
至少在最後幾週是真的有在唸書。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮後的IG貼文，把『感謝詞』寫成具體的學生時代細節，比泛泛的感謝更打動人
variations: 要感謝的人太多了，所以我直接感謝咖啡因、宵夜攤、還有那個願意載我去考試的人。
畢業不是我一個人的事，是一整條24小時營業的街都幫了我。

en.content:
If I had to give a graduation speech,
it would go like this:

Thanks to the classmate who traded notes with me.
Thanks to the professor who never failed me.
Thanks to the 3 a.m. coffee.
Thanks to the Wi-Fi for not crashing the second I clicked submit.
Thanks to me —
for actually showing up, at least in those final weeks.
en.editorNote: A graduation-day Instagram caption — turning the cliche thank-you list into specific student-life moments lands far better than a generic shoutout
variations: Too many people to thank, so let me just thank caffeine, late-night snacks, and whoever drove me to the exam.
I didn't graduate alone — the entire 24-hour block of convenience stores carried me.

items.sourceNote: 受『迷路也是一種前進』類型語錄啟發，重寫成更口語、更貼近年輕人焦慮的版本

zh-tw.content:
如果你畢業了，
但還不知道自己要幹嘛——

沒關係。

沒有人在發畢業證書的同時，
附贈一張人生地圖。

你不是迷路，
你只是還沒走到那個
回頭看才會知道的轉彎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後幾週、開始焦慮『大家都知道自己要幹嘛只有我不知道』的時候，傳給朋友或貼在限動，溫柔但不雞湯
variations: 畢業後的迷茫不是你的問題，是這個階段本來就長這樣。
你不是還沒找到方向，你是還沒到能看出方向的那個位置而已。

en.content:
If you graduated
but still don't know what you're doing —

that's okay.

Nobody hands you a life map
at the same time they hand you the diploma.

You're not lost.
You just haven't reached
the turn you'll only recognize
looking back.
en.editorNote: For the weeks after graduation when everyone seems to have a plan except you — send it to a friend or post on your story, gentle without being saccharine
variations: The post-grad confusion isn't a flaw. This stage just looks like this.
You're not directionless. You just haven't reached the point where the direction becomes visible.

items.sourceNote: 受『畢業是一段關係的結束』類型語錄啟發，重寫成更聚焦在『日常的消失』

zh-tw.content:
我們不會大吵大鬧地分開。

只是有一天你會發現，
那個會在午餐時間問你「要去哪吃」的人，
變成了IG上偶爾按讚的人。

那個會跟你一起翹課的人，
變成了結婚時你會去喝喜酒的人。

畢業不會把感情拿走，
只是把『每天』這件事，
慢慢地、安靜地，
從你們之間抽走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後幾個月，開始發現自己跟好朋友變得不太一樣的時候——不是難過，是一種誠實的承認
variations: 最大的告別不是哭著說再見，是某天突然意識到你們已經很久沒聊天了。
感情沒有不見，只是從『每天』變成『偶爾』，從『一起』變成『各自』。

en.content:
We don't part with a big dramatic scene.

One day you just notice
that the person who used to ask "where are we eating?" at lunch
is now someone who occasionally likes your Instagram posts.

The one who used to skip class with you
is now someone you'll see at their wedding.

Graduation doesn't take the friendship.
It just slowly, quietly,
pulls the word 'every day'
out from between you.
en.editorNote: For a few months post-grad, when you realize the closeness has shifted — not sad exactly, more like an honest acknowledgement of how friendships move
variations: The biggest goodbye isn't the tearful one. It's the day you realize you haven't actually talked in a while.
The friendship didn't disappear. It just moved from 'every day' to 'sometimes', from 'together' to 'apart'.

items.sourceNote: 受『In My ___ Era』IG限動風潮啟發，重寫成更有畫面感、更貼近2026年畢業生口吻的版本

zh-tw.content:
正式進入：

「不知道自己在幹嘛，
但看起來很有自信」的時期。

畢業證書已備妥。
人生計畫正在載入中⋯⋯
（請耐心等候，預估時間：未知）
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天的IG限動神文案，承接2026年「In My ___ Era」的流行語感，但把那種看似從容、實則一頭霧水的狀態寫得更真實
variations: 畢業後第一個身份：很有氣勢的迷路者。
目前進度：學會了走路時看起來很從容，內心其實在Google『下一步怎麼辦』。

en.content:
Now entering:

my "no idea what I'm doing
but walking with intent" era.

Diploma: acquired.
Life plan: loading...
(estimated time: unknown)
en.editorNote: A graduation-day Instagram story caption riding the 2026 "In My ___ Era" trend — captures that walking-confidently-while-internally-lost vibe better than a generic milestone post
variations: Post-grad identity: very confident pedestrian going absolutely nowhere.
Current progress: mastered the calm walk. Internally still Googling 'what now'.

items.sourceNote: 受『thank you, iced coffee』類型搞笑感謝清單啟發，改寫成更有2026台灣大學生日常感的版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業致謝清單（誠實版）：

感謝那杯讓我撐到早上八點的冰美式。
感謝願意收我作業到午夜12:59的Moodle。
感謝那位從不點名的教授（你真的救了我）。
感謝Google翻譯與ChatGPT，沒有你們我不會走到今天。
感謝我自己——
至少在每個交報告的前一晚，都還願意爬起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮限動的搞笑致謝文，把『感謝XX』的格式套在學生真正會用到的東西上，比制式致詞有梗很多
variations: 畢業前最大的恩人：截止時間前的三小時。
感謝那些幫我簽到的朋友，今天的學位有一部分是你們的。

en.content:
Graduation thank-you list (honest version):

Thank you to the iced americano that got me to 8 a.m.
Thank you to the LMS that accepted my submission at 11:59 p.m.
Thank you to the professor who never took attendance (real one).
Thank you to Google Translate and ChatGPT — I wouldn't be here without you.
Thank you to me —
for still showing up the night before every deadline.
en.editorNote: A graduation-day Instagram caption — uses the classic 'thank you to' template but swaps in the stuff that actually got you through, far more relatable than a polished speech
variations: Biggest hero of my college years: the three hours before a deadline.
Shoutout to the friends who signed me in. This degree is partly yours.

items.sourceNote: 受『Class of 2026』勵志短語啟發，重寫成更有畫面、不雞湯的台式口吻

zh-tw.content:
四年前，
你拖著行李第一次走進這個地方，
不知道會發生什麼。

四年後，
你拖著一樣的行李走出來，
還是不知道會發生什麼。

但這次不一樣的是——
你已經知道，
不知道也沒關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後傳給朋友、或是自己貼IG的長文版，把『成長』寫得不浮誇——你變強的方式是學會跟未知共處
variations: 成長不是知道更多答案，是學會在沒有答案的時候，還能繼續走。
你帶走的不只是學歷，是『不確定也沒事』的這份膽量。

en.content:
Four years ago,
you dragged your suitcase in for the first time,
not knowing what would happen.

Four years later,
you drag the same suitcase out,
still not knowing what will happen.

The difference now:
you've learned
that not knowing
is also fine.
en.editorNote: A longer-form Instagram caption for after the ceremony — frames growth honestly: you didn't get more answers, you got better at sitting with the lack of them
variations: Growth isn't getting more answers. It's learning to keep moving without them.
What you take with you isn't just the diploma — it's the nerve to be uncertain.

items.sourceNote: 受『diploma secured / mission complete』短句風潮啟發，重寫成毒雞湯式的反差短文案

zh-tw.content:
畢業是一場
從『不想上學』
升級成『也不想上班』
的儀式。

恭喜你，
正式從一個籠子，
畢業到下一個籠子。

（這個籠子還會給你薪水，算進步了。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業前夕的限動毒雞湯，承接2026年厭世風潮——比起『前程似錦』，這種誠實但帶笑點的版本反而最多人轉發
variations: 從學生過渡到社畜，唯一的好處是——終於可以光明正大地討厭星期一。
畢業：從『被當』的恐懼，平移到『被資遣』的恐懼。

en.content:
Graduation:
the official ceremony where
"I don't want to go to class"
upgrades to
"I don't want to go to work."

Congrats —
you've graduated from one cage
to the next.

(This one pays you, though. Progress.)
en.editorNote: A pre-grad Instagram story caption with bite — leans into the 2026 burnout-humor vibe; lands harder than 'bright future ahead' because everyone secretly agrees
variations: Going from student to office worker. The one perk: you can finally hate Mondays out loud.
Graduation: smoothly transitioning from fear of failing the class to fear of getting laid off.

items.sourceNote: 受『最後一次』畢業告別風潮啟發，重寫成聚焦在日常小場景的告別版本

zh-tw.content:
你不會記得
所有的考試、所有的報告。

但你會記得——
那個下午，
陽光從教室窗戶斜斜地照進來，
旁邊的人睡到流口水，
講台上的教授還在講重點。

那一刻平凡到
當時的你完全不覺得會懷念。

結果現在卻是
最常想起的那一個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業前最後幾週、或畢業後幾個月貼的長版IG文案——比起『再見學校』的口號，這種具體到讓人鼻酸的場景才會真正擊中人
variations: 最讓人想念的從來不是大事，是那些當下覺得很無聊的下午。
你以為會懷念畢業典禮，結果懷念的是上課傳紙條那個瞬間。

en.content:
You won't remember
every exam or every paper.

But you'll remember —
that one afternoon,
sunlight slanting through the classroom window,
the person next to you asleep with their mouth open,
the professor still listing the key points.

A moment so ordinary
you never thought you'd miss it.

And somehow,
it's the one you think about most.
en.editorNote: A longer Instagram caption for the last weeks of school or a few months after — beats any generic 'goodbye school' line because the specificity is what actually makes people tear up
variations: What you miss isn't the big moments. It's the boring afternoons you didn't know you'd want back.
You thought you'd miss the ceremony. Turns out you miss passing notes during class.

items.sourceNote: 受『In my ___ era』短句風潮啟發，重寫成中文版本的時代感畢業文案

zh-tw.content:
上一個時代：
背書、補考、被當的恐懼。

下一個時代：
還沒想好，
但已經沒人會幫我簽請假單了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天的限動短句，用『時代轉換』的比喻取代老套的『新階段』，配上一張畢業袍背影最有感
variations: 我的學生時代正式下檔，下一季預告：未知。
結束學生身分的那一刻才發現——以後遲到沒有人會幫我點名了。

en.content:
Previous era:
late-night cramming, retakes, fear of failing.

Next era:
still TBD,
but nobody's signing my permission slip anymore.
en.editorNote: A graduation-day Instagram story line that flips the trendy 'in my ___ era' format into something with more bite — pairs perfectly with a back-shot of you in the gown
variations: My student era just wrapped. Next season: unannounced.
The moment your student ID expires, you realize nobody's marking you present anymore.

items.sourceNote: 受『感謝同學一路相伴』風潮啟發，重寫成具體場景的友情告別版本

zh-tw.content:
謝謝你
在我作業沒寫完的早上借我抄，
在我被當掉的那天請我喝飲料，
在我哭得很醜的那次假裝沒看到。

以後我們不會每天見面，
但你是那種——
就算三年沒講話，
再見面還是可以一起罵教授的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業當天tag最好的朋友的IG限動文，用具體小事代替『一輩子的好朋友』，反而更有重量；也適合放在合照下方當caption
variations: 有些人不用每天聯絡，但一見面就知道——位置一直幫你留著。
畢業之後最值得的關係是這種：不需要常常見面，但永遠願意接你的電話。

en.content:
Thanks for
letting me copy your homework before first period,
buying me a drink the day I failed that class,
and pretending not to notice the ugly crying.

We won't see each other every day anymore,
but you're the kind of friend —
the kind I could not talk to for three years
and still pick up exactly where we left off,
roasting the same professor.
en.editorNote: Tag-your-best-friend graduation caption — works better than 'friends for life' because the specific memories carry the weight; also great under a group photo
variations: Some people you don't have to talk to every day. You just know the seat is still saved.
The best friendships after graduation are the ones that don't need constant contact — they just always pick up the phone.

items.sourceNote: 受『Plot twist: I graduated』短句風潮啟發，重寫成意想不到的反差幽默

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：
那個大一被預測會被退學的人，
畢業了。

編劇我看是放棄了，
直接讓主角過關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種一路驚險過關的人發限動，用『戲劇反轉』比喻意外順利畢業，比『努力終於有回報』更輕鬆有趣
variations: 劇本應該寫我被當掉，結果我居然走到了畢業典禮。看來編劇也累了。
從大一被預言畢不了業，到大四真的畢業——人生這部劇真的很愛打臉。

en.content:
Plot twist:
the one everyone bet would drop out freshman year
just graduated.

Honestly,
I think the writers gave up
and let me win.
en.editorNote: For the survivors who barely made it — uses the 'plot twist' format to laugh at your own near-misses, lighter than the usual 'hard work paid off' line
variations: The script said I'd fail. I walked the stage anyway. The writers must be exhausted.
Freshman year prediction: dropout. Senior year reality: diploma. Life loves a good plot hole.

items.sourceNote: 受『下次開學我的校園沒有你』TikTok畢業文案啟發，重寫成更聚焦『缺席感』的版本

zh-tw.content:
九月開學那天，
校園還是一樣熱鬧。

新生在拍合照，
社團在發傳單，
福利社的麵包還是賣同一款。

只是這次，
那條走廊沒有你，
那張椅子沒有你，
那群人裡，
也沒有我了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業後幾個月、新學期開始時最有殺傷力的IG長文——用『校園還在但你不在』的具體場景，比『懷念校園』更打中人
variations: 原來離開最遺憾的不是學校變了，是學校一點都沒變，只是少了你。
畢業最殘忍的事情：學校照常開學，只是名單上再也沒有你的名字。

en.content:
First day of the new school year,
the campus looks exactly the same.

Freshmen taking group photos,
clubs handing out flyers,
the same bread in the same vending machine.

It's just that this time,
you're not in that hallway,
you're not in that seat,
and in that group of people,
I'm not there either.
en.editorNote: A months-after-graduation Instagram caption — the gut-punch isn't 'I miss school,' it's 'school is exactly the same without me,' which is way more specific and lands harder
variations: Turns out the saddest part of leaving isn't that the school changed. It's that nothing did, except you weren't in it.
Graduation's quietest cruelty: the semester starts again on schedule, just without your name on the roster.

items.sourceNote: 受 2026 IG 流行『In my graduated era』短句啟發，改寫成更口語的台式版本

zh-tw.content:
正式進入：

畢業era。

下一階段：
還在載入中，
請勿關閉視窗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天最適合的限動文案，短到可以直接蓋在自拍上——比一長串感言更有梗，也更符合『其實我也不知道要幹嘛』的真實心境
variations: 畢業era 開啟，劇情大綱：未完待續。
歡迎來到畢業era，特色：再也沒有寒暑假，但週末也沒比較開心。

en.content:
Officially entering:

my graduated era.

Next chapter:
still loading,
please don't close the window.
en.editorNote: Drop this over a grad-day selfie — short enough to fit as a caption sticker, captures the 'I have no idea what's next' vibe better than any heartfelt speech
variations: Graduated era unlocked. Plot summary: TBD.
Welcome to my graduated era. Perks: no more breaks. Also no more breaks.

items.sourceNote: 受『I would like to thank Google』感謝梗啟發，改寫成更貼近台灣學生的版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業感言：

感謝Google，
感謝ChatGPT，
感謝那個從來不點名的教授，
感謝期末給我抄筆記的同學，
感謝願意讓我分組搭便車的組員。

當然，
還有我自己——
沒有放棄，
是因為退費太麻煩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮致謝文照搬實在太肉麻，改成這種『誠實版本』反而更有共鳴。適合丟在畢業合照下，朋友看到會直接tag你
variations: 我能站在這裡畢業，要特別感謝那些考試前突然出現的共筆。
畢業致詞精華版：感謝所有願意收留我這個雷組員的好心人。

en.content:
Graduation speech:

I'd like to thank Google,
ChatGPT,
the professor who never took attendance,
the classmate who let me copy notes at midnight,
and every group member who carried me through.

Also me —
for not dropping out,
because refunds are a nightmare.
en.editorNote: Skip the cheesy thank-you speech and post this honest version — works perfectly under a grad-photo carousel, your friends will tag themselves in the comments
variations: Standing here today thanks to the shared Google Doc that appeared 12 hours before every final.
Highlight of my graduation speech: thanking everyone who tolerated me as a group project teammate.

items.sourceNote: 受『畢業是新旅程的開始』類祝福語啟發，改寫成不矯情但仍有溫度的版本

zh-tw.content:
我不想說『前程似錦』，
那種話太遠了。

我只希望——
你下一份工作不要遇到很爛的主管，
你的房東願意退你押金，
你想吃宵夜的時候，
還有人會接你的電話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給死黨的畢業祝福，比『鵬程萬里』更實在。把抽象的祝福換成具體的『日常小事順利』，反而更打中人，也更像會真的發生在生活裡的事
variations: 祝你畢業後：薪水準時入帳、外送沒灑、捷運有位子坐、想哭的時候有人接電話。
比起前程似錦，我更希望你下一個十年，半夜餓的時候永遠有人陪你吃。

en.content:
I'm not going to say 'wishing you a bright future' —
that's too far away to mean anything.

What I actually hope:
your next boss isn't a nightmare,
your landlord gives back your deposit,
and when you want late-night snacks,
someone still picks up the phone.
en.editorNote: A graduation message for your closest friends — swap the abstract 'wishing you success' for specific, real-life small wins. Lands way harder because it sounds like something that could actually happen
variations: Post-grad wishes: paycheck on time, food delivery uncrushed, a seat on the train, and someone to call when it's 2am and you're sad.
Forget 'bright future' — I just hope the next ten years, you always have someone who'll grab midnight noodles with you.

items.sourceNote: 受『The tassel was worth the hassle』押韻金句啟發，改寫成中英都有節奏感的反思短句

zh-tw.content:
穗子撥過去那一秒，
我才發現——

四年的累，
是真的。

四年的好，
也是真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當下最有畫面感的短文，適合搭配撥穗那張照片發IG。沒講大道理，但用『兩個都是真的』把畢業最複雜的情緒一句話寫完
variations: 撥穗的那一秒，所有累的、好的、不甘心的、捨不得的，全部都被收進那一秒裡。
原來畢業典禮不是儀式，是時間在你面前按下『存檔』。

en.content:
The second the tassel moved,
I finally got it —

the four years of exhaustion
were real.

The four years of good stuff
were real too.
en.editorNote: A grad-day caption that fits perfectly under the tassel-turn photo. No big lessons, just lets both truths sit side by side — which is what graduation actually feels like
variations: When the tassel moved, every tired, happy, unfinished, and unwilling-to-leave moment got packed into that one second.
Turns out graduation isn't a ceremony. It's just time hitting the save button right in front of you.

items.sourceNote: 受 2026『In my ___ era』IG 趨勢啟發，改寫成更有畢業生內心戲的版本

zh-tw.content:
正式進入「不知道自己在幹嘛但看起來很有方向感」era。

簡稱：畢業生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短到可以直接當IG限動主標的畢業梗，搭上era梗熱潮。適合那種「明天要面試但今晚還在追劇」的真實狀態
variations: From「期中還沒讀完」era → 直接跳到「履歷還沒寫完」era。中間沒有過渡。
新身份解鎖：看起來有目標的失業中。

en.content:
Officially entering my "no clue what I'm doing but make it look intentional" era.

Also known as: post-grad.
en.editorNote: Short enough to use as a story headline. Rides the 'era' caption trend, but hits the real mood — interview tomorrow, still rewatching a show tonight
variations: Went straight from 'haven't finished the readings' era to 'haven't finished the resume' era. No transition.
New title unlocked: unemployed but make it look like a plan.

items.sourceNote: 受『Plot twist: I graduated』畢業搞笑句啟發，改寫成更戲劇化的版本

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：

那個大一在課堂上睡著、
大二翹了一整學期早八、
大三差點被當掉、
大四交報告交到凌晨四點的人——

畢——業——了。

編劇你是不是寫太誇張？
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配畢業典禮全身照當IG貼文，把自己過去四年的狼狽寫成「劇情大反轉」，比硬說自己很努力來得有共鳴
variations: 本人四年大學主要成就：沒被退學。導演卡，這就是結局。
預告：廢物大學生最終逆襲畢業。劇情合理性？零分。

en.content:
Plot twist:

The person who slept through 8 a.m. lectures freshman year,
skipped a whole semester sophomore year,
almost failed junior year,
and pulled 4 a.m. essay-finishes senior year —

actually. graduated.

Writers, this script is wild.
en.editorNote: A perfect grad-day caption under a full ceremony shot. Re-frames four years of chaos as 'plot twist,' which lands way harder than pretending you had it together
variations: Four-year college achievement unlocked: not getting kicked out. Roll credits.
Trailer: dumpster-fire student somehow graduates. Plot realism: zero.

items.sourceNote: 受 2026 IG「comment trigger」貼文趨勢啟發，寫成會讓朋友想留言的互動型畢業文案

zh-tw.content:
我們做到了。

但「我們」這個詞，
從今天開始，
會散到不同的城市、不同的時區、不同的早餐店。

留言告訴我——
你接下來會在哪一個城市，
好讓我以後路過的時候，
知道要找誰吃飯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把畢業貼文變成同學的集合點。最後那句『留言告訴我你會在哪』直接邀請互動，比起單方面感性更能引起整篇貼文的留言潮
variations: 從今天起，我們的下一頓飯，可能要排到下次有人路過你的城市。所以——你會在哪裡？
畢業不是失聯的開始，是知道彼此在哪裡的開始。留言告訴我你的下一站。

en.content:
We made it.

But starting today,
"we" gets scattered —
different cities, different time zones, different breakfast spots.

Drop your next city in the comments,
so when I'm passing through someday,
I know exactly who to text for dinner.
en.editorNote: Turns a graduation post into a class roll-call. The 'drop your city below' line invites comments — way more engaging than a one-way sentimental caption
variations: From today on, our next meal might depend on someone passing through your city. So — where are you landing?
Graduation isn't the start of losing touch. It's the start of knowing exactly where to find each other. Drop your next stop below.

items.sourceNote: 受『I worked my tassel off』押韻雙關句啟發，改寫成中文也有節奏的短句

zh-tw.content:
別人問我這四年怎麼過的，

我說：
用學貸、咖啡因、
和「明天再說」這三樣東西，
換來這張紙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 極短、節奏感強、用三個具體名詞收尾，IG限動或畢業照配字都好用。比『四年很辛苦』更有畫面感
variations: 我的大學配方：學貸 × 咖啡 × 拖延症。產出：一張畢業證書、兩個黑眼圈、三公斤。
成績單可以丟，學貸丟不掉。歡迎來到大學畢業生售後服務。

en.content:
People ask me how I got through these four years.

The answer:
student loans, caffeine,
and a deeply held belief
that I'd "deal with it tomorrow."

And somehow — diploma.
en.editorNote: Short, punchy, three-noun rhythm. Works as a story caption or under a grad photo. Way more vivid than 'these four years were tough'
variations: College recipe: student loans × coffee × procrastination. Output: one diploma, two eye bags, three extra kilos.
You can throw out the transcript. The student loan stays. Welcome to post-grad customer service.

items.sourceNote: 受『100% sure I have no idea what I'm doing』類自嘲句啟發，改寫成有溫度的反向勵志

zh-tw.content:
畢業典禮那天，
身邊每個人看起來都好像知道自己接下來要幹嘛。

後來才知道——
大家只是穿同一件學士服，
演著同一齣「我看起來很穩」的戲。

所以如果你現在覺得自己很慌，
沒事，
旁邊那個笑得很燦爛的人，
可能比你還慌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後一個月、開始焦慮的時候發。把『大家都很迷茫』講成一個共同秘密，比硬給雞湯更治癒，會讓正在徬徨的朋友覺得自己不是異類
variations: 畢業典禮上每個人都笑得很燦爛，後來才知道那叫做：撐場面。
如果你正在懷疑自己是不是同屆裡最沒方向的那個——沒有，大家都是，只是沒人想先承認。

en.content:
On graduation day,
everyone around me looked like they had it all figured out.

Turns out —
we were all wearing the same gown,
putting on the same show called "I'm totally fine."

So if you feel completely lost right now,
don't worry.
That person next to you smiling so wide?
Probably more lost than you.
en.editorNote: Best posted a month after graduation, when the anxiety actually hits. Reframes 'everyone's lost' as a shared secret — more comforting than fake motivation, and makes the friend reading it feel less like an outlier
variations: Everyone smiled big on grad day. Later we found out the technical term for it was: faking it.
If you're wondering whether you're the most directionless one in your class — you're not. Everyone is. Nobody just wants to admit it first.

items.sourceNote: 受『Hotter by one degree』『Class of 2026』類短句啟發，改寫成有畫面感的雙語版

zh-tw.content:
今天起，
我比昨天的我多了一個學位。

體溫沒變，
身高沒變，
但帳戶餘額——
往下掉了一個學位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短而帶轉折的IG限動文案，前面用『多一個學位』玩雙關，最後一句反轉到學貸現實，比單純炫畢業更討喜，留言區會有人接梗
variations: 畢業後體感升溫一度，據說是學貸燒出來的。
從今天開始，我多了一個學位，跟一筆要還很久的紀念品。

en.content:
As of today,
I'm one degree hotter than yesterday.

Same body temperature.
Same height.
But my bank balance —
that dropped by exactly one degree.
en.editorNote: Short, punchy IG caption with a pun pivot. Opens with the classic 'one degree hotter' joke, lands on the student-debt twist — way more shareable than a straight flex, and the comments will write themselves
variations: Felt one degree warmer after graduation. Turns out it was the student loans catching fire.
I gained one degree today. And one souvenir I'll be paying off for a decade.

items.sourceNote: 受『Class dismissed. Now accepting job offers.』類短語啟發，改寫成台灣求職現場版

zh-tw.content:
下課了。

現在開放投履歷。
（接受任何月薪有三萬以上的工作）
（其實兩萬八也行）
（兩萬五我們再談）
（拜託回我）
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮結束、開始海投履歷的那一週發。把『接受job offer』翻成台灣應屆畢業生的現實，越往下越卑微，剛踏進社會的人會邊笑邊哭
variations: 畢業 = 從一個排隊買飯的地方，換到另一個排隊面試的地方。
正式接受所有面試邀請，包含那種要你做一份免費作業的那種。

en.content:
Class dismissed.

Now accepting job offers.
(Anything paying over 50k)
(Okay, 45k works)
(40k? Let's talk)
(Please just reply to my email)
en.editorNote: Best posted the week after graduation, when you're knee-deep in applications. The escalating desperation lands harder than a generic 'job hunting' caption — anyone who's been there will laugh-cry
variations: Graduation: trading one line (food court) for another line (job interviews).
Officially open to all interviews. Including the kind that asks you to do an unpaid take-home assignment.

items.sourceNote: 受『Grad-ually figuring it out』雙關句啟發，改寫成有節奏的成長型文案

zh-tw.content:
我沒有「畢業之後要幹嘛」的答案。

我只有：
慢慢來、慢慢試、慢慢搞清楚自己是誰。

畢業不是一個句點，
是一個——
「我會慢慢弄懂」的開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個還沒找到方向、有點焦慮的同學或自己。把『不知道要幹嘛』講成『慢慢弄懂』，比『加油！』那種空話有重量得多
variations: 別人問我畢業要幹嘛，我說：『慢慢搞清楚中』。這也是一種計畫。
畢業不是知道答案，是開始有資格慢慢找答案。

en.content:
I don't have a clean answer for "what's next after graduation."

What I have is:
take my time, try things, figure out who I am — slowly.

Graduation isn't a period.
It's the start of a sentence that begins with:
"I'll grad-ually figure it out."
en.editorNote: For the friend (or yourself) staring down the post-grad fog. Reframing 'I have no plan' as 'I'm gradually figuring it out' carries more weight than a hollow 'you got this!'
variations: People ask what I'm doing after graduation. I say: figuring it out. That counts as a plan.
Graduation isn't about having the answer. It's about earning the time to look for it.

items.sourceNote: 受『青春不散場』類華語經典畢業句啟發，重新詮釋成更口語、有畫面感的版本

zh-tw.content:
其實我們不會散。

我們只是換了一種方式繼續存在——

變成手機裡那個很少傳訊息、
但生日一定會記得的人。
變成某個夜晚突然想起的那個笑話。
變成你之後遇到新朋友時，
會說「我以前有一群同學⋯⋯」的那一群。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮當天或之後一週，配畢業照貼IG。把『不散場』講得很具體——不是天天聯絡，而是用各種方式還在彼此的人生裡，這種寫法比『友誼永固』動人多了
variations: 我們不會分開，我們只是從『每天見』變成『偶爾想起來笑很久』。
畢業之後，我們不在同一個地方了，但你還在我每次說『我以前⋯⋯』的那個故事裡。

en.content:
We're not actually going our separate ways.

We're just shifting into a different version of being in each other's lives —

The person in your contacts who rarely texts but never forgets your birthday.
The joke that randomly pops into your head at 2 a.m.
The group you'll mention years from now when you say,
"Back in college, I had this crew..."
en.editorNote: Best paired with graduation photos, posted on the day or the week after. Reframes 'we'll stay friends' from a hollow promise into something specific — the small ways people stay woven into your life — which lands much deeper than 'friends forever'
variations: We're not splitting up. We're just upgrading from 'see you every day' to 'randomly remembering something you said and laughing alone.'
After graduation, we won't be in the same place. But you'll still be in every story I tell that starts with 'back when...'

items.sourceNote: 受 Denzel Washington『Fall Forward』畢業演講啟發，改寫成更口語、有節奏的版本

zh-tw.content:
如果你要跌倒，
那就往前跌。

往後跌，你只看到剛剛走過的地方。
往前跌，你至少還看得到下一步該往哪裡去。

畢業之後不是不會跌，
是學會——
用比較不蠢的姿勢跌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種害怕畢業後失敗、不敢踏出去的朋友。把『不要害怕失敗』講成『跌也要往前跌』，比『你可以的！』那種空話有畫面感、也好記
variations: 畢業之後一定會跌跤，重點是：跌的時候臉朝著哪個方向。
別怕跌倒，怕的是你一直往後看那個讓你跌倒的地方。

en.content:
If you're going to fall,
fall forward.

Fall backward, all you see is where you came from.
Fall forward, at least you catch a glimpse of where to go next.

Graduating doesn't mean you stop falling.
It just means you learn to fall
with slightly better form.
en.editorNote: Best for the friend who's scared to take the first step after graduation. Reframing 'don't be afraid of failure' as 'fall forward' gives it a visual and a rhythm — way more memorable than a generic 'you got this'
variations: You'll fall after graduation — guaranteed. The only question is which direction you were facing.
Don't fear the fall. Fear the version of you that keeps looking back at the thing that tripped you.

items.sourceNote: 受 T.S. Eliot『Only those who will risk going too far can possibly find out how far one can go』啟發，重新詮釋成畢業情境

zh-tw.content:
你不會知道自己可以走多遠，
直到你願意走得有點太遠。

太遠這件事，
要踩過去之後才知道是不是真的太遠。

畢業就是——
那個你終於有資格走得太遠的開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個有夢想、但一直被『會不會太冒險』綁住的人。把『勇敢冒險』講成『先走得太遠看看』，比『加油去追夢！』少了點空話、多了點實際
variations: 走太遠不可怕，可怕的是你連『太遠』在哪都沒走過。
畢業之後最值得做的事：去一個讓以前的自己覺得『也太遠了吧』的地方。

en.content:
You'll never know how far you can go
until you're willing to go a little too far.

"Too far" is something
you only confirm by stepping past it.

Graduation is the moment
you finally get permission
to find out where your "too far" actually is.
en.editorNote: For the dreamer who keeps stalling because 'it might be too risky.' Reframing 'be brave' as 'go find your too far' feels more concrete than another 'chase your dreams!' caption
variations: Going too far isn't the scary part. Never having been near 'too far' — that's the scary part.
The best thing to do after graduation: go somewhere that would've made your old self say 'that's way too far.'

items.sourceNote: 受『致青春，後會有期』類華語畢業金句啟發，改寫成更生活化、有距離感的版本

zh-tw.content:
致青春。

謝謝你讓我們在還不知道社會多複雜之前，
先有過一段——
可以為一個比賽哭、
可以為一個誤會冷戰一個禮拜、
可以為一杯飲料認真道謝的時光。

後會有期，
但那個版本的我們，就留在這裡了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮當天或畢業後幾天，配上學生時期合照貼IG。把『青春再見』講得很具體——把那些『現在看起來很傻』的小事一一列出，比『懷念青春』那種抽象詞句更打中人
variations: 後會有期的是我們，留在這裡的是『會為一杯飲料認真道謝』的那個自己。
青春不是離開，是被我們小心地放在那個只有當時的我們才打得開的抽屜裡。

en.content:
To our younger selves —

Thank you for letting us live,
before we knew how complicated the world really was,
through a stretch of time when:
we could cry over losing a competition,
give each other the silent treatment for a week over a misunderstanding,
and sincerely thank someone for buying us a single bubble tea.

We'll meet again.
But that version of us — it stays here.
en.editorNote: Post this on the day of graduation or a few days after, paired with old school photos. Listing out the small, slightly embarrassing things makes 'goodbye to youth' land much harder than vague nostalgia
variations: We'll meet again. What stays behind is the version of us that took a free drink as the world's greatest gift.
Youth doesn't leave. We just quietly file it into a drawer only our younger selves know how to open.

items.sourceNote: 受 Class of 2026 一系列『you have everything you need』類英語金句啟發，改寫成更口語、有自嘲感的版本

zh-tw.content:
致 2026 屆畢業生：

世界沒有準備好接你，
但說真的——
你也沒準備好接世界。

所以剛剛好。

誰也沒佔便宜，
大家都是邊走邊學。
你那個『我是不是還不夠』的感覺，
其實就是入場券。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業前後感到焦慮、覺得『我真的可以嗎』的人。把『你已經準備好了』翻過來講——『沒人準備好，你也算正常』，這種寫法反而讓人鬆一口氣
variations: 畢業典禮上沒人會說的真話：你還沒準備好，但其他人也沒有，所以放心。
「我夠了嗎？」這個問題會跟你一輩子。畢業只是第一次正式被問到。

en.content:
To the Class of 2026:

The world isn't ready for you.
But let's be honest —
you're not really ready for it either.

So we're even.

No one's at an advantage.
Everyone's figuring it out on the way.
That feeling of "am I actually enough?"
Is basically your entry ticket.
en.editorNote: For anyone spiraling around graduation, wondering if they're really ready. Flipping 'you've got this' into 'no one's got this, and that's normal' tends to land as relief rather than another pep talk
variations: The truth no graduation speaker will say out loud: you're not ready. Neither is anyone else. Relax.
"Am I enough?" is a question that follows you for life. Graduation is just the first time it gets asked formally.

items.sourceNote: 受『感謝 Google 跟 Quizlet 讓我畢業』類英語搞笑梗啟發，改寫成更貼近自嘲的版本

zh-tw.content:
在這個重要的日子，
我想感謝幾個沒被寫在學位證書上、
但其實沒有他們我畢不了業的對象——

Google、ChatGPT、
那個總是把筆記放雲端的同學、
還有我那條
「明天再讀」的命。

沒有你們，就沒有今天的我。
（但主要是沒有你們。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮當天貼IG，配學士帽或畢業證書照。畢業致詞通常感謝家人老師，把感謝對象換成『Google、雲端筆記、明天再讀的命』反而更真實，也讓朋友會心一笑
variations: 致學位證書上沒列出來的共同作者：Google、雲端、還有那個願意分享筆記的好心人——這張紙有你們一份。
我不是一個人畢業的，我是跟我的瀏覽器分頁、雲端硬碟、還有「考前一晚」一起畢業的。

en.content:
On this important day,
I'd like to thank a few names
that didn't make it onto the diploma,
but without whom I wouldn't be here —

Google, ChatGPT,
the one classmate who always uploaded their notes,
and my deeply held belief
that I could "start tomorrow."

I couldn't have done it without you.
(Mostly because I literally didn't.)
en.editorNote: Perfect for graduation day on Instagram, paired with a cap or diploma photo. Most speeches thank family and professors — swapping in 'Google, the cloud, and my plan to start tomorrow' lands as the more honest version everyone secretly relates to
variations: Shoutout to the uncredited co-authors of this diploma: Google, the cloud, and the kind soul who shared their notes.
I didn't graduate alone. I graduated with my browser tabs, my shared drive, and the phrase 'I'll start tomorrow.'

items.sourceNote: 受 Lin-Manuel Miranda『最不確定也最刺激的時期』類英語金句啟發，改寫成更生活化的版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，
你會進入人生最不確定，
也最刺激的一段時間。

沒有課表、
沒有期末考、
沒有人會在學期結束跟你說「你過了」。

但也沒有人會跟你說「你過不了」。

從現在開始，
所有的「通過」，
都是你自己給自己的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給剛畢業、覺得『接下來該怎麼辦』的朋友。把『沒人說你過了』和『沒人說你沒過』放在一起講，比單純喊『加油』更能讓人意識到——主導權真的回到自己手上
variations: 學校最大的好處是會給你成績單，畢業最大的挑戰是從此你要自己給自己打分數。
畢業之後沒有期末考，但你會發現——其實每一天都在被自己打分數。

en.content:
After graduation,
you step into the most uncertain
and most thrilling stretch of your life.

No class schedule.
No final exams.
No one telling you at the end of the semester, "you passed."

But also no one telling you, "you didn't."

From here on out,
every "pass"
is one you give yourself.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just graduated and is quietly panicking about what's next. Pairing 'no one says you passed' with 'no one says you failed' hits harder than another generic 'you got this' — it puts the steering wheel back in their hands
variations: School's biggest gift was the report card. Adult life's biggest challenge is that you're now the one grading yourself.
There are no final exams after graduation. But somehow, you grade yourself every single day.

items.sourceNote: 受『long story short, I made it』類英語短句啟發，改寫成更接近台灣口語的版本

zh-tw.content:
說來話長。

總之，
我畢業了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業限動最好用的萬用文案——配畢業照、學士帽、合照都適合。短到不用解釋，但又把『過程很多但不想細講』那種感覺講得剛剛好
variations: 故事很長，結果很短：我畢業了。
中間發生了很多事，但這張照片只需要一句話——畢業了。

en.content:
Long story.

Short version:
I made it.
en.editorNote: The most foolproof graduation caption — works under a diploma photo, a cap toss, or a group shot. Short enough not to need context, but somehow says everything about the messy middle you don't want to recap
variations: Long story. Short ending: I graduated.
A lot happened. This photo only needs one sentence — I made it.

items.sourceNote: 受『勿忘我』『記得我們』類華語畢業金句啟發，改寫成更日常、不煽情的版本

zh-tw.content:
其實我沒有要你「不要忘記我」。

你大概會忘記我哪一年生日、
忘記我那時候喜歡喝什麼、
忘記我們吵過什麼又怎麼和好。

沒關係。

只要哪天你經過一間早餐店、
聞到某個味道、
突然笑了一下——
那就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後傳給特別的那個朋友，或寫在畢業紀念冊裡。把『不要忘記我』翻成『你會忘記沒關係，只要某個瞬間想到就好』，比起索取永遠的記得，這種版本反而更動人也更不有壓力
variations: 我不要你永遠記得我，我只要你某天突然想起我的時候，是笑著的。
細節都會忘，沒關係。只要那個味道、那條路、那首歌還能讓你笑一下，我就還在。

en.content:
I'm not actually asking you to "never forget me."

You'll probably forget when my birthday is,
forget what I used to order back then,
forget what we fought about and how we made up.

That's okay.

As long as one day you walk past a breakfast spot,
catch a familiar smell,
and quietly smile to yourself —
that's enough.
en.editorNote: For that one specific friend after graduation, or to write inside a yearbook. Flipping 'don't forget me' into 'it's okay to forget the details, just smile when something reminds you' carries way more weight — and asks for less
variations: I don't need you to remember me forever. I just need you to be smiling on the day you suddenly do.
You'll forget the details. That's fine. As long as a certain smell, street, or song still makes you smile — I'm still around.

items.sourceNote: 受 Harrison Ford 2026 ASU 畢業致詞『build something that didn't exist yesterday』段落啟發，改寫成更生活化、不喊口號的版本

zh-tw.content:
其實你不用做什麼很偉大的事。

你只要——
做一件昨天還不存在的小事。
替一個沒人替他講話的人講一句話。
把兩個原本不認識的人介紹給彼此。

做完之後不用拍照、不用發文。
那個世界，已經因為你，稍微不一樣了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在畢業典禮後傳給那個總覺得『我又沒多厲害』的朋友。把『改變世界』縮小成『做一件昨天還不存在的小事』，比起喊口號，更能讓人真的開始行動
variations: 改變世界的不是口號，是某一天你願意多做一件昨天還沒有的小事。
你不用變成誰，你只要今天比昨天，多留下一點點本來沒有的東西。

en.content:
You don't have to do something huge.

Just —
do one small thing that didn't exist yesterday.
Say one sentence for someone who can't say it for themselves.
Introduce two people who would've never met otherwise.

No photo. No post. No caption.
The world is already, quietly, a little different because of you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying "but I'm not really doing anything important" after graduation. Shrinking 'change the world' down to 'leave behind one thing that wasn't there yesterday' makes it actually doable instead of paralyzing
variations: Changing the world isn't a slogan. It's the day you decide to leave one small thing behind that wasn't there before.
You don't have to become someone. You just have to leave today a little fuller than you found it.

items.sourceNote: 受 Eric Church 2026 UNC 畢業致詞『waiting for a permission slip that was never going to arrive』金句啟發，改寫成中文語感版

zh-tw.content:
你在等一張，
沒有人會發給你的許可單。

沒有人會打電話跟你說：
「你可以開始了。」

沒有人會傳訊息告訴你：
「現在輪到你了。」

你的人生，
從來就不是排隊制。
是你決定動的那一刻，
才正式開始的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給那個畢業後一直說『我再準備一下』『等時機對了』的朋友。Eric Church UNC 那段『等一張不會發給你的許可單』在2026畢業季很紅，改寫成中文後一樣有後座力
variations: 你不會收到一張寫著『你準備好了』的卡片。你只會在某天突然發現——啊，我已經開始了。
人生不是排隊抽號碼牌，是你決定走進去那一刻，才算開門。

en.content:
You're waiting for a permission slip
that was never going to arrive.

No one is calling to say:
"You can start now."

No one is texting to say:
"It's your turn."

Your life was never a line.
It starts the second you decide to move —
and not a moment before.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps saying 'I just need to prep a little more' after graduation. The Eric Church UNC line about waiting for a permission slip went viral in 2026 graduation season for a reason — it hits the exact nerve
variations: You're not going to get a card in the mail that says 'you're ready.' One day you'll just look up and realize you already started.
Life isn't a queue. The door opens the moment you decide to walk through it.

items.sourceNote: 受 2026 華盛頓郵報整理畢業致詞『承認接下來會難』的共同主題啟發，改寫成不灌雞湯的告別版本

zh-tw.content:
我不想跟你說「未來一片光明」。

因為老實講——
接下來會有很多時候，
你會懷疑自己選錯了、做錯了、走錯了。

但每一個你現在覺得很厲害的人，
都是這樣走過來的。

不是因為他們沒懷疑過自己，
是因為他們懷疑的時候，
還是繼續走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後幾個月、朋友開始撞牆、覺得『大家好像都過得比我好』的時候傳給他。比『加油你可以的』有用一百倍，因為它先承認了難
variations: 未來不會一片光明，但會有夠多的光，讓你看清下一步要踏哪裡。
厲害的人不是沒懷疑過自己，是懷疑的時候沒停下來。

en.content:
I'm not going to tell you the future is bright.

Because honestly —
there'll be plenty of moments
when you'll doubt every choice you made.

But every person you currently admire
got here the same way.

Not because they never doubted themselves.
Because when they doubted themselves,
they kept walking anyway.
en.editorNote: Send this a few months after graduation — when your friend hits the wall and starts thinking 'everyone else seems to have it figured out except me.' Way more useful than 'you got this' because it admits the hard part first
variations: The future won't be all bright. But there'll be just enough light to see where to put your next step.
The people you admire didn't stop doubting themselves. They just didn't stop walking while they did.

items.sourceNote: 綜合2026多篇『畢業卡片祝福語』類華語素材，改寫成適合畢業卡片內頁、口語但有重量的版本

zh-tw.content:
你不用變成那個「成功的大人」。

你只要——
還是會在某首歌響起來的時候停下來、
還是會為了一頓好吃的繞路、
還是會在朋友需要的時候馬上回訊息。

那個你，就已經夠了。

剩下的，慢慢來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫在畢業卡片裡剛剛好——不喊『前程似錦』，而是告訴對方『你不用變』，反而比所有勵志祝福都動人。也適合包個小禮物附在裡面送給剛畢業的朋友
variations: 祝你以後變得更厲害的同時，還是那個會為了一杯奶茶開心一整個下午的你。
畢業之後會學很多新的事情，但拜託——別把那個原本很可愛的你弄丟了。

en.content:
You don't have to become "a successful adult."

You just have to —
still pause when a certain song comes on,
still take the long way for a good meal,
still reply right away when a friend needs you.

That version of you is already enough.

The rest, take your time.
en.editorNote: Perfect for writing inside a graduation card — instead of 'bright future ahead,' you tell them 'you don't need to change,' which lands harder than any cheerleading. Also great tucked into a small gift
variations: May you get more impressive over the years and still be the person who lights up over one good cup of tea.
You'll learn a lot of new things after graduation. Please — just don't lose the version of you that was already pretty great.

items.sourceNote: 綜合多篇2026畢業IG短caption素材改寫，鎖定『超短一句、配畢業照很有力』的方向

zh-tw.content:
劇情大轉折：

我居然畢業了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配這句，搭一張你自己也很驚訝的表情更好。比『畢業快樂』有趣十倍，朋友滑到會停下來笑
variations: 故事很長，總之——我畢業了。
原來我比我自己想像的還要會念書一點點。

en.content:
Plot twist:

I actually graduated.
en.editorNote: Pairs perfectly with a graduation photo, especially if you look just as surprised as everyone else. Way better than 'congrats to me' — friends will stop scrolling
variations: Long story short — I made it.
Turns out I was a tiny bit smarter than I gave myself credit for.

items.sourceNote: 改寫經典『聚是一團火，散是滿天星』方向，用更口語、更現代的方式重寫

zh-tw.content:
我們以前是同一間教室裡的人，
以後是各自城市裡的人。

但很奇怪——
散開之後，
你們反而變得更亮了。

像是天空變大了，
我才看清楚每一顆星本來就會發光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後傳到群組裡剛剛好，或寫在畢業紀念冊。重點不是『我們要分開了』的悲傷，而是『分開後你會發光』的祝福，這個角度更耐讀
variations: 教室裝得下我們所有人，但裝不下我們各自要去的地方。
以前你們是我的同學，現在你們是我抬頭看天空時，會想起的那些光。

en.content:
We used to be people in the same classroom.
Now we'll be people in different cities.

But here's the strange part —
once we scattered,
you all got brighter.

Like the sky just got bigger,
and I finally noticed every one of you was already glowing.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat after the ceremony, or write it in a yearbook. The angle isn't 'we're splitting up' — it's 'you'll shine more apart,' which lands harder and ages better
variations: The classroom was big enough for all of us. It was never big enough for the places we each had to go.
You used to be my classmates. Now you're the lights I think of when I look up at the sky.

items.sourceNote: 改寫多篇『搞笑畢業金句』類英文素材，配合台灣畢業生的生活情境

zh-tw.content:
畢業證書到手的瞬間，
我以為我會感動。

結果腦袋裡只有一句話：

「所以……我接下來要去哪裡上班？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮當天、或剛開始投履歷投到懷疑人生的時候發出來。會引發大量已經出社會的人在底下留言『歡迎入坑』
variations: 我以為畢業是終點，結果只是『下一個關卡』的讀取畫面。
畢業證書真的有用嗎？有，可以拿來證明你失業中。

en.content:
The moment they handed me my diploma,
I thought I'd feel something profound.

Instead my brain just went:

"Cool. So where am I working next?"
en.editorNote: Post this on graduation day, or when you're deep into the job-hunt despair spiral. Guaranteed to get 'welcome to the club' replies from everyone who graduated before you
variations: I thought graduation was the finish line. Turns out it's just the loading screen for whatever's next.
Is the diploma useful? Yes. It officially certifies that you are unemployed.

items.sourceNote: 改寫『老師寫給學生』方向的華語素材，調整為更現代、更不說教的口氣

zh-tw.content:
老師不會跟你說「未來無限可能」，
因為那句話太大、太空。

老師只想跟你說——

以後遇到不會的事，
記得你以前也不會走路、
也不會講話、
也不會考及格。

但你都學會了。

所以這次也會的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合老師寫給畢業生的卡片、或學長姐寫給學弟妹。不喊口號，而是提醒對方『你以前也是從不會到會』，比『加油』有重量很多
variations: 你不用一畢業就知道自己要幹嘛，你只要記得——你以前每一個『不會』的東西，後來都變成『會』了。
未來的事不用現在全部想清楚，慢慢學就好，反正你從以前到現在一直都是這樣長大的。

en.content:
I'm not going to tell you "the possibilities are endless."
That sentence is too big and too empty.

What I want to tell you is —

when you hit something you can't do,
remember: you also couldn't walk once,
couldn't talk once,
couldn't pass a single test once.

And you figured all of it out.

You'll figure this one out too.
en.editorNote: Great for a teacher writing in a graduation card, or an older sibling writing to a younger one. No big slogans — just a reminder that 'can't' has always turned into 'can' for them. Lands heavier than 'you got this'
variations: You don't need to know what you're doing the day you graduate. Just remember — every 'I can't' you've ever had eventually became 'I can.'
You don't have to figure out the future today. Just keep learning the way you've always been learning — that's how you got this far.

items.sourceNote: 受 2026 IG 流行的『In My ___ Era』畢業 caption 風潮啟發，改寫成台灣口語版本

zh-tw.content:
正式進入「不知道自己在幹嘛但裝得很冷靜」era。

簡稱：大人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配這句很適合，特別是那種你穿學士服但表情有點空的照片。比『畢業快樂』可愛多了，朋友看到會笑著按愛心
variations: 正式進入「假裝自己準備好了」era。
正式畢業，正式開始「邊走邊想」的人生階段。

en.content:
Officially entering my "no idea what I'm doing but pretending to be calm" era.

Also known as: adulthood.
en.editorNote: Pairs great with a grad photo, especially the one where you're in the cap and gown looking slightly vacant. Way more charming than 'congrats to me' — friends will smile-react it
variations: Officially entering my "pretending I'm ready for this" era.
Officially graduated, officially starting the "figure it out as you go" chapter.

items.sourceNote: 受 2026 喬治城大學整理畢業致詞『不要讓野心超過你的同理心』啟發，改寫成不說教的台灣版本

zh-tw.content:
出社會之後你會發現——

變強很重要，
但比變強更難的，
是變強之後還記得自己以前是什麼樣子。

別走著走著，
就把那個會為了別人難過的你弄丟了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片裡、或畢業典禮後傳給那個你覺得『他一定會變很厲害但拜託別變得很冷』的朋友。比『加油』有層次很多
variations: 你會越來越有能力，但拜託——別讓你的能力把你的溫度蓋過去。
厲害不難，難的是厲害之後還願意彎下腰看別人。

en.content:
Here's what you figure out after graduation —

getting stronger matters,
but what's harder than getting stronger
is remembering who you were before you got there.

Don't walk so far that you lose
the version of you that still hurts for other people.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation card, or sending to the friend you know will absolutely succeed but you're a little worried might harden along the way. Lands with more weight than 'you got this'
variations: You'll get more capable over time. Just don't let your capability drown out your warmth.
Becoming impressive isn't the hard part. Staying willing to bend down and notice people once you're up there — that is.

items.sourceNote: 綜合 2026 多篇『超短畢業金句』英文素材，改寫成台灣語感的雙關短句

zh-tw.content:
今天的標題是：

「終於」。

沒了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照的萬用配文，越短越有力。特別適合那種你跟同學笑到歪掉的合照，標題越敷衍照片越有戲
variations:
標題：

下班。

今日重點：

畢業。

以下空白。

en.content:
Today's headline:

"Finally."

That's it.
en.editorNote: All-purpose caption for any grad photo — the shorter, the harder it hits. Especially good for the one where you and your friends are dying laughing — the more casual the caption, the funnier the photo
variations:
Headline:

Clocked out.

Today's update:

Graduated.

No further comment.

items.sourceNote: 綜合 2026 多篇『家長寫給孩子』類畢業祝福語素材，重寫成不催促、不比較的家長版本

zh-tw.content:
媽媽（或爸爸）想跟你說——

你不用急著變成「讓我們驕傲的孩子」，
你本來就是。

以後遇到事情，
不知道怎麼辦的時候，
就先回來吃個飯。

剩下的，我們再一起想。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合家長寫在畢業卡片裡——不喊『前程似錦』、不催著孩子表現，反而說『回來吃飯』，這個角度殺傷力最大。送孩子的小禮物附這張卡片剛剛好
variations: 出去闖沒關係，但記得——這裡永遠有一桌飯在等你。
你不用一個人扛所有事。累了就回家，這個家不會因為你還沒成功就把門關起來。

en.content:
What I want to tell you is —

you don't need to rush to become "a kid we can be proud of."
You already are.

Whenever something happens
and you don't know what to do,
just come home for a meal first.

We'll figure the rest out together.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a parent writing in a graduation card — no 'bright future ahead,' no pressure to perform, just 'come home and eat.' That angle hits hardest. Pair it with a small gift and you're done
variations: Go out and try things. Just remember — there's always a table set for you here.
You don't have to carry everything alone. When you're tired, come home. This door doesn't close just because you haven't 'made it' yet.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自『Plot twist: I graduated』類短句搞笑風格，加入台灣口語

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：

我居然畢業了。

連我自己都沒料到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配文神器，那種你也覺得『欸我居然撐到今天』的氛圍。短、有梗、不裝厲害，反而最有共鳴——尤其適合大學或研究所那種跌跌撞撞才畢業的人
variations:
本季最大爆點：

我畢業了。

誰能想到啊。

連我都不敢相信，
但學校真的把證書給我了。

en.content:
Plot twist:

I actually graduated.

Didn't see that coming either.
en.editorNote: A go-to caption for that grad photo where even you're a little surprised you made it. Short, funny, doesn't try too hard — hits especially well for anyone who stumbled their way to the finish line
variations:
Biggest plot twist of the season:

I graduated.

Who saw this coming.

Not me. But the school handed me the diploma anyway.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 Class of 2026 勵志祝福素材，調整為老師寫給學生的口吻

zh-tw.content:
老師想跟你說——

你不一定要成為很厲害的人。
你只要成為一個

「自己不會討厭的人」

就好了。

剩下的，慢慢來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合老師寫在畢業卡片、紀念冊或結業致詞裡。不講『要成功』、不要『一定要怎樣』，反而說『不要變成自己討厭的人』，這個門檻其實才是最難也最重要的
variations:
你不用變得很特別，
你只要不要變得跟你以前討厭的大人一樣，
就已經很棒了。

老師沒有要你考第一名，
只希望你以後遇到難關時，
還能像現在這樣笑出來。

en.content:
What I want to tell you is —

you don't have to become someone impressive.
You just have to become

"someone you don't hate."

The rest can wait.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a teacher writing in a graduation card, yearbook, or closing speech. Skips the 'go be great' and goes straight to 'just don't become the kind of adult you used to hate.' That's a higher bar than it sounds — and it matters more
variations:
You don't need to be special.
Just don't grow up
into the kind of adult you used to dislike.
That alone is a lot.

I'm not asking you to be top of your class.
I just hope that when life gets hard later,
you can still laugh the way you do now.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自『thank Google and Quizlet』類自嘲梗，本地化為台灣學生熟悉的工具

zh-tw.content:
畢業致詞：

感謝 Google、
感謝 ChatGPT、
感謝那個總是借我筆記的同學。

沒有你們，
就沒有今天站在這裡的我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照、限動、群組分享都好用。自嘲式的感謝最有親和力，比起正經的『感謝師長』更貼近現代學生的真實感受——尤其那個借筆記的同學看到一定笑死
variations:
得獎感言：

感謝隊友、感謝小抄、感謝期末週的咖啡。

我不是一個人畢業的，
我是跟我的 ChatGPT、我的同學、
還有圖書館那台快壞掉的印表機，
一起畢業的。

en.content:
Graduation speech:

Thank you, Google.
Thank you, ChatGPT.
Thank you to the classmate
who always shared their notes.

Without y'all,
I wouldn't be standing here today.
en.editorNote: Works for grad photos, stories, group chats — the self-deprecating thank-you lands harder than a formal 'thanks to the faculty.' That note-sharing friend is definitely going to laugh when they see it
variations:
Acceptance speech:

Thanks to my group project carriers, my cheat sheets, and finals-week coffee.

I didn't graduate alone.
I graduated with ChatGPT, my classmates,
and that one half-broken printer in the library.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 2026 IG 畢業梗『In My ___ Era』流行語，本地化為台灣語感

zh-tw.content:
現在進入

「有畢業證書
但人生沒方向」

era。

證書好看，
方向再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業限動、群組發文都適用。比起『前途無量』這種空話，這種承認自己暫時迷惘但還是有把學位拿到手的語氣，反而更接地氣、更得共鳴
variations:
畢業生狀態：

履歷寫得很厲害，
人生過得很茫然。

但這就是新鮮人嘛。

我的畢業致詞只有一句：

「先讓我緩一下，
人生我等等再規劃。」

en.content:
Now entering my

"has a diploma
but no direction"

era.

The certificate looks great.
The direction can wait.
en.editorNote: Works for graduation stories or a group chat drop. The honest 'I got the degree but not the plan' tone hits harder than any 'bright future' caption — that's exactly the 2026 mood
variations:
Graduate status:

Resume looks impressive.
Life feels like a draft.

That's just being new at this.

My entire graduation speech:

"Let me cook for a second,
I'll figure life out
right after this nap."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自寫給畢業好友的告別信主題，調整成台灣朋友間的語氣

zh-tw.content:
謝謝你陪我走完這一段。

那些我自己一個人不敢做的事，
是你拉著我去的。
那些我覺得撐不下去的時候，
是你坐在我旁邊不講話的。

以後我們會在不同的城市，
但我想你會永遠是
那個讓我變成「我」的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片裡，或畢業典禮後傳給那個從大一陪你到大四的朋友。不講什麼『加油』、『前程似錦』，而是承認對方在你生命裡的份量，這種告白比任何祝福都重
variations:
我們以後可能不會每天見面了。
但我想跟你說，
你在我生命裡的位置，
沒有人換得掉。

畢業之後最捨不得的，
不是學校、不是宿舍，
是那個在凌晨三點還會接我電話的你。

en.content:
Thank you for walking this part with me.

The things I was too scared to do alone,
you dragged me into.
The nights I thought I couldn't get through,
you just sat next to me
and didn't say anything.

We'll be in different cities soon.
But you'll always be
the person who helped me become me.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a graduation card or a long message to the friend who's been there from freshman year. Skips the 'good luck' and goes straight to admitting how much they shaped you — that lands deeper than any standard wish
variations:
We probably won't see each other every day anymore.
But I need you to know —
the place you have in my life
isn't something anyone else can fill.

The hardest part of graduating
isn't leaving the school or the dorm.
It's leaving the person
who picked up my 3 AM phone calls.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自畢業勵志名言主題，重新詮釋為現代年輕人的語氣

zh-tw.content:
畢業不是結局，
是「沒人會幫你排課表」
的開始。

聽起來有點可怕，
但其實也代表——

從現在開始，
你終於可以選自己要的時間表。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在送給畢業生的卡片，或自己發在IG限動。把『畢業是新開始』這個老梗，換成『沒人幫你排課表』這種具體又貼近生活的比喻，會更有畫面感、更打中人
variations:
以前是學校在分配你的時間，
以後是你在分配自己的時間。

這個自由很重，
但也是你拿得起來的。

畢業之後沒有人會點名了，
但每天還是要記得，
自己想成為什麼樣的人。

en.content:
Graduation isn't the end.
It's the start of
"nobody is making your schedule
for you anymore."

Sounds scary.
But it also means —

from now on,
you finally get to choose
what your days look like.
en.editorNote: Great for a card to a new graduate, or your own Instagram story. Trades the tired 'new beginning' line for something more vivid — nobody's setting your timetable now — which makes the message land instead of slide past
variations:
School used to manage your time.
Now you do.

That freedom is heavy,
but it's a weight you can carry.

After graduation,
no one's taking attendance.
But every day,
you still have to remember
who you want to become.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自畢業自嘲式 IG 文案主題，融入台灣學生熟悉的場景

zh-tw.content:
畢業證書
GPA：保密
出席率：不予置評
小組報告貢獻度：精神支持

但——

我畢業了。
這才是重點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照、IG 限動超適合配這段。承認自己不是模範生，但還是把證書拿到了——這種自嘲不會貶低自己，反而會讓朋友會心一笑、按愛心給你
variations:
畢業生履歷：

專長：補進度
證照：DEADLINE 駕馭執照
備註：終於畢業了，本人比履歷想像中更努力。

我畢業的方式：

不是輕鬆通過，
是用最後一學期
把前三年欠的全部還清。

en.content:
Diploma: acquired
GPA: classified
Attendance: no comment
Group project contribution: emotional support

But —

I graduated.
That's the part that counts.
en.editorNote: Pair this with a grad photo or Instagram story. Owning the fact that you weren't a model student but still made it across the line — that self-aware honesty gets way more love than any 'top of my class' caption
variations:
Graduate resume:

Skill: catching up at the last minute
Certification: deadline survival
Note: I graduated, and I worked harder than this resume suggests.

How I graduated:

Not by cruising through —
by spending my final semester
paying off three years of academic debt.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自2026畢業IG文案主題，融入AI世代的自嘲語氣

zh-tw.content:
我請AI幫我寫畢業感言。

它寫了三段，
每一段都比我四年寫的報告
還要有條理。

但證書還是我的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配畢業照貼IG，承認自己在AI時代畢業的尷尬與真實。比起裝模作樣的勵志文，這種坦白反而更會被朋友按愛心
variations:
ChatGPT陪我畢業，
但典禮上走紅毯的還是我。
功勞我收下，謝謝。

四年裡，
我學會的最重要技能是：
知道哪些東西可以丟給AI，
哪些東西必須自己扛。

en.content:
I asked AI to write my graduation speech.

It wrote three paragraphs,
each one more organized
than anything I produced in four years.

But the diploma's still mine.
en.editorNote: Perfect Instagram caption for grad photos — owns the awkward reality of graduating in the AI era. Honest self-aware humor lands way better than fake-deep inspiration
variations:
ChatGPT helped me graduate,
but I'm the one walking the stage.
I'll take the credit, thanks.

The most important skill I learned in four years:
knowing which things to hand off to AI,
and which things I have to carry myself.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自畢業感謝父母主題，調整為台灣家庭關係的含蓄表達

zh-tw.content:
你們從來沒說過「我以你為榮」這四個字。

但你們會在我考試前
多煮一道我喜歡的菜。
你們會在我哭著打電話回家時
說「沒事，回來再說」。

這張證書，
上面寫著我的名字，
但其實有一半是你們的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在畢業典禮後私下傳給爸媽，或寫在卡片裡夾在畢業證書旁。台灣父母不擅長講愛，但很會用行動，這段把那種說不出口的感謝寫出來，會讓他們眼眶泛紅
variations:
我畢業了。

但這四年最辛苦的不是我，
是每次我說「不想念了」，
卻假裝沒聽到、繼續準備早餐的你們。

謝謝你們從來沒逼我成為誰，
只是讓我慢慢變成現在的自己。

這條路你們走得比我還累，
我都知道。

en.content:
You never said the words "I'm proud of you."

But you'd cook my favorite dish
the night before my exams.
You'd say "it's okay, come home and we'll talk"
when I called crying.

My name is on this diploma —
but honestly,
half of it belongs to you.
en.editorNote: Send this privately to your parents after the ceremony, or slip it into a card with the diploma. Many parents struggle to say 'I love you' but show it constantly — this gives words to the gratitude that usually goes unsaid
variations:
I graduated.

But the hardest part of these four years wasn't mine.
It was every time I said "I want to quit"
and you pretended not to hear me,
then made breakfast anyway.

Thank you for never pushing me to be someone else.
You just let me slowly become me.

I know this road wore you out
more than it wore me out.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自畢業勵志短句主題，轉成更貼近現代年輕人迷惘狀態的語氣

zh-tw.content:
「畢業後你要幹嘛？」

我也不知道。
但我知道——

我不用今天就知道。
我也不用下個月就知道。
我只要繼續走，
總有一天會走到自己想去的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合給被「畢業後要幹嘛」這個問題逼到喘不過氣的人。比起硬塞「未來無限可能」這種空話，承認「不知道也沒關係」反而更療癒、更有力量
variations:
二十二歲不知道自己要幹嘛，
是正常的。
四十二歲還在調整方向的人，
比你想像的多很多。

別人問你「以後要做什麼」，
你可以回答：
「我先把今天過好。」

這個答案，沒有錯。

en.content:
"So what are you doing after graduation?"

I don't know.
But here's what I do know —

I don't have to figure it out today.
I don't have to figure it out next month.
I just have to keep walking,
and eventually I'll get somewhere I actually want to be.
en.editorNote: For anyone getting suffocated by the 'what's next' question. Telling someone 'the future is yours' feels hollow — admitting 'it's okay to not know' actually lands and helps
variations:
Being 22 and not knowing what you want
is normal.
There are way more 42-year-olds
still figuring it out than you'd guess.

When people ask what you're doing next,
you're allowed to say:
"I'm focused on getting through today."

That's a real answer.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自大學畢業IG文案主題，融入台灣大學生熟悉的生活場景

zh-tw.content:
大學四年學到的東西：

1. 早八的課，能不選就不選
2. 教授說「這個不會考」就是會考
3. 系上群組越安靜，事情越嚴重
4. 學貸要還的時候，才真的畢業

以上，畢業生敬上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配這段IG文案，是學生時代結束的最佳幽默總結。用條列式呈現四年來的「人生智慧」，會讓同屆的人邊笑邊轉發
variations:
我大學主修：
勉強及格學。
副修：
截止日期前才開始寫報告學。

以上兩個我都拿到學位了。

畢業前最後一個學期，
你會發現：
選修的學分還差三學分，
你最熟的教授不記得你是誰，
但你還是會畢業。

大學就是這樣的地方。

en.content:
Things I learned in four years of college:

1. If you can avoid 8 AM classes, avoid them
2. "This won't be on the test" means it will be on the test
3. The quieter the group chat, the worse the situation
4. You're not really graduated until the student loans show up

Signed, the Class of 2026.
en.editorNote: List-format Instagram caption for grad photos — perfect summary of college life through dry humor. Classmates will laugh, screenshot, and share because everyone lived this
variations:
My college major:
Barely Passing Studies.
Minor:
Starting Papers the Night Before.

Graduated with honors in both.

In your last semester you discover:
you're three credits short on electives,
your favorite professor can't remember your name,
and somehow you're still graduating.

That's just what college is.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自畢業IG感性文案主題，轉成自我對話式的真誠語氣

zh-tw.content:
想跟四年前的自己說：

你會撐過去的。
那些你以為自己過不了的關，
會一個一個過。
那些你哭著想放棄的夜晚，
會變成你最後在台上笑著回想的故事。

謝謝你沒有放棄。
剩下的，我來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮當天發IG，或是寫在最後一篇大學日記。寫給「四年前的自己」這個視角，會讓那些走過低潮的人特別有感觸，比起對未來喊話，對過去溫柔更打動人
variations:
現在的我，
是四年前那個哭著不知道怎麼辦的我，
咬牙撐出來的。

我欠他一個謝謝。

畢業這天我想說：

不是我變強了，
是那個一直沒放棄的我
終於走到這裡了。

他比我厲害。

en.content:
Something I want to tell myself from four years ago:

You make it through.
Every wall you thought you couldn't climb,
you climb.
Every night you cried and wanted to quit
becomes a story you smile through on stage.

Thank you for not giving up.
I've got it from here.
en.editorNote: Great Instagram caption for graduation day, or the last entry in a college journal. Speaking to your past self hits hard for anyone who got through dark times — looking back with gratitude lands deeper than shouting at the future
variations:
Who I am today
is just four-years-ago me
crying and refusing to quit.

I owe him a thank you.

On graduation day I want to say this:

I didn't get stronger.
The version of me who never gave up
finally made it here.

He's the impressive one.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業證書到手的那一天，
求職網站的通知也準時上線。

一張紙說學生時代結束了，
一個App說噩夢才正要開始。

人生的儀式感，原來是同步進行的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業和求職焦慮幾乎同一天報到，這種無縫接軌的荒謬感最有共鳴，傳給一起投履歷的同學剛好
variations: 畢業典禮的隔天，行事曆自動把「上課」改成「面試」，從頭到尾沒人問過我的意見。

en.content:
The same day my diploma showed up,
the job apps started pinging.

One piece of paper said student life is over,
one app said the real nightmare just started.

Apparently life's milestones come bundled now.
en.editorNote: Graduation and job-hunt panic land on basically the same day — that seamless absurdity hits home. Send it to whoever's submitting résumés next to you
variations: The day after graduation my calendar quietly swapped 'class' for 'interviews.' Nobody ran that by me first.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 流蘇撥過去，這一章就翻過去了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 翻自英文caption「Tassel turned, chapter earned」，撥穗那一刻最有畫面感，配學士帽照片一拍即合
variations: 帽子還沒丟出去，新的一章就已經偷偷開始了。

en.content: Tassel turned, chapter earned.
en.editorNote: Short, rhyming, and built for the moving-the-tassel shot — one of the cleanest grad captions you can drop
variations: The cap's still mid-air, but the next chapter already started without asking.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們沒有走散，
只是各自走進了不同的人生。

以後不會每天見面，
但只要想起那幾年，
你還是會第一個出現在我腦海裡。

這樣就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後傳給最好的那群朋友，不灑狗血卻很戳，配上合照效果最好
variations: 我們之間不需要每天聯絡來證明什麼，那幾年早就把證明寫好了。

en.content:
We didn't drift apart.

We just walked into different lives.

We won't see each other every day anymore,
but whenever I think back to those years,
you're still the first face that shows up.

And that's enough.
en.editorNote: For the closest friends after graduation — quiet, not dramatic, and it lands hard next to a group photo
variations: We don't need daily texts to prove anything. Those years already wrote the proof for us.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
別急著知道你要成為誰。

現在的你只是還沒長成的版本，
不是失敗的版本。

慢慢來，這條路本來就不是用衝的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些畢業後焦慮「同學好像都很有方向、只有我還在迷路」的人，溫柔但有力量
variations: 你不是落後了，你只是在用自己的速度長大。

en.content:
Don't rush to figure out who you're supposed to become.

The you right now is just the unfinished version,
not the failed one.

Take your time. This road was never meant to be sprinted.
en.editorNote: For grads spiraling because 'everyone else has a plan and I'm still lost' — gentle, but it carries weight
variations: You're not falling behind. You're just growing up at your own pace.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作（社畜era）

zh-tw.content:
四年下來，
我學會了通宵趕報告、
臨時抱佛腳、
還有把一句廢話講成三千字。

恭喜我，
這些技能在職場全部都用得到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 自嘲大學學到的「實用技能」，職場版的共鳴超強，貼IG限動或傳給同事都好笑
variations: 大學沒教我怎麼報稅，但教會我怎麼把一頁的內容拉成十頁，這個真的有用。

en.content:
Four years in,
I mastered the all-nighter,
last-minute cramming,
and stretching one sentence into a 3,000-word essay.

Congrats to me —
every single one of these transfers straight to the workplace.
en.editorNote: Self-roasting the 'practical skills' college actually taught you — the work-life version hits hard. Great for a Story or sending to coworkers
variations: College never taught me how to file taxes, but it did teach me how to stretch one page into ten. Genuinely useful.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑IG限動梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：
我居然真的畢業了。

感謝Google、感謝考古題、
感謝那個半夜還願意把筆記傳給我的人。

這張全世界最貴的紙，我收下了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配這段限動文字最剛好，自嘲又不油，適合不想寫太正經感言但又想留個紀錄的人
variations: 這張紙花了我四年加無數個崩潰的夜晚，現在它終於是我的了。

en.content:
Plot twist:
I actually graduated.

Shoutout to Google, to past exam papers,
and to whoever was still sending me their notes at 2am.

The most expensive piece of paper on earth — and it's mine now.
en.editorNote: Pair it with a grad photo for a Story — self-deprecating without being cringe, perfect for people who don't want a sappy caption but still want to mark the moment
variations: This paper cost me four years and countless breakdowns. It's finally mine.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，關於不確定，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
不確定，不是壞事。

它只是「還有很多可能」換了一個說法。

你現在站的這個路口看起來很空，
是因為還沒有人告訴你哪條路是你的——
包括你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給畢業後對未來感到茫然的人，把焦慮重新詮釋成「可能性」，溫柔又有力量
variations: 你以為的迷路，其實是還沒被決定的自由。

en.content:
Uncertainty isn't a bad thing.

It's just possibility wearing a different name.

The crossroads you're standing at looks empty
because no one's told you which road is yours yet —
including you.
en.editorNote: For grads feeling lost about what's next — reframes the anxiety as possibility. Gentle but it lands
variations: What feels like being lost is really just freedom that hasn't been spent yet.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志短句，關於勇氣與出發，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業證書幫你打開了門，

但真正走進去的，
是你的勇氣，不是那張紙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短到可以直接當IG貼文文案或畢業卡片祝福，給即將踏入社會的人最實在的提醒
variations: 學歷負責敲門，剩下的路，要靠你自己走進去。

en.content:
Your diploma opens the door.

But the thing that actually walks through it
is your courage — not the paper.
en.editorNote: Short enough to drop straight into an Instagram caption or a grad card — a grounded reminder for anyone stepping into the real world
variations: A degree knocks. Courage is what walks in.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
歷時十六年的超長組員專案，
終於結案。

沒有續約，謝謝大家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把整個求學生涯講成一個終於做完的專案，職場與學生都懂的冷面幽默，適合貼限動
variations: 讀書這個專案我做了十六年，今天正式驗收通過，不接續約。

en.content:
A sixteen-year-long group project
has finally wrapped.

No renewal. Thanks, everyone.
en.editorNote: Frames your entire school career as one project that's finally done — deadpan humor both students and office workers get. Great for a Story
variations: I've been on this group project for sixteen years. Final review passed. Declining the contract extension.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
順利畢業。
目前無業。
但心態樂觀。

三個狀態，同時成立。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把畢業的尷尬現實講得很坦然，自嘲又不沮喪，最適合貼在畢業照下面當IG文案
variations: 畢業了、待業中、心情好——這就是我現在的三圍。

en.content:
Graduated.
Unemployed.
Optimistic.

All three, at the same time.
en.editorNote: Owns the awkward reality of graduating with zero self-pity — perfect deadpan caption to sit under a cap-and-gown photo
variations: Status update: degree acquired, job pending, vibes immaculate.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
想看看全世界最貴的一張紙長什麼樣子嗎？

來，就是這張。

（請忽略後面那串貸款。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 拿畢業證書開貸款的玩笑，是大學生最有共鳴的自嘲，配畢業證書特寫照超適合
variations: 這張紙很貴，貴到我接下來幾年都會記得它有多貴。

en.content:
Ever wondered what the world's most expensive piece of paper looks like?

Here you go.

(Please ignore the loan attached to it.)
en.editorNote: The student-loan joke every grad secretly relates to — pairs perfectly with a close-up shot of the diploma
variations: Most expensive paper I'll ever own. And I'll be reminded of the price for years.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業不是終點線。

它比較像跑道的盡頭——
那不是要你停下來，
是要你開始飛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業卡片或致詞引用，把「結束」翻轉成「起飛」，給對未來感到不安的人一點力量
variations: 你以為這是抵達，其實這是起跑。
求學是助跑，畢業才是真正離地的那一刻。

en.content:
Graduation isn't the finish line.

It's more like the end of a runway —
not a place to stop,
but the point where you start to fly.
en.editorNote: Great for a grad card or a speech line — reframes the ending as a launch, a little reassurance for anyone nervous about what's next
variations: You thought this was the arrival. It's actually the takeoff.
School was the runway. Graduation is the moment you leave the ground.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
身後，是你所有的回憶。
眼前，是你所有的夢。

別急著回頭，
但也別忘了，你是被那些回憶送到這裡的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在畢業合照下方，或寫進給好友的卡片，兼顧懷念過去與期待未來，情緒很細膩
variations: 回頭看是滿滿的回憶，往前走是還沒拆封的夢。
走過的每一段，都在替現在的你鋪路——包括那些當時覺得撐不過去的。

en.content:
Behind you, every memory.
Ahead of you, every dream.

Don't rush to look back —
but don't forget those memories are what carried you here.
en.editorNote: Lovely under a group graduation photo or inside a card to a close friend — holds both the nostalgia and the hope without tipping into cheesy
variations: Look back and it's all memories. Look forward and it's dreams still unopened.
Every chapter you survived paved the road you're standing on now — even the ones that felt impossible.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在這個值得紀念的日子，
我想感謝幾位陪我走完全程的恩人：

Google、維基百科、ChatGPT，
還有發明複製貼上的那位天才。

沒有你們就沒有今天的我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業謝詞或IG發文都很適合，自嘲又精準，現代學生看了都會心一笑；想再加碼可以把名單換成自己真正靠過的工具
variations: 畢業證書上應該要加掛三個人的名字：我、咖啡、還有截稿前的恐懼。
感謝爸媽，感謝老師，但最該感謝的其實是凌晨三點還沒睡的我。

en.content:
On this momentous day,
I'd like to thank the ones who got me here:

Google, Wikipedia, ChatGPT,
and whoever invented copy and paste.

I literally couldn't have done it without you.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a grad caption or a tongue-in-cheek speech line — self-aware and painfully accurate for any modern student; swap the list for the tools you actually leaned on
variations: My diploma should really list three names: me, coffee, and pure deadline panic.
Thanks Mom, thanks Dad — but the real MVP was 3 a.m. me who refused to sleep.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們說，畢業之後我可以成為任何我想成為的人。

所以我選擇成為一個——
非常累的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 厭世系畢業文必備，短到可以直接當IG限動文字；想反轉一下情緒可以接「不過至少是自己選的累」
variations: 從小被說「你以後可以做任何事」，長大才發現「任何事」裡面包含了「加班」。
畢業典禮上他們喊著前途無量，沒人提醒我前途也無薪一陣子。

en.content:
They told me that after graduation
I could become anything I wanted.

So I became
extremely tired.
en.editorNote: A staple for the burnt-out grad post — short enough to drop straight onto an Instagram Story; soften it with a follow-up like "but at least it's a tired I chose"
variations: Grew up hearing "you can do anything." Turns out "anything" quietly included "overtime."
At the ceremony they shouted about a bright future. Nobody mentioned the unpaid part comes first.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
未來，是留給那些相信自己夢想很美的人的。

所以別管別人說你的夢想太大、太遠、太天真。
你信，它就有重量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫進畢業卡片送給有目標的朋友，或當畢業致詞的收尾金句，正能量但不空泛
variations: 夢想會不會實現，先看你願不願意相信它值得。
別人覺得你的夢想太大，是因為那不是他們的夢想，不是你的問題。

en.content:
The future belongs to the ones
who believe their dreams are worth it.

So let them say your dream is too big,
too far, too naive.
The moment you believe it, it has weight.
en.editorNote: Slot it into a grad card for a friend with a goal, or use it as the closing line of a speech — uplifting without floating off into cliché
variations: Whether your dream comes true starts with whether you'll believe it deserves to.
People call your dream too big because it isn't theirs — that's their limit, not yours.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
每一個新的開始，
都是從另一個結束長出來的。

所以今天不是失去了什麼，
是有一段路，正準備在你腳下展開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在畢業合照下方或寫進卡片，幫還在捨不得的人把「結束」重新看成「開場」，情緒收得很穩
variations: 門關上的聲音，有時候其實是另一扇門打開的前奏。
你以為這是句點，它只是換行而已。

en.content:
Every new beginning
grows out of some other beginning's end.

So today you didn't lose anything —
a whole road is just getting ready
to open up under your feet.
en.editorNote: Nice under a group grad photo or inside a card — helps anyone who's still grieving the goodbye reframe "ending" as "opening," lands the emotion without overdoing it
variations: Sometimes the sound of a door closing is just the intro to another one opening.
You thought this was a period. It's only a line break.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你有沒有想過，
全世界最貴的一張紙長什麼樣子？

喏，在我手上。
四年的學費，換來這一張可以裱框的紙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 拿著畢業證書拍照配這段文字超有梗，自嫌兼炫耀一次到位；數字可依實際學制改成「兩年」「六年」
variations: 這張紙的單位面積成本，大概贏過我家所有家具。
別人收藏郵票、收藏球鞋，我收藏一張用四年青春換來的紙。

en.content:
Ever wondered what the most expensive
piece of paper in the world looks like?

Here it is, right in my hand.
Four years of tuition for one frame-worthy sheet.
en.editorNote: Pair it with a photo of you holding the diploma — equal parts flex and self-roast; change "four years" to match your actual program
variations: Per square inch, this sheet of paper cost more than everything in my apartment.
Some people collect stamps or sneakers. I collect one piece of paper that cost me four years.

zh-tw.content:
敢走太遠的人，
才有機會知道自己到底能走多遠。

所以別急著回頭，
你連一半的路都還沒看到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業旅行或一個人出發那天貼在IG，配上機場、車站或一條空蕩蕩的路；送給正準備離開舒適圈、去外地工作或留學的朋友也很對味
variations: 你會怕，是因為這條路夠遠；夠遠，才值得走。
現在不冒險，等你老了就只剩後悔可以冒了。

en.content:
Only the ones brave enough to go too far
ever find out how far they can actually go.

So don't turn back yet —
you haven't even seen half the road.
en.editorNote: Great for the day you head off solo — pair it with an airport, a train platform, or an empty open road; also lands well sent to a friend leaving their comfort zone for a new city or to study abroad
variations: If it scares you, it's because the road is long. And long roads are the ones worth walking.
Take the risk now. Wait too long and the only thing left to gamble on is regret.

zh-tw.content:
他們說，
未來屬於相信夢想的人。

我是滿相信的啦，
只希望未來也屬於付得出房租的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業季求職焦慮時自嘲一下剛剛好，貼在IG限動很有共鳴；面試前互相打氣傳給同學也很可愛
variations: 夢想很重要，但這個月先把房租繳一繳。
我相信夢想，夢想最好也相信我繳得出帳單。

en.content:
They say the future belongs
to those who believe in their dreams.

I do believe, honestly —
I just hope it also belongs to those who can make rent.
