en.editorNote: Perfect self-deprecating caption for job-hunting season; post it as an IG story for instant relatability, or send it to a classmate as a pre-interview pep talk
variations: Dreams matter. So does making rent this month.
I believe in my dreams. I just need my dreams to believe in my ability to pay bills.

zh-tw.content:
恭喜你畢業。

從今天起，
你可以用一種更有學問的方式，
繼續沒錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業卡上寫這句保證笑出來，土味祝福之餘又很真實；配學貸或薪水梗一起發更有共鳴
variations: 畢業證書到手，恭喜你正式取得「有文憑的窮人」資格。
讀了這麼多年，終於學會怎麼有氣質地吃土。

en.content:
Congratulations on graduating.

Starting today,
you're free to be broke
in a far more educated way.
en.editorNote: Write this in a grad card for a guaranteed laugh — cheesy congrats with a very real edge; pairs nicely with any student-loan or first-paycheck joke
variations: Diploma unlocked. You now officially qualify as a broke person with credentials.
All those years of study, and you finally learned how to be poor with style.

zh-tw.content:
歡迎來到真實世界。

這裡跟學校很像，
一樣有交不完的東西、做不完的事，
只是這次的截止日期，
會真的咬人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛進職場、被現實洗禮的新鮮人互相取暖，貼在IG或群組都很有梗；想反差一點可搭配西裝照或第一天上班的照片
variations: 真實世界的截止日不會延，老闆也不收病假單當補交理由。
以前熬夜趕報告，現在熬夜趕的東西沒有學分，只有KPI。

en.content:
Welcome to the real world.

It's a lot like school —
endless things due, endless things to do —
except this time
the deadlines actually bite.
en.editorNote: Made for fresh grads getting their first taste of reality; great in a group chat or as an IG caption, especially paired with a first-day-at-work or business-attire photo for contrast
variations: Real-world deadlines don't grant extensions, and your boss won't take a doctor's note for late work.
You used to pull all-nighters for grades. Now you pull them for KPIs — no credit, no GPA, just vibes and dread.

zh-tw.content:
在你真的去做之前，
它都會看起來像不可能。

做完之後，
它就只是你做過的一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 簡短有力，適合當畢業致詞金句或IG caption；送給對未來沒把握、覺得目標太遠的同學特別合適
variations: 所謂不可能，只是還沒被你完成的那一件事。
別問做不做得到，做了你就知道了。

en.content:
It only looks impossible
right up until you do it.

After that,
it's just something you did.
en.editorNote: Short and punchy — works as a graduation speech line or an IG caption; especially good for a classmate who's unsure about the future or feels their goals are out of reach
variations: Impossible is just the thing you haven't finished yet.
Stop asking whether you can. Do it, and you'll find out.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業典禮恭喜你，
你現在已經完全有資格
做你之前在做的那些打工了。

而且這次還附上學貸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月畢業季最有共鳴的自嘲，適合貼在IG配學士服照片，或傳到同學群組一起苦笑；剛投履歷被「需要相關經驗」打槍的新鮮人會特別有感
variations: 讀了四年，總算可以名正言順地說：我不知道我在幹嘛。
畢業證書到手，下一個關卡：證明這張紙真的有用。

en.content:
Congratulations, graduate.

You are now fully qualified
for all the jobs you were already doing.

Now with student loans included.
en.editorNote: The most relatable self-deprecating line for June grad season — pair it with a cap-and-gown photo on Instagram, or drop it in the group chat for a collective laugh; hits hardest for new grads getting rejected for lacking 'relevant experience'
variations: Four years of school just so I can confidently say I have no idea what I'm doing.
Diploma acquired. Next level: proving this piece of paper actually does something.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這段友情不會因為畢業而結束，
它只是換了一種方式存在。

以後不能每天見面了，
但只要一通電話，
我們還是可以從上次中斷的地方繼續講下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月畢業季送給最捨不得的那位同學，寫在卡片或畢業紀念冊上都很合適；比起「畢業快樂」更能說中那種「以後就不一樣了」的心情
variations: 畢業不是分開，是我們把同一個校園，換成了各自的人生。
好的友情不怕分隔兩地，怕的是再也不主動聯絡——所以這次換我先傳訊息。

en.content:
This friendship doesn't end with graduation.

It just changes shape.

We won't see each other every day anymore,
but one phone call,
and we'll pick up right where we left off.
en.editorNote: For June grad season — give this to the classmate you'll miss most, perfect for a card or yearbook message; says what 'happy graduation' can't quite capture about things never being the same
variations: Graduation isn't goodbye. We just traded one shared campus for our own separate lives.
Real friendship survives the distance — what it can't survive is no one reaching out. So this time, I'll text first.

items.sourceNote: 畢業IG短句，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我來了，我看了，我畢業了。

剩下的部分，再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 極短的IG caption，懶得想長篇感言時直接用；配畢業照、撥穗瞬間或丟學士帽的照片都很對味
variations: 計畫：畢業。完成度：100%。下一步：載入中⋯
通關了。接下來的關卡還沒解鎖，但先慶祝再說。

en.content:
I came, I saw, I graduated.

The rest is still loading.
en.editorNote: A super-short IG caption for when you can't be bothered to write a whole speech; pairs perfectly with a grad photo, the tassel-flip moment, or a cap toss
variations: Plan: graduate. Progress: 100%. Next step: loading...
Level cleared. Haven't unlocked what's next, but we're celebrating anyway.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
別急著知道未來會變成什麼樣子。

你現在站的地方，
曾經也是你以為自己到不了的未來。

往前走就對了，
答案會在路上慢慢出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月畢業季送給對前途焦慮、覺得「畢業之後到底要幹嘛」的同學，溫暖但不空泛；當畢業致詞金句或卡片結尾都很有力
variations: 你不必現在就看懂整條路，看清楚下一步就夠了。
今天站的這裡，就是你以前拼命想到達的那個地方——所以別怕，繼續走。

en.content:
Don't rush to know how the future turns out.

Where you're standing right now
was once a future you thought you'd never reach.

Just keep walking.
The answers show up along the way.
en.editorNote: For June grad season — for the classmate anxious about what's next, warm without being hollow; works as a graduation speech line or a strong closing for a card
variations: You don't need to see the whole road today. Seeing the next step is enough.
Right where you stand now is the place you once fought so hard to reach — so don't be afraid. Keep going.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我畢業了。

意思是我現在正式成為一個
很會假裝自己知道在幹嘛的人。

畢竟這四年都在練這個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在畢業照IG限動超有梗，特別適合那些撐過無數報告、其實還沒準備好出社會的人；自嘲又療癒
variations: 畢業證書到手，正式取得「在會議上點頭但其實沒聽懂」的執照。
別人問我畢業有什麼規劃，我也很想知道。

en.content:
I graduated.

Which officially makes me a professional
at pretending I know what I'm doing.

Four years of solid practice.
en.editorNote: A great Instagram story caption with your grad photo — perfect for the ones who survived endless deadlines but aren't quite ready for the real world; self-deprecating and oddly comforting
variations: Diploma acquired. I am now licensed to nod in meetings without understanding a single thing.
People keep asking what my plans are after graduation. I'd love to know too.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，取自畢業致詞核心概念重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你會害怕，是因為前面什麼都還沒定形。

但別忘了，
沒定形的東西，
才是可以被你親手捏成想要的樣子的。

這個世界比你以為的還軟，
它在等你出手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月畢業季送給對未來迷惘卻又躍躍欲試的同學，把「不確定」翻轉成「有機會」；當畢業卡片金句或致詞收尾都很有力量
variations: 未來還沒寫死，這不是壞消息，這是你的空白頁。
正因為一切都還沒決定，所以一切都還來得及由你決定。

en.content:
You're scared because nothing ahead has taken shape yet.

But remember —
things that haven't set
are exactly the things you can mold with your own hands.

The world is softer than you think.
It's waiting for you to make your move.
en.editorNote: For June grad season — for the classmate who's anxious about the future but secretly itching to start; reframes uncertainty as opportunity. Strong as a card line or a closing for a speech
variations: The future isn't written yet. That's not bad news — that's your blank page.
Because nothing's decided yet, everything is still yours to decide.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，我們會各自忙各自的。

訊息會越回越慢，
見面會越約越難，
但有些人不用每天聯絡，
也還是會在你心裡佔一個位子。

那個位子，誰都搶不走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後傳給最捨不得的那群朋友，或貼在合照下方；說出了「不常聯絡但永遠重要」的那種感情
variations: 不是不聯絡了，是我們都長大了——但你永遠是我青春裡刪不掉的那一段。
有些友情不靠頻率維持，靠的是一句『欸你還記得那時候嗎』。

en.content:
After graduation, we'll all get busy with our own lives.

Replies will get slower,
meetups harder to plan,
but some people don't need daily contact
to keep their spot in your heart.

And that spot? No one can take it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friends you can't bear to leave after the ceremony, or post under a group photo; captures the kind of bond that stays important even when you rarely talk
variations: It's not that we stopped talking — we just grew up. But you'll always be a chapter of my youth I can't delete.
Some friendships don't run on frequency. They run on 'hey, remember that time?'

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們說大學是人生最美好的四年。

我的存款表示：
它有不同的看法。
zh-tw.editorNote: 超短一句適合IG貼文或限動配畢業照，誰看了都會心一笑；剛經歷學貸、打工、吃泡麵的人最有感
variations: 畢業最大的收穫：一張證書，外加一身不知道怎麼養成的省錢技能。
回顧大學四年，學到最多的科目是『如何用最少的錢活下去』。

en.content:
They say college is the best four years of your life.

My bank balance
would like to file a dissent.
en.editorNote: A super short line for an Instagram post or story with your grad photo — guaranteed smile; hits hardest for anyone who survived loans, part-time jobs, and instant noodles
variations: Biggest takeaway from graduation: one diploma, plus a set of money-saving skills I never signed up for.
Four years of college, and my best subject turned out to be 'surviving on the least money possible.'

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天你把帽子拋上天，
那不是結束的記號，
是『開工』的信號。

書唸完了，
但真正想搞懂的東西，
現在才正要開始學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞、卡片或IG長文，把「畢業」重新定義成「另一種開始」；給那些以為念完書就解脫、其實人生才正要展開的同學
variations: 畢業不是把書本闔上，是把世界這本書翻開第一頁。
證書代表你學完了該學的，接下來要學的，課表上都沒寫。

en.content:
When you toss your cap in the air today,
it's not a sign of the end.
It's the signal to clock in.

The books are done,
but the stuff you actually want to figure out?
That's only just beginning.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation speech, a card, or a longer Instagram post — redefines 'graduation' as another kind of beginning; for the classmate who thinks finishing school means it's over, when really life is just getting started
variations: Graduation isn't closing the textbook. It's opening the world to page one.
Your diploma says you learned what you were taught. What comes next isn't on any syllabus.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，
我正式從『假裝在唸書』，
升級成『假裝知道自己在幹嘛』。

聽起來很像進步，
其實只是換了個劇本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼IG限動配畢業照，自嘲又不失幽默；給那些頂著畢業證書、心裡其實一片空白的人，比假掰的勵志文更討喜
variations: 畢業證書的意思是：恭喜你，從此可以用更專業的姿態繼續迷惘。
別人問我畢業後有什麼規劃，我規劃了一個很有自信的微笑回應他。

en.content:
After graduating,
I officially leveled up
from 'pretending to study'
to 'pretending I know what I'm doing.'

Sounds like progress.
Really it's just a new script.
en.editorNote: Perfect for an Instagram story with a grad photo — self-deprecating without trying too hard; for everyone holding a diploma with a blank mind behind it
variations: A diploma means: congratulations, you may now be confused at a more professional level.
When people ask my plans after graduation, my plan is a very confident smile.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不是說我撐過了四年大學，
我是說——
我『真的』撐過了四年大學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 超短一句，重複強調製造笑點，適合IG貼文標題或限動文字；不需多解釋，懂的人秒懂那份劫後餘生
variations: 畢業心得只有四個字：我還活著。
回顧大學最大的成就，是我居然真的走到了今天。

en.content:
I'm not saying I survived four years of college.

I'm saying
I survived four years of college.
en.editorNote: A super short line that lands through repetition — great as an Instagram caption or story text; no explanation needed, the survivors get it instantly
variations: My entire graduation reflection: I'm still here.
Looking back at college, my greatest achievement is that I actually made it to today.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你身後留下的，
和你前方等著的，
都比不上你心裡裝著的那些東西。

證書會泛黃，
但那股『我做得到』的勁，
會跟著你走很遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 改寫自愛默生名句的精神內核，適合畢業卡片、致詞或IG長文；給那些覺得自己一無所有、其實最強裝備一直在身上的畢業生
variations: 別回頭數你失去什麼，往前看你帶得走的——那才是真正屬於你的行李。
學校給你的是知識，真正帶你走過後面那段路的，是你自己長出來的膽量。

en.content:
What you leave behind,
and what waits up ahead,
are small things
next to what you carry inside.

The diploma will yellow.
But that 'I can do this' spark
will take you a long, long way.
en.editorNote: Reworks the spirit of Emerson's famous line — great for a graduation card, speech, or longer Instagram post; for the grad who feels they have nothing, when their best gear was inside all along
variations: Don't look back counting what you lost. Look ahead at what you get to keep — that's the only luggage that's truly yours.
School handed you knowledge. What actually carries you through the next stretch is the nerve you grew yourself.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
想感謝這一路上幫過我的人，
名單很長，
但最想感謝的，
是那些在我自己都不相信我的時候，
還默默挺我的——咖啡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞開場破冰，或IG貼文配上一杯咖啡和畢業帽的照片；熬夜趕報告、靠咖啡因續命過來的人最有感
variations: 我的畢業證書，有一半是咖啡因撐起來的。
致謝名單第一名：那台從沒讓我失望的咖啡機。

en.content:
I'd like to thank everyone who helped me get here.
It's a long list.

But top of all of them:
the coffee that believed in me
when I didn't believe in myself.
en.editorNote: Great as an icebreaker opening for a grad speech, or an Instagram post with a coffee cup and a grad cap; hits home for anyone who ran on caffeine through all-nighters
variations: Half of this diploma was held up by pure caffeine.
Number one on my thank-you list: the coffee machine that never let me down.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
每一個結束，
其實都是另一個開始的起點。

所以今天不用太捨不得——
你不是在關上一扇門，
你只是還沒看到，
下一扇正在為你打開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 化用「每個開始都來自另一個結束」的意境，適合畢業典禮後傳給情緒滿出來的朋友，或貼IG安撫那份離別的不捨
variations: 別把畢業當成散場，把它當成下一段故事的第一頁。
捨不得是真的，但前面等你的好事，也是真的。

en.content:
Every ending
is just the starting line of something else.

So don't ache too much today —
you're not closing a door.
You just haven't seen yet
the one already opening for you.
en.editorNote: Channels the spirit of 'every new beginning comes from some other beginning's end' — send it to a friend overflowing with emotion after the ceremony, or post it to soften the sting of goodbye
variations: Don't treat graduation as the credits rolling. Treat it as page one of the next story.
The ache is real. But so are the good things waiting up ahead for you.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑IG文案（求職焦慮），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
成功完成四年
假裝自己知道在幹嘛的訓練。

接下來要進入職場，
繼續無縫接軌地假裝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給所有畢業即失業、或剛踏入職場還在裝懂的人，發限動或貼文都很有共鳴，朋友看了會說「這也太是我」
variations: 他們說我可以成為任何人，於是我成為了一個很累的人。
已畢業。待業中。但保持樂觀（暫時）。

en.content:
Successfully completed four years
of pretending I knew what I was doing.

Next stop: the workforce,
where the pretending continues seamlessly.
en.editorNote: For everyone who graduated straight into unemployment, or just started a job and is still faking competence — works as a story or a post, and friends will reply 'this is so me'
variations: They told me I could be anything, so I became tired.
Graduated. Unemployed. Optimistic (for now).

items.sourceNote: 化用 Bono 賓州大學畢業演講「世界比你想的更有可塑性」意境，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這個世界，
比你以為的還要軟，還要好捏。

它不是早就定型的水泥，
而是一塊還在等你動手的黏土——
所以別客氣，
捏成你想要的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給對未來感到迷惘、覺得「一切早就被決定了」的畢業生，提醒對方世界其實還沒成形，主動權在自己手上
variations: 未來不是寫死的劇本，是還沒乾的水泥——趁現在，把你的腳印踩上去。
別等世界給你位置，世界正在等你出手。

en.content:
The world
is softer than you think.

It isn't concrete that already set —
it's clay still waiting for your hands.
So don't hold back.
Shape it into what you want.
en.editorNote: Send it to a graduate who feels lost or convinced everything is already decided — a reminder that the world isn't finished forming, and they're the one holding the tools
variations: The future isn't a locked script. It's wet cement — press your footprint in before it dries.
Don't wait for the world to hand you a spot. The world is waiting on your move.

items.sourceNote: 化用 Emerson「真正重要的是內在的力量」意境，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你走過的路，
還有眼前要走的路，
跟你心裡帶著的那股力量比起來，
其實都是小事。

畢業帶不走那張桌子、那間教室，
但你帶得走最重要的——
那個變強了的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮卡片或長輩、學長姐寫給後輩的祝福，比起祝「前途光明」更有重量，強調真正的資產是內在
variations: 外面的風浪再大，都不及你心裡那份穩。
證書會放進抽屜，但這幾年練出來的你，會跟著你走一輩子。

en.content:
The road behind you
and the road still ahead
are small things
next to the strength you carry inside.

Graduation can't pack up the desk or the classroom,
but you get to keep the one thing that matters —
the stronger version of you.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation card or a note from a mentor or older sibling to someone just starting out — it carries more weight than 'bright future ahead' by pointing to inner growth as the real asset
variations: No storm out there outweighs the steadiness you've built in here.
The diploma goes in a drawer. The person these years made of you goes everywhere.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短文案（嗆親戚版），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業了，
現在我正式擁有資格，
向永遠聽不懂的親戚
解釋我到底讀了什麼科系。
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年發限動或畢業文必備，專治那些問「這個出來能做什麼」的長輩，自嘲又精準，留言區會被同溫層佔領
variations: 我念的科系，連我自己都還在搞懂，更別說要跟三姑六婆解釋了。
畢業最大的成就：終於可以理直氣壯地說「這很難解釋」。

en.content:
Graduated.

I am now officially qualified
to explain my major
to relatives who will never understand it.
en.editorNote: A must-post for graduation season or family-gathering stories — aimed squarely at the relatives who ask 'so what can you even do with that,' self-deprecating and precise enough to flood your comments with kindred spirits
variations: I'm still figuring out my own major, never mind explaining it to the whole extended family.
Biggest achievement of graduating: I can now confidently say 'it's hard to explain.'

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業了。
失業了。
但我很樂觀。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業季發限動或當IG貼文超有共鳴，三行精準命中剛畢業還在投履歷的心情，自嘲到位又不喪氣，留言區會一片「這就是我」
variations: 畢業典禮上他們說我可以成為任何人，於是我成為了一個正在等回信的待業者。
目前狀態：拿到全世界最貴的一張紙，正在用它換一份工作。

en.content:
Graduated.
Unemployed.
Still optimistic.
en.editorNote: Drop this as a story or post during graduation season — three lines that nail the just-graduated-and-job-hunting mood, self-aware without being bleak, guaranteed to fill your comments with 'literally me'
variations: At graduation they told me I could be anyone, so I became someone refreshing their inbox.
Current status: holding the most expensive piece of paper on earth, trying to trade it for a job.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
四年下來，
我成功完成了一件事：
假裝自己知道在做什麼，
而且沒被發現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業文或畢業帽標語都很適合，戳中每個假裝很懂其實邊做邊查的人，配畢業照發出去會引爆同溫層共鳴
variations: 我的學位證明的不是我學會了什麼，而是我撐過了什麼。
畢業的真相：不是變厲害了，是變得很會裝沒事。

en.content:
Four years later,
I have officially mastered one skill:
looking like I know what I'm doing
without getting caught.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation post or a cap slogan — it speaks to everyone who faked confidence while secretly googling everything, pair it with your grad photo and watch the kindred spirits pile in
variations: My diploma doesn't prove what I learned. It proves what I survived.
The truth about graduating: I didn't get smarter, I just got better at pretending I'm fine.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人勵志語錄主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這一刻不是結束。

是你終於有機會，
成為那個一直藏在心裡、
還沒來得及長出來的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫進畢業卡片或送給即將踏入新階段的朋友，溫柔又有力量，比起喊「加油」更能打動人心
variations: 畢業不是抵達終點，是你終於拿到了出發的票。
今天合上的不是一本書，是一個章節——故事還很長，主角還是你。

en.content:
This moment isn't an ending.

It's the first chance you've had
to become the person
you've been quietly carrying inside all along.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a graduation card or for a friend stepping into a new chapter — gentle but powerful, it lands harder than a generic 'good luck'
variations: Graduation isn't reaching the finish line. It's finally getting handed your ticket to leave.
What closed today wasn't a book — it was a chapter. The story's still long, and you're still the lead.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄主題（行動與未來），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
沒有人能告訴你未來會怎樣。

但有一件事是確定的：
你不會用「等」的，
等到一個你想要的未來。

未來是走出來的，不是等出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞、卡片祝福，或送給對未來感到迷惘的朋友，務實又激勵，不是空泛的雞湯
variations: 預測未來最好的方法，就是親手把它做出來。
你不必看清整條路，只要先走出第一步，下一步自然會出現。

en.content:
Nobody can tell you how the future turns out.

But one thing is certain:
you won't get the future you want
by waiting for it.

The future is walked into, not waited for.
en.editorNote: Works for a graduation speech, a card, or a friend who feels lost about what's next — grounded and motivating rather than empty feel-good filler
variations: The best way to predict your future is to build it with your own hands.
You don't need to see the whole road. Take the first step and the next one shows up.

items.sourceNote: 畢業短句語錄（IG標語）主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
流過的汗、熬過的夜、補考前的崩潰——

全部，
換成了這一頂帽子上的流蘇。

值了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照配文首選，短短幾句把四年的辛苦濃縮成一個畫面，丟到IG當caption剛剛好，畢業帽流蘇的特寫照最搭
variations: 這條流蘇，是用無數個趕報告的夜晚換來的。
回頭看那些撐不下去的瞬間，原來都在替今天鋪路。

en.content:
Every all-nighter, every panic before an exam,
every 'I can't do this'—

all of it
traded in
for the tassel on this cap.

Worth it.
en.editorNote: A go-to caption for grad photos — it compresses four years of struggle into one image, perfect length for Instagram and made for a close-up of that tassel
variations: This tassel cost me a hundred deadline nights. Paid in full.
Turns out every moment I almost quit was quietly paving the way to today.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（畢業是開始不是結束）主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業證書證明的，
不是你會結束一件事，
是你扛得完一件事。

那現在——
換你去證明，
你也敢開始一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫進畢業卡片或畢業致詞，也很適合傳給對未來感到迷惘的朋友，比空泛的「加油」更有重量
variations: 畢業不是終點線，是換你自己鳴槍起跑。
你已經證明你撐得到最後，接下來要證明的是：你敢踏出第一步。

en.content:
Your diploma isn't proof
that you can finish things.

It's proof you can carry one
all the way through.

Now—
go prove
you can begin one too.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation card or speech, and for a friend who feels lost about what's next — carries more weight than a hollow 'good luck'
variations: Graduation isn't the finish line. It's where you fire the starting gun yourself.
You've proven you can make it to the end. Now prove you're brave enough to start.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄（畢業後現實）主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我花了四年、燒了一筆學費，
終於拿到一張紙，

證明我有能力，
去做一份跟我科系完全無關的工作。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業照或求職季貼IG的自嘲文，剛畢業還在投履歷的人看了會心一笑，比正經畢業感言更有梗
variations: 四年所學，求職時被一句「歡迎重新培訓」全部歸零。
畢業證書：一張很貴的、確認你已經長大要繳稅了的紙。

en.content:
Four years and a small fortune in tuition later,
I finally earned a piece of paper

proving I'm fully qualified
to do a job that has nothing to do with my major.
en.editorNote: A self-deprecating caption for grad photos or job-hunting season — freshly graduated people still firing off applications will grin at this, way funnier than a solemn graduation reflection
variations: Four years of study, instantly reset by the words 'we'll train you from scratch.'
Diploma: a very expensive piece of paper confirming you're now an adult who pays taxes.

items.sourceNote: 畢業致詞語錄（不要因為害怕而做決定）主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
出社會以後你會發現，
讓人後悔的，
很少是「我做了卻失敗」。

大多數是——
「我當時太怕了，所以我沒做。」

別把人生交給害怕來決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞收尾，或送給正站在人生岔路、不敢做選擇的朋友，比口號式的鼓勵更打中人
variations: 你會後悔的，通常不是失敗，是當初連試都沒試。
別讓「萬一」這兩個字，幫你把人生過完。

en.content:
Out in the real world you'll learn
that the regrets that sting
are rarely 'I tried and failed.'

Most of them are—
'I was too scared, so I didn't.'

Don't let fear make your decisions for you.
en.editorNote: A strong closer for a graduation speech, or a message for a friend frozen at a crossroads — lands harder than slogan-style encouragement
variations: What you'll regret usually isn't the failure. It's never having tried at all.
Don't let the word 'what if' live your whole life for you.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄（同學情誼）主題，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們大概不會再像現在這樣，
每天見面、坐在一起發呆。

但我不擔心。

因為有些朋友，
不是靠每天見面維繫的，
是靠那段一起長大的時間，
一輩子算數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮後傳給死黨，或配上一起拍的畢業合照貼IG，溫暖但不煽情，最適合那種「不常聯絡但永遠在」的友情
variations: 以後就算各奔東西，那段一起翹課、一起趕報告的日子，誰都拿不走。
好的友情不怕距離，怕的只是當初沒有好好一起瘋過。

en.content:
We probably won't get to do this again—
see each other every day,
sit around doing nothing together.

But I'm not worried.

Because some friendships
aren't held together by seeing each other daily.
They're held by the years you grew up side by side—
and those count for life.
en.editorNote: Send to your closest friends after the ceremony, or pair with a group grad photo on Instagram — warm without being sappy, perfect for the 'we don't talk often but we're always there' kind of friendship
variations: Even when we scatter, no one can take away the classes we skipped and the deadlines we survived together.
Real friendship doesn't fear distance. It only regrets not going a little wild together while it could.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業了。
失業了。
但我很樂觀。

畢竟前兩件事我都做到了，
第三件總有一天也會成真吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛丟出履歷、還在等回音的畢業生，自嘲又不悲情，貼IG限動或傳給一起待業的同學都很對味
variations: 畢業、待業、保持樂觀——人生三連發，目前完成度六成七。
別人畢業是邁向人生新階段，我畢業是邁向人力銀行的會員等級。

en.content:
Graduated.
Unemployed.
Still optimistic.

I mean, I pulled off the first two—
the third one's bound to come around eventually.
en.editorNote: Perfect for grads who just hit submit on a dozen applications and are refreshing their inbox — self-deprecating without being a downer, great for a story or a text to your fellow job-hunters
variations: Graduated, unemployed, optimistic — life's hat trick, currently two-thirds complete.
For some, graduation is the start of a new chapter. For me, it's a new tier of job-board membership.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我來了，
我看見了，
我活過了無數次的小組報告。

畢業證書上沒寫，
但這才是我這幾年真正的成就。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給每一個扛過豬隊友、半夜趕簡報的人，配上畢業合照超有梗，留言區一定一堆人喊+1
variations: 讀書我不一定最強，但在沒人回訊息的群組裡獨自完成報告，我是專家。
畢業最大的成就不是成績單，是我居然在那些小組報告裡活了下來。

en.content:
I came.
I saw.
I survived approximately one hundred group projects.

The diploma doesn't mention it,
but that's the real achievement here.
en.editorNote: For everyone who carried the dead-weight teammates and built the whole slide deck at 3am — pair it with a grad photo and watch the comments fill up with 'so real'
variations: I may not have been the smartest in the room, but finishing a project solo in a group chat where no one replied? Expert level.
My biggest graduation flex isn't the GPA. It's that I made it out of those group projects alive.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
未來還很模糊，
這沒關係。

模糊的意思不是迷路，
是還沒被人決定。

而那張白紙，
剛好交到你手上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合對畢業後感到迷惘的人，把「不確定」重新詮釋成「還有得選」，溫柔但有力量，當畢業祝福送人很加分
variations: 前方看不清，不代表沒有路，只代表那條路還在等你親自畫出來。
二十幾歲本來就是一堆岔路口，每關上一扇門，總有另一扇正在打開。

en.content:
The future's blurry.
That's fine.

Blurry doesn't mean you're lost—
it means nothing's been decided for you yet.

And that blank page
just happened to land in your hands.
en.editorNote: For the grads feeling adrift, this reframes 'uncertain' as 'still wide open' — gentle but grounding, and it makes a thoughtful message to send with a graduation card
variations: Can't see the road ahead doesn't mean there isn't one — it just means it's still waiting for you to draw it.
Your twenties are one long string of crossroads. Every door that closes has another quietly swinging open.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
四年前走進來的那個人，
和今天走出去的，
已經不太一樣了。

沒有人通知你哪一天變的，
但回頭看，
你確實長大了。

而且，長得比你以為的還要好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業時寫給自己，或長輩、老師送給學生，肯定那段看不見卻真實發生的成長，溫暖不說教
variations: 你不是突然長大的，是在每一次撐過去的夜裡，一點一點變成現在的自己。
畢業最珍貴的不是那張紙，是這幾年悄悄變得更勇敢的你。

en.content:
The person who walked in four years ago
and the one walking out today
aren't quite the same anymore.

Nobody told you which day it happened,
but looking back,
you really did grow up.

And into someone better than you expected.
en.editorNote: Great to write to yourself at graduation, or for a teacher or parent to give a student — it honors the quiet, invisible growth without preaching
variations: You didn't grow up all at once. You became this version of yourself a little at a time, on every night you got through.
The most valuable thing about graduating isn't the diploma — it's the braver person you quietly became along the way.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
學業：完成。
睡眠：即將開始。

接下來的人生規劃，
我打算先睡飽再說。

醒來如果有靈感，
再考慮要不要當大人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給熬夜熬到畢業的所有人，把「人生規劃」自動降級成「先睡飽」，厭世又療癒，貼限動超有共鳴
variations: 畢業後第一個目標：把這四年欠的睡眠，一次補回來。
別人問我畢業後的計畫，我的計畫是先把鬧鐘關掉，剩下的醒來再說。

en.content:
Education: complete.
Sleep: now loading.

As for my five-year plan,
I'm going to start by catching up on rest.

If inspiration strikes when I wake up,
I'll consider becoming an adult.
en.editorNote: For everyone who pulled all-nighters right up to the finish line — it quietly downgrades 'life plan' to 'nap first,' equal parts burnt out and comforting, made for a story post
variations: First post-grad goal: repay four years of sleep debt in a single sitting.
People keep asking about my plans after graduation. The plan is to turn off the alarm and figure out the rest when I wake up.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
恭喜你完成了
長達十六年、
以螢光筆和焦慮為主要投資項目的計畫。

回收期：待定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給把所有課本畫滿重點、考前焦慮到失眠的人，用「投資報酬」的口吻調侃求學人生，畢業卡片或限動都適用
variations: 十六年的學費，換來一張紙和一身把待辦清單做到最後一刻的本事。
感謝這些年陪我度過的螢光筆、咖啡因和半夜的崩潰，沒有你們我畢不了業。

en.content:
Congratulations on completing
a sixteen-year project
funded primarily by highlighters and anxiety.

Return on investment:
still pending.
en.editorNote: For anyone who color-coded every textbook and lost sleep before every exam — it roasts the whole student experience in mock-financial language, perfect for a card or a story post
variations: Sixteen years of tuition, traded for one piece of paper and a lifelong talent for finishing to-do lists at the last possible second.
Shout-out to the highlighters, the caffeine, and the 2 a.m. breakdowns that got me across the finish line.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業不是一道門的關上，
是一扇門的打開。

它不是你的終點站，
只是你買票時，
剛好停的那一站。

接下來要去哪，
方向盤一直都在你手上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給即將離開校園、對未來既期待又茫然的朋友，把畢業重新定義成「中途站」而非終點，溫暖又給力
variations: 畢業證書證明你有能力把一件事走到完，現在換你去學會，怎麼把一件事好好開始。
別怕前面的路看不清楚，那不是迷路，那是還沒有人替你決定要往哪走。

en.content:
Graduation isn't a door closing.
It's a door opening.

It's not your final stop—
it's just the station
where your ticket happened to pause.

Where you go next,
the wheel has always been in your hands.
en.editorNote: A warm, encouraging send-off for a friend leaving school feeling both excited and lost — it reframes graduation as a waypoint, not a finish line, great for a card or a heartfelt message
variations: A diploma proves you can finish something. Now comes the part where you learn how to begin one.
Don't be scared that the road ahead looks unclear — that's not being lost, it just means nobody's decided your direction for you yet.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
最捨不得的，
從來不是那間教室。

是下課後一起賴著不走的那群人，
是吵著要去哪吃飯、
結果在原地站了二十分鐘的我們。

地方會留下，
但這群人會散到各自的城市去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業時傳給死黨，或寫在合照下面，戳的是「不是離開學校，是離開一起耍廢的人」這個痛點，超容易讓人鼻酸
variations: 畢業才發現，最難說再見的不是校園，是那些陪我把無聊日子過得不無聊的人。
以後想找人臨時揪吃宵夜，可能要先看一下時差了。

en.content:
What I'll miss most
was never the classroom.

It's the crew who lingered after the bell,
the us who argued about where to eat
and then just stood there
for twenty minutes deciding nothing.

The place stays.
But this crew scatters
to cities of their own.
en.editorNote: Perfect to send a best friend at graduation or caption under a group photo — it hits the real ache, that you're not leaving a building, you're leaving the people you wasted time with, guaranteed to make someone tear up
variations: Turns out the hardest goodbye at graduation isn't the campus — it's the people who made the boring days not boring.
Next time I want a spontaneous midnight snack run, I might have to check the time zones first.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
最重要的課，
往往是課表結束之後才開始上的。

學校教你怎麼答對題目，
接下來的人生，
要教你怎麼問對問題。

別急著有答案，
先學會待在沒有答案的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮分享、寫給學弟妹或自己，點出「真正的學習在畢業後」這個觀點，有深度但不說教，貼文加分
variations: 畢業證書只是告訴世界你會考試，剩下的價值，要靠你自己一題一題地活出來。
學校給你標準答案，社會給你選擇題，而長大就是學會：有些題目根本沒有標準答案。

en.content:
The most important lessons
usually begin after the syllabus ends.

School taught you how to get the answer right.
What comes next
will teach you how to ask the right question.

Don't rush to have answers—
first learn to sit in the places
that don't have any.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation speech, a note to younger students, or yourself — it lands the idea that real learning starts after you graduate, thoughtful without being preachy, a caption with substance
variations: A diploma just tells the world you can pass a test. The rest of its worth, you earn one lived question at a time.
School hands you the right answers. Life hands you the choices. Growing up is learning that some questions don't have a right answer at all.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業：成功完成四年假裝自己知道在幹嘛。

接下來換個地方，
繼續假裝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業合照貼文或傳給一起混過來的同學，自嘲又有共鳴，比正經感言更討喜
variations: 認真說，我畢業了。原來我比看起來聰明一點點。
四年大學最大的收穫，就是學會在不知道答案的時候表情管理。

en.content:
Graduation: successfully completed four years of pretending I knew what I was doing.

Next step—
new place, same act.
en.editorNote: Great for a grad photo caption or to send the friends you barely survived it with — self-deprecating and relatable, more fun than a serious speech
variations: Long story short, I made it. Turns out I'm smarter than I look.
My biggest takeaway from four years of college: keeping a straight face when I have no idea what's going on.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有沒有想過全世界最貴的一張紙長怎樣？

喏，在這。

值不值得，要再用三十年來算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 舉著畢業證書拍照時的最佳配文，幽默中帶一點現實，學貸族特別有感
variations: 這張紙花了我四年青春跟一筆學貸，請大家看清楚一點。
畢業證書：薄薄一張，重量驚人。

en.content:
Ever wondered what the most expensive piece of paper in the world looks like?

Here you go.

Whether it was worth it—ask me again in thirty years.
en.editorNote: The perfect caption for that holding-up-the-diploma shot — funny with a wink of reality, hits home for anyone with student loans
variations: This paper cost me four years and a loan, so everybody take a good long look.
A diploma: paper-thin, surprisingly heavy.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
沒有人能回頭重新開始，

但任何人都可以從今天起，
給故事一個新的結局。

畢業不是把過去蓋章封存，
是拿起筆，繼續寫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮分享或寫給正在迷惘的自己，把「重新開始」講得溫柔又有力量，貼文有質感
variations: 你改不了開頭，但結局一直握在你手裡。
畢業這一天，不是句點，是換頁。

en.content:
Nobody gets to go back and start over.

But anyone can start today
and write the story a new ending.

Graduation doesn't stamp the past shut—
it hands you the pen.
en.editorNote: Ideal for a graduation speech or a note to yourself when you feel lost — frames 'starting over' as gentle but powerful, a caption with real weight
variations: You can't change the beginning, but the ending has always been yours to write.
Graduation day isn't a full stop. It's a page turn.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
未來不是你拿來等的，
是你拿來做的。

預測未來最準的方法，
從來不是算命，
是動手把它蓋出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給即將踏入社會的畢業生，把「未來操之在己」講得直接有勁，當祝福或自我打氣都好用
variations: 與其問未來會怎樣，不如問自己今天想蓋出什麼。
夢想不會自己長大，它只在被你動手實現的時候才長大。

en.content:
The future isn't something you wait for.

It's something you build.

The most reliable way to predict it
was never a crystal ball—
it's picking up the tools and making it.
en.editorNote: Great to give a graduate stepping into the real world — a direct, energizing take on owning your future, works as a wish or a pep talk to yourself
variations: Instead of asking what the future holds, ask what you want to build into it today.
Dreams don't grow on their own. They only grow the moment you start making them real.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄／IG梗圖，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
恭喜畢業。

從今天起，
你正式有資格用更高的學歷，
做你以前就在做的那些事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在畢業照下面當IG文案，或傳給剛拿到證書、卻發現工作內容沒什麼變的朋友，自嘲又療癒
variations: 畢業證書的意思是：你現在可以用更專業的姿態，繼續迷惘。
學歷升級，迷惘不變，這就是畢業。

en.content:
Congrats, you graduated.

Starting today,
you're officially overqualified
for everything you were already doing.
en.editorNote: Perfect as an Instagram caption under a cap-and-gown photo, or to send a friend who just got the diploma and realized the job stayed exactly the same — self-deprecating and oddly comforting
variations: A diploma means you can now be confused at a much more professional level.
Degree upgraded. Confusion unchanged. That's graduation.

items.sourceNote: 畢業IG文案，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
好消息：再也不用做分組報告了。
壞消息：以後每天上班都是分組報告。

但至少這次有薪水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業季貼IG或傳同學群組，戳中每個被分組報告折磨過的人，再用「有薪水」收尾，苦中帶笑
variations: 畢業後才知道，分組報告不是學生時代的惡夢，是職場的日常。
再也不用跟組員催進度了——以後改成老闆催你。

en.content:
Good news: no more group projects.
Bad news: every job is just a group project.

Upside—this one comes with a paycheck.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation-season Instagram post or the class group chat — it lands with anyone scarred by group work, then softens the blow with the paycheck punchline
variations: Turns out group projects weren't a school thing. They were a forever thing.
No more chasing teammates for their part — now your boss chases you.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（面對不確定），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
前面的路看不清楚，沒關係。

那不是因為前面沒路，
是因為那條路還沒被你走出來。

往前一步，路就會多一段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給對未來感到迷惘的畢業生，把「看不清楚」翻譯成「還沒走」，給人勇氣而不灌雞湯，適合卡片或畢業祝福
variations: 不確定不是壞事，那代表你的故事還沒寫死，怎麼走都還來得及。
看不到終點不用怕，反正你要做的，本來就只是先跨出下一步。

en.content:
Can't see the road ahead? That's okay.

It's not that there's no path—
it's that the path doesn't exist
until you walk it.

One step forward, and the road grows by one.
en.editorNote: For the graduate feeling lost about what's next — reframes "I can't see it" as "I haven't walked it yet," encouraging without the saccharine; works on a card or as a sendoff message
variations: Uncertainty isn't a problem. It means your story isn't finished — there's still room to write it however you want.
You don't need to see the finish line. You just need to take the next step anyway.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人／告別語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝這幾年。

謝謝那些一起熬夜的、一起翹課的、
一起在走廊大笑被老師瞪的。

我們沒有變成最厲害的人，
但我們陪彼此變成了現在的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮後傳給死黨，或貼在畢業合照下面，把「沒有變很厲害但有彼此」講得真誠不浮誇，特別戳普通但溫暖的友情
variations: 我們可能沒拿到什麼了不起的成就，但我們拿到了彼此，這已經夠了。
謝謝你陪我把這幾年過成了值得回頭看的樣子。

en.content:
Thank you for these years.

For the all-nighters, the skipped classes,
the hallway laughs that got us glared at.

We didn't turn into the most impressive people—
but we kept each other company
while becoming who we are now.
en.editorNote: Send this to your closest friends after the ceremony, or caption a group grad photo with it — it names the ordinary-but-warm kind of friendship honestly, no grand achievements required
variations: We may not have collected big accomplishments, but we collected each other — and that was plenty.
Thanks for helping me turn these years into something worth looking back on.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（面對不確定），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你正要走進人生最不確定、
卻也最讓人興奮的一段日子。

會迷路、會不安、會懷疑自己選的路對不對——
這些不是出了什麼錯，
這就是二十幾歲該有的樣子。

一扇門關上，總有另一扇正在打開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給剛畢業、對未來既期待又害怕的人，把「迷惘是正常的」講清楚，比一味叫人加油更能安慰到心裡
variations: 二十幾歲本來就站滿了岔路口，會猶豫該往哪走，是因為你認真在選。
不確定不代表你走錯了，只代表故事還沒寫完。

en.content:
You're stepping into the most uncertain—
and most thrilling—chapter of your life.

You'll get lost, feel anxious,
second-guess the road you picked.
None of that means something went wrong.
It just means you're in your twenties.

When one door closes, another is already opening.
en.editorNote: For the freshly graduated friend who's both excited and scared — naming 'feeling lost is normal' comforts more than a generic 'you got this'
variations: Your twenties are full of crossroads. Hesitating over which way to go just means you're choosing on purpose.
Uncertainty doesn't mean you took a wrong turn — it means the story isn't finished yet.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（創造未來），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
預測未來最準的方法，
就是親手把它做出來。

你手上這張證書不是終點，
是一根點著的火柴——
要把它丟去哪裡，你自己決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞、卡片祝福或送給準備創業、闖蕩的朋友，把「未來握在自己手上」講得有畫面又不老套
variations: 未來不是用等的，是用做的。
別問路會通到哪，先走，路是走出來的。

en.content:
The surest way to predict the future
is to build it yourself.

That diploma in your hand isn't a finish line—
it's a lit match.
Where you throw it is entirely up to you.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation speech, a card, or a friend about to start something of their own — makes 'the future is yours to make' vivid instead of clichéd
variations: The future isn't something you wait for — it's something you make.
Don't ask where the road leads. Start walking; the road appears as you go.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄（高材生變累），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
從小大家都說：
你以後想做什麼都可以。

結果我做了，
我選擇當一個很累的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在畢業合照或限動，自嘲又精準，特別戳中那些「夢想很大、電量很低」的剛畢業族群
variations: 他們說天空是極限，沒人提醒我機票這麼貴。
畢業證書到手，附贈一份終身有效的疲勞。

en.content:
Growing up, everyone told me
I could be anything I wanted.

So I did it.
I chose to be tired.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a grad photo caption or story — self-deprecating and dead-on, especially for the freshly graduated crowd running on big dreams and low battery
variations: They said the sky's the limit. Nobody mentioned how expensive the flight is.
Diploma acquired. Comes bundled with lifetime exhaustion.

items.sourceNote: 畢業短句IG caption（劇情大逆轉），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
劇情大逆轉：
那個老是被當的人，
竟然畢業了。

編劇你還好嗎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 超適合當畢業限動或合照短文，短到一看就笑，自嘲學渣翻身的反差感最對味
variations: 長話短說——我畢業了。
本以為會延畢，結果我比劇本還會演。

en.content:
Plot twist:
the one who kept failing
actually graduated.

You okay over there, writers?
en.editorNote: Great as a short grad-story or photo caption — quick enough to land an instant laugh, with that underdog-glow-up energy
variations: Long story short: I made it.
Bet on a fifth year. Turns out I outacted the script.

items.sourceNote: 畢業友情語錄（換城市不換情誼），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
之後我們會搬去不同城市，
做著不同的工作，
過著各自的人生。

但有些人不需要天天見，
一開口還是當年那個樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給一起畢業、即將各奔東西的死黨，比「友誼長存」更有畫面，貼合卡片或臨別訊息都好用
variations: 畢業不是說再見，是換個方式繼續當朋友。
距離會變遠，但那種一秒回到當年的默契不會。

en.content:
Soon we'll live in different cities,
work different jobs,
live separate lives.

But some people you don't need to see every day—
one hello, and it's like no time passed at all.
en.editorNote: For the best friends graduating and scattering in different directions — more vivid than 'friends forever,' and fits a card or a goodbye message
variations: Graduation isn't goodbye — it's just a new way to stay friends.
The distance grows. That instant snap-back to old times doesn't.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（結束即開始），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你以為這是結束，
其實只是有一扇門關上了。

關門的聲音之所以這麼響，
是為了讓你聽見
旁邊那扇正在打開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞、卡片或送給對未來不安的朋友，把「結束就是開始」講得有畫面，安慰人也不空泛
variations: 畢業不是終點，是把舊地圖收起來，自己畫一張新的。
一段路走到底了，剛好可以抬頭看看別的方向。

en.content:
You think this is the end,
but really just one door has closed.

It slams so loudly
so that you'll finally hear
the one swinging open beside it.
en.editorNote: Works for a graduation speech, a card, or a friend nervous about what's next — makes 'every ending is a beginning' feel concrete instead of empty
variations: Graduation isn't the finish line — it's putting away the old map and drawing your own.
One road has run out. Perfect time to look up and pick another.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄（撐過畢業），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不是說我「念完」了大學，
我是說我「撐過」了大學。

這兩個聽起來很像，
但只有一個人會懂差別，
那個人就是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在IG畢業照下面剛剛好，自嘲又有梗，特別適合那些覺得自己是用毅力（和咖啡）硬撐到畢業的人
variations: 畢業證書上寫的是「完成學業」，我心裡寫的是「我居然沒被當掉」。
別人問我大學怎麼過的，我只能說：很驚險，但我還在。

en.content:
I'm not saying I finished college.

I'm saying I survived college.

Those two sound the same,
but only one person knows the difference—
and that person is me.
en.editorNote: Drops perfectly under a graduation photo on Instagram — self-deprecating and funny, especially for anyone who got to the finish line on sheer willpower (and caffeine)
variations: The diploma says 'completed degree.' My heart says 'somehow didn't fail.'
People ask how college went. All I can say is: close calls, but I'm still here.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句（沒有小組報告了），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業最大的好處：

從今以後，
再也沒有人可以把我拉進一個
「明天要交但只有我在做」的小組報告。

這份自由，比文憑還珍貴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼IG或丟群組，所有經歷過爛隊友的人都會會心一笑，搞笑之餘也是一種釋懷
variations: 謝謝大學讓我學會一件事：有些隊友的名字，只會出現在報告封面上。
畢業快樂，敬那些報告我自己寫完、但成績大家一起拿的日子。

en.content:
The best part of graduating:

From now on,
no one can ever drag me into a group project
that's "due tomorrow" and somehow only I'm working on.

That freedom beats the diploma.
en.editorNote: Great for Instagram or the group chat — anyone who's suffered through bad teammates will smile, and there's real relief under the joke
variations: Thanks, college, for teaching me this: some teammates only ever show up on the cover page.
Happy graduation — here's to all the projects I wrote alone but we all got graded for.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（與不確定共處，改編自 Lin-Manuel Miranda 概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
親愛的、有點害怕的畢業生：

你正要走進人生最不確定的一段路。

但「不確定」的另一個名字，
叫做「什麼都還有可能」。

別急著把它看成壞事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業致詞或送給對未來焦慮的朋友，把「迷惘」翻譯成「可能性」，安慰人而不說教
variations: 你以為的迷路，有時候只是還沒走到看得見風景的地方。
現在什麼都不確定，剛好代表你還沒被任何一條路綁死。

en.content:
Dear slightly terrified graduate:

You're about to walk into the most uncertain stretch of your life.

But "uncertain" has another name—
it's called "everything is still possible."

Don't rush to call it a bad thing.
en.editorNote: Fits a graduation speech or a friend anxious about the future — reframes 'lost' as 'open,' comforting without lecturing
variations: What feels like being lost is sometimes just not reaching the viewpoint yet.
Nothing's certain right now — which means no single road has claimed you yet.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄（最後一次的那些時刻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們從來不知道，
哪一次是最後一次。

最後一次一起翹課去買飲料，
最後一次在走廊喊你的名字，
最後一次說「明天見」。

如果早知道，
那天我一定會多待一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在畢業照或傳給一起走過的同學，戳中「來不及好好告別」的遺憾，比直白的祝福更有後勁
variations: 最捨不得的不是畢業典禮，是那些當下沒發現、後來才知道是最後一次的小事。
原來真正的再見，很多都是在不知不覺中說完的。

en.content:
We never knew
which time would be the last.

The last time we skipped class for drinks,
the last time I called your name down the hall,
the last "see you tomorrow."

If I'd known,
I would've stayed a little longer that day.
en.editorNote: For a graduation photo or a message to the classmates who walked it with you — lands on the ache of goodbyes you didn't get to say, more lasting than a plain blessing
variations: The hardest part isn't the ceremony. It's the small last times you only recognized later.
Turns out most real goodbyes get said before we ever notice them happening.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（自己創造未來，改編自 Peter Drucker 概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
沒有人能告訴你未來會怎樣，

因為未來不是用來「預測」的，
是用來「動手做」的。

你今天踏出的那一步，
就是答案的第一個字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業卡片、座右銘或送給站在人生路口的人，把抽象的「掌握未來」變成具體的「現在就動手」
variations: 與其猜未來長什麼樣，不如從今天開始把它畫出來。
未來不會憑空出現，它是你一步一步走出來的。

en.content:
No one can tell you how the future turns out,

because the future isn't something you predict—
it's something you build.

The step you take today
is the first word of the answer.
en.editorNote: Works as a graduation card, a motto, or a gift for someone standing at a crossroads — turns the abstract 'own your future' into the concrete 'start now'
variations: Instead of guessing what the future looks like, start drawing it today.
The future doesn't appear on its own — you walk it into being, one step at a time.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄（流蘇翻面梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
四年，
換來一張紙、一頂帽子，
還有把流蘇從右邊撥到左邊的那一秒。

值了。

大概吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配畢業典禮撥穗的照片，自嘲又不失溫度，最後那句「大概吧」是整段的笑點，留白給人會心一笑
variations: 全部的努力，濃縮成撥穗那零點五秒的儀式感。
畢業典禮最有成就感的動作，就是把流蘇從這邊撥到那邊。

en.content:
Four years,
for a piece of paper, a funny hat,
and the one second it takes to flip the tassel to the other side.

Worth it.

Probably.
en.editorNote: Pair it with a tassel-flip photo — self-deprecating but warm, and that final 'probably' is the punchline that earns the knowing smile
variations: Every ounce of effort, distilled into the half-second ceremony of moving a tassel.
The most rewarding move of the whole ceremony is flipping the tassel from one side to the other.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（學會與不確定共處，改編自 Lin-Manuel Miranda 概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你正要走進人生最不確定、
也最讓人興奮的一段日子。

別急著把每一步都想清楚，
有些路是走著走著，才看得見的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給站在人生路口、對未來感到焦慮的人，把「不確定」重新定義成「值得期待」，畢業卡片或座右銘都適用
variations: 看不清前面的路，不代表你走錯了，只代表你還在往前。
不用一次想通整條路，先走出下一步就好。

en.content:
You're stepping into the most uncertain—
and most thrilling—stretch of your life.

Don't rush to map out every step.
Some roads only show themselves
once you start walking them.
en.editorNote: For anyone at a crossroads feeling anxious about what's next — reframes 'uncertain' as 'worth looking forward to', great for a graduation card or a personal motto
variations: Not seeing the road ahead doesn't mean you took a wrong turn — it just means you're still moving.
You don't have to figure out the whole path at once. Just take the next step.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄（多年後最後悔的是沒做的事，改編自 Mark Twain 概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
二十年後，
讓你後悔的，
不會是你做過的那些事，

而是那些你想做、
卻一直不敢做的。

所以，去吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給畢業生的勵志金句，把「怕失敗」翻轉成「更怕沒試過」，適合畢業卡片或臨別贈言，最後一句乾脆有力
variations: 比起做錯，更可惜的是連試都沒試。
別讓「當初要是有去做就好了」變成你以後最熟的一句話。

en.content:
Twenty years from now,
what you'll regret
won't be the things you did—

it'll be the ones
you wanted to do
but never dared.

So go.
en.editorNote: An inspiring send-off for graduates — flips 'afraid to fail' into 'more afraid of never trying', great for a card or a parting line, and that closing 'So go' lands clean
variations: Getting it wrong stings less than never trying at all.
Don't let 'I wish I'd just gone for it' become the line you say most often.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄（同學從此各分東西），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，
我們不會再每天見面了。

但有些人就是這樣——
就算很久沒聯絡，
再見面的第一句話，
還是「欸你也太誇張」。

那種感情，不會畢業。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給一起走過這幾年的好朋友，安慰「分開」的不捨，也肯定「真感情不怕距離」，貼畢業合照特別有感
variations: 有些朋友不用天天見，但永遠都在你那一邊。
我們會走散，但不會走丟——需要的時候，總找得到彼此。

en.content:
After graduation,
we won't see each other every day.

But some people are just like that—
no matter how long it's been,
the first thing you say when you meet again
is still "okay, you're ridiculous."

That kind of bond doesn't graduate.
en.editorNote: Send to the friends who walked these years with you — eases the ache of parting while affirming that real friendship outlasts distance, great with a group graduation photo
variations: Some friends you don't see daily, but they're always on your side.
We'll scatter, but we won't lose each other — when it counts, we always find our way back.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作（流蘇/成績梗）

zh-tw.content:
四年來我最擅長的事，
就是把死線拖到最後一秒，
然後在最後一秒神奇地活下來。

所以你問我畢業後怕不怕？
拜託，我可是專業的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配畢業照發IG，用自嘲的方式化解「畢業後好焦慮」的情緒，比一本正經的雞湯更討喜也更有共鳴
variations: 報告可以拖、考試可以硬撐，連畢業都被我拖到了——但至少，我到了。
別人問我怎麼撐過來的，我也不知道，反正我人就在這裡了。

en.content:
My greatest skill these four years:
procrastinating every deadline to the very last second,
then somehow surviving it anyway.

So you ask if I'm scared about life after graduation?
Please. I'm a professional.
en.editorNote: Pair with a cap-and-gown photo on Instagram — self-deprecating humor defuses the post-grad anxiety better than a straight-faced pep talk, and it lands more relatable
variations: Deadlines got pushed, exams got survived, even graduation ran late — but hey, I made it.
People ask how I pulled it off. No clue. But I'm standing here, so.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作（門/新章節）

zh-tw.content:
畢業從來不是終點站，
它只是一扇門。

門後面沒有地圖，
沒有標準答案，
但那正是它最好玩的地方——

因為接下來要走去哪，
第一次，完全由你決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給即將踏入職場或繼續升學的朋友，把「畢業後的未知」重新框成「自由」，比制式祝福更有力量
variations: 以前有課表，現在沒有了——恭喜你，時間終於是你自己的了。
沒有人幫你排下一堂課，因為接下來的路，是你自己開的。

en.content:
Graduation was never the finish line.
It's just a door.

There's no map on the other side,
no right answer to circle,
and that's exactly what makes it good—

because where you go next
is, for the first time, entirely up to you.
en.editorNote: A meaningful message for friends heading into their first job or grad school — reframes post-grad uncertainty as freedom, which hits harder than a generic congratulations
variations: You used to have a class schedule. Now you don't — congrats, your time is finally your own.
Nobody's assigning your next class, because from here on out, you're the one paving the road.

items.sourceNote: 畢業短語錄，重新創作（簡短IG caption）

zh-tw.content:
來了。撐過了。畢業了。

剩下的，慢慢寫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 超短版，適合當IG貼文的一句話caption，或直接配在畢業照下面；留白的「慢慢寫」給人往前走的餘韻
variations: 這一章，寫完了。下一頁，空白的，也是我的。
不轟轟烈烈，就是好好地，把這段走完了。

en.content:
Showed up. Held on. Graduated.

The rest, I'll write as I go.
en.editorNote: An ultra-short one-liner caption for Instagram, or to drop straight under a graduation photo — the open-ended "write as I go" leaves a forward-looking note
variations: This chapter's done. The next page is blank — and it's mine.
No fireworks. Just quietly, fully, seeing it through.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作（創造自己的未來）

zh-tw.content:
他們老是問：「你以後想做什麼？」

好像未來是一個現成的東西，
等在那裡讓你去挑。

但未來不是拿來挑的，
是拿來做的。

所以別急著回答，
去把它做出來就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給還沒想清楚方向、被「你以後要幹嘛」問到很煩的畢業生，把壓力轉成一種「慢慢創造」的底氣
variations: 不用現在就有答案，答案是一邊走一邊長出來的。
與其猜未來長什麼樣，不如動手把它做成你想要的樣子。

en.content:
They keep asking, "So what do you want to do?"

As if the future were something ready-made,
sitting there waiting to be picked.

But the future isn't picked.
It's built.

So don't rush to answer.
Just go make it.
en.editorNote: For the graduate who hasn't figured out their direction and is tired of being asked "what's your plan" — turns the pressure into quiet confidence about building things over time
variations: You don't need the answer now — it grows in as you go.
Instead of guessing what the future looks like, go build it into the shape you want.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作（履歷篇）

zh-tw.content:
花了四年拿到一張證書，

證明我很會考試、
很會趕報告、
很會在期限前一天生出一整個學期的東西。

這些技能，職場好像也用得上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛畢業要開始投履歷的人自嘲，把「臨時抱佛腳」講成一種可轉移技能，貼IG或傳給同樣在找工作的朋友都很有共鳴
variations: 畢業證書的意思是：我通過了「在最後一刻完成事情」的四年密集訓練。
大學教會我最實用的一件事，就是怎麼在來不及的時候還是把它交出去。

en.content:
Four years for one piece of paper,

proving I'm great at taking exams,
great at rushing reports,
great at producing a whole semester's work the night before it's due.

Turns out those skills transfer to the workplace just fine.
en.editorNote: For the fresh grad about to start job-hunting — reframes chronic procrastination as a transferable skill; relatable as an Instagram caption or a message to a friend also on the hunt
variations: My diploma means I passed a four-year intensive course in getting things done at the last possible second.
The most practical thing college taught me was how to still turn it in when there was no time left.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作（散場篇）

zh-tw.content:
最後一次一起走出校門，

我們沒有特別說什麼，
就像以為明天還會再見一樣。

後來才懂，
有些再見，是說給以後的自己聽的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業季配上和朋友的合照，寫給那些「當下不覺得是最後一次」的告別，情緒細膩不煽情
variations: 散場的時候大家都笑著，只有回家的路上，才發現有點空。
我們以為還有很多次，結果那次就是最後一次了。

en.content:
The last time we walked out those gates together,

we didn't say anything special —
as if we'd just see each other tomorrow.

Only later did I understand:
some goodbyes are meant for the version of you that comes later.
en.editorNote: Pair with a photo of you and your friends; written for the goodbyes that didn't feel like goodbyes at the time — tender without being over the top
variations: We were all smiling when it ended. It was only on the walk home that it felt a little empty.
We thought there'd be plenty more times. That was the last one.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作（比較篇）

zh-tw.content:
畢業以後你會很想拿自己跟別人比。

誰先進大公司，
誰先買車，
誰的動態看起來過得比較好。

但每個人的時區不一樣，
你沒有遲到，
只是還沒輪到你的那一段而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給畢業後陷入比較焦慮的人，尤其是看到同學一個個「上岸」而自己還在摸索的時候，給人一種被理解的安定感
variations: 別人跑得快不代表你走錯路，你們本來就不是在同一條跑道上。
花不會因為旁邊那朵先開就覺得自己失敗，你也不用。

en.content:
After graduation you'll really want to measure yourself against everyone else.

Who landed the big job first,
who bought a car first,
whose feed looks like they've got it more figured out.

But everyone runs on their own clock.
You're not behind —
it's just not your turn yet.
en.editorNote: For the grad spiraling into comparison anxiety — especially while watching classmates "make it" one by one — offers the steadying feeling of being understood
variations: Someone moving faster doesn't mean you took a wrong turn — you were never on the same track.
A flower doesn't feel like a failure because the one next to it bloomed first. Neither should you.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑短句，重新創作（IG caption）

zh-tw.content:
學生生涯：正式結束。
睡到自然醒的日子：也正式結束。
zh-tw.editorNote: 超短一句話caption，適合直接配畢業照，戳中所有即將被鬧鐘支配的社會新鮮人
variations: 恭喜畢業！下一站：早八變全天。
從此以後，我的暑假只剩回憶和特休。

en.content:
Student life: officially over.
Sleeping in until I wake up on my own: also officially over.
en.editorNote: An ultra-short one-liner caption to drop under a graduation photo — lands with every fresh grad about to be ruled by an alarm clock
variations: Congrats, graduate! Next stop: 8 a.m. class becomes all day.
From now on, summer break lives only in my memories and my PTO.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作（迷惘也沒關係）

zh-tw.content:
如果你畢業了，
卻還是不知道自己要幹嘛，

沒關係。

沒有人是拿到證書那天
就突然想通一切的。

方向不是一次找到的，
是一步一步走出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給那些看著別人好像都很有計畫、只有自己一片空白的畢業生，把「迷惘」正常化，讀起來像有人拍拍你的肩膀
variations: 還沒想清楚也可以先出發，很多答案是走到一半才遇到的。
不知道要去哪，那就先往有光的地方走一步再說。

en.content:
If you've graduated
but still have no idea what you're doing,

that's okay.

Nobody suddenly figures it all out
the day they get the diploma.

Direction isn't found all at once —
it's walked into, one step at a time.
en.editorNote: For the grad staring at everyone else's apparent master plan while their own page is blank — normalizes the fog and reads like a hand on your shoulder
variations: You can set off before you've got it figured out — a lot of answers you only meet halfway down the road.
Don't know where to go? Take one step toward wherever the light is, and figure out the rest from there.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑畢業語錄，重新創作（過度資格）

zh-tw.content:
恭喜畢業。

現在的你，
終於有資格去做
那些完全不需要學歷的工作了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配畢業照當IG貼文，也很適合傳給那個科系和未來工作八竿子打不著的朋友，一句話戳中所有人的共同命運
variations: 讀了四年，換來一張證明我可以熬夜的紙。
學歷：有了。用途：待確認。

en.content:
Congrats on graduating.

You are now officially overqualified
for every job that doesn't require your degree at all.
en.editorNote: A caption for the graduation photo, or a message for the friend whose major has nothing to do with their new job — one line that nails a fate we all share
variations: Four years of study, traded for a piece of paper proving I can pull all-nighters.
Degree: acquired. Practical use: pending.

items.sourceNote: 感人畢業語錄，重新創作（C.S. Lewis 精神——前方更好）

zh-tw.content:
別捨不得離開。

你會捨不得，
是因為這裡真的很好。

但相信我，
前面還有很多很好的地方，
是你現在還沒去過的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給對校園、對這段日子依依不捨的畢業生，把離別的難過翻成一種溫柔的期待，適合貼在畢業照下方
variations: 會不捨得，是因為值得。而值得的事，前面還有很多。
你留戀的每一天，都會變成你走下去的力氣。

en.content:
Don't be sad to leave.

If it hurts to go,
it's only because this place was that good.

But trust me —
there are just as many good places ahead
that you simply haven't been to yet.
en.editorNote: For the grad who can't let go of campus and these years — turns the ache of leaving into a gentle kind of hope, perfect under a graduation photo
variations: It hurts because it mattered. And there's a lot more that will matter still ahead.
Every day you'll miss becomes fuel for the road in front of you.

items.sourceNote: 勵志畢業語錄，重新創作（下一章自己寫）

zh-tw.content:
這一章，
寫的是別人給你的題目。

下一章，
終於輪到你自己下筆。

寫錯了也沒關係，
這本書從頭到尾，都是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給正要離開學校、開始為自己人生做決定的畢業生，把「畢業」比喻成從答題轉為創作，讀起來很有力量
variations: 考卷交出去了，接下來的每一題，答案由你決定。
以前照著課表走，現在該由你來排課了。

en.content:
This chapter
was written from prompts other people gave you.

The next one
is finally yours to write.

And if you get it wrong, that's fine —
from the first page to the last, this book is yours.
en.editorNote: For the grad leaving school to start making their own calls — reframes graduation as the shift from answering questions to authoring them, and reads with real punch
variations: You've handed in the exam. From here, every answer is yours to decide.
You used to follow the timetable. Now you're the one who sets the schedule.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 畢業不是抵達終點，而是拿到一張通往更大世界的門票。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片開頭，鼓勵同學勇敢往前
variations: 畢業從來不是結束，而是換一張更大世界的門票。
這一天不是終點，是你踏進更寬廣世界的起點。

en.content: Graduation isn't the finish line—it's your ticket to a much bigger world.
en.editorNote: Great as an opening line for a graduation card
variations: This isn't the end; it's your pass into a wider world.
You didn't reach the finish line—you just got a ticket to somewhere bigger.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 我們或許會走向不同的城市，但那些一起熬夜、一起大笑的日子，會一直住在心裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫給要各奔東西的好朋友，帶點感性
variations: 就算以後住在不同的城市，那些一起熬夜的夜晚我不會忘記。
路會分岔，但我們一起笑過的日子，會一直留在心底。

en.content: We might end up in different cities, but those late nights and endless laughs will always live in me.
en.editorNote: For close friends heading their separate ways; a little sentimental
variations: Different cities, maybe—but I'm keeping every one of those late nights and inside jokes.
Our paths split here, but the laughs we shared aren't going anywhere.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 恭喜畢業！接下來歡迎光臨現實世界，這裡沒有暑假，但有薪水，也算是一種安慰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫給熟到可以開玩笑的同學，輕鬆又應景
variations: 畢業快樂！現實世界沒有暑假，但至少發薪水那天很快樂。
歡迎進入沒有寒暑假的人生，好消息是這次遲到不會被記警告。

en.content: Congrats on graduating! Welcome to the real world—no more summer break, but hey, you get a paycheck now.
en.editorNote: For friends you can joke around with; light and on-theme
variations: You did it! The real world has no summer vacation, but payday almost makes up for it.
Welcome to adult life: no more breaks, but at least being late won't land you in detention.

items.sourceNote: funny graduation caption inspiration, rewritten

zh-tw.content:
四年下來，我學會的最大技能，
就是很有自信地假裝自己知道在幹嘛。

這張證書證明我很會裝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當畢業照的IG貼文文案，自嘲又不失真實，比正經的感言更容易被朋友按讚留言
variations: 畢業證書，正式認證我具備「一臉專業地不知道自己在幹嘛」的能力。
唸了四年，最強的一招還是——臨場硬撐，笑著點頭。

en.content:
After four years, my biggest skill
is confidently pretending I know what I'm doing.

This diploma is proof I'm good at it.
en.editorNote: Great as an Instagram caption for grad photos — self-deprecating but honest, the kind of line that pulls in likes and comments faster than a serious speech
variations: My diploma officially certifies my ability to look professional while having no idea what's going on.
Four years of school, and my best move is still winging it with a confident nod.

items.sourceNote: funny grad quote about caffeine, rewritten

zh-tw.content:
感謝這一路上的咖啡因，
在我不相信自己的時候，
它一直撐著我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給一起熬夜趕報告的同學，或當作寫給自己的畢業註解，笑點裡藏著真的辛苦
variations: 這張畢業證書，一半是我的努力，一半是咖啡的功勞。
謝謝那些睡不著的夜，還有陪我撐過來的那杯又一杯。

en.content:
Big thanks to the caffeine
that believed in me
when I didn't believe in myself.
en.editorNote: Send it to the friend you pulled all-nighters with, or use it as your own grad caption — funny on the surface, but it lands because the exhaustion was real
variations: This diploma is half hard work, half coffee.
Shoutout to the sleepless nights and every cup that got me through them.

items.sourceNote: inspirational quote about uncertainty, rewritten

zh-tw.content:
畢業不是終點線，
只是把你的人生賽道，
換成一條還沒畫好的路。

而那份不確定，其實就是各種可能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給對未來還很茫然的同學，溫柔地把「不知道要幹嘛」翻譯成「什麼都有機會」，比空泛的加油更有力量
variations: 前方看不清楚，不代表沒有路，只是那條路還等你自己走出來。
畢業後最迷惘的那段時間，往往也是最自由的那段。

en.content:
Graduation isn't the finish line.
It just swaps your track
for a road that hasn't been drawn yet.

And all that uncertainty? It's just possibility in disguise.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels lost about what's next — it gently reframes "I have no idea what to do" into "anything is still possible," which hits harder than a generic pep talk
variations: Not being able to see the road ahead doesn't mean there isn't one — it means you get to walk it into existence.
The most uncertain season after graduation is often the freest one you'll ever have.

items.sourceNote: inspirational quote about courage and degrees, rewritten

zh-tw.content:
文憑幫你打開了門，
但真正走過去的，
是你的勇氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片或送給即將求職、出國的朋友，把重點從學歷拉回到「敢不敢邁出那一步」，簡短又有力
variations: 學歷只是門把，推開門、走進去的還是你自己。
證書證明你到得了這裡，勇氣決定你能走多遠。

en.content:
Your degree opens the door.
But it's your courage
that actually walks you through it.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a graduation card or a friend about to job-hunt or move abroad — it shifts the spotlight from credentials to the guts it takes to step forward; short and punchy
variations: A degree is just the doorknob. You're the one who has to open it and walk in.
The diploma proves you got here. Courage decides how far you go next.

items.sourceNote: funny short graduation caption about pretending to know what you're doing, rewritten

zh-tw.content:
順利完成四年
假裝自己知道在幹嘛的訓練。

特此頒發證書一張。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當畢業照的IG caption，短短一句就把「其實一路都在裝懂」的心聲說出來，比正經的畢業感言更好笑也更真實
variations: 大學四年學到最扎實的技能，就是面不改色地假裝自己聽懂了。
畢業證書：一張證明你成功撐完四年的收據。

en.content:
Successfully completed four years
of pretending to know what I was doing.

Certificate attached.
en.editorNote: A ready-made Instagram caption for your grad photo — one line that admits everyone was just winging it, funnier and more honest than a formal graduation speech
variations: The most solid skill I picked up in four years: nodding like I understood.
A diploma is just a receipt proving you survived four years.

items.sourceNote: funny graduation caption 'now accepting job offers', rewritten

zh-tw.content:
畢業快樂。

現在開始接受工作邀約，
以及任何願意付我薪水的善意。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛畢業、正在找工作的人發IG，把求職焦慮講得又好笑又直白，比哀怨的文案更容易引來朋友留言關心（或內推）
variations: 學生時期正式結束，帳單時期正式開始。
畢業證書到手，下一個目標：讓它變現。

en.content:
Happy graduation to me.

Now accepting job offers,
and any kindness willing to pay a salary.
en.editorNote: Great for a fresh grad in the middle of the job hunt — it turns job-search anxiety into a punchline, which pulls in encouraging comments (or referrals) better than a gloomy post
variations: The student era has officially ended. The bills era has officially begun.
Diploma acquired. Next objective: turning it into a paycheck.

items.sourceNote: graduation quote 'you've been becoming someone this whole time', rewritten

zh-tw.content:
畢業不是結束。

是你終於發現，
這幾年你一直在慢慢變成一個人，
而今天，你第一次把這個人
介紹給全世界。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片或畢業典禮致詞，把畢業從「結束」翻轉成「登場」，送給那些覺得自己好像沒什麼長進的畢業生特別打動人
variations: 你不是走到終點，你是走到了可以介紹自己的那一刻。
這幾年最大的成果不是那張證書，是那個現在站在這裡的你。

en.content:
Graduation isn't an ending.

It's the moment you realize
you've been becoming someone this whole time —
and today, for the first time,
you get to introduce that person to the world.
en.editorNote: Ideal for a graduation card or a commencement toast — it reframes graduation from 'the end' to 'the debut,' and lands hardest for grads who feel like they haven't changed much
variations: You didn't reach the finish line. You reached the moment you finally get to introduce yourself.
The biggest thing you built these years isn't the diploma — it's the person standing here now.

items.sourceNote: class of 2026 quote about being prepared for a world that doesn't exist yet, rewritten

zh-tw.content:
有人擔心你們是為了一個
還不存在的世界做準備。

但其實，
那正是最好的準備——
因為那個世界，
要靠你們去把它蓋出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給對就業市場、AI時代感到不安的畢業生，把「學的東西可能過時」的焦慮，轉成「這個未來由你定義」的底氣
variations: 沒有人能給你未來的地圖，因為那條路還沒被走出來——由你來畫。
世界變太快不是壞消息，那代表還有一大塊空白等你去填。

en.content:
People worry you were trained
for a world that doesn't exist yet.

But that's exactly
the best preparation there is —
because that world
is the one you get to build.
en.editorNote: For grads uneasy about the job market or the AI era — it flips the fear that 'what I learned might be obsolete' into the confidence that 'this future is mine to define'
variations: No one can hand you a map of the future, because that road hasn't been walked yet — you draw it.
A world changing this fast isn't bad news. It means there's a huge blank space left for you to fill.

items.sourceNote: graduation wordplay caption 'the tassel was worth the hassle', rewritten

zh-tw.content:
熬夜、報告、期末、崩潰。

全部加起來，
就為了今天把帽子上的穗子
從右邊撥到左邊。

值了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搭配撥穗那張畢業照當IG caption，用一個超小的動作收束四年的辛苦，反差感很強，簡短又有記憶點
variations: 四年的努力，濃縮成三秒鐘的撥穗動作。划算。
為了這頂帽子付出的，遠不只學費。

en.content:
All-nighters, deadlines, finals, breakdowns.

Add it all up,
and it was for one thing:
moving this tassel
from the right side to the left.

Worth it.
en.editorNote: Pairs perfectly with the tassel-turning grad photo — it compresses four hard years into one tiny gesture, and that contrast makes it stick
variations: Four years of effort, distilled into a three-second tassel flip. Solid deal.
What this cap actually cost me was a lot more than tuition.

items.sourceNote: funny caption 'officially too educated to be this broke', rewritten

zh-tw.content:
終於畢業了。

現在的我，
學歷高到不像會這麼窮的人，
窮到不像有這麼高學歷的人。

完美平衡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在IG畢業照或傳給同樣剛出社會的朋友，用自嘲把「畢業＝有錢」的錯覺戳破，越窮的人越有共鳴
variations: 領到畢業證書那天我才發現，這是我人生中最貴、也最不能拿去換錢的一張紙。
恭喜畢業！正式升級成一個受過高等教育的窮人。

en.content:
Finally graduated.

Now I'm officially
too educated to be this broke,
and too broke to look this educated.

Perfectly balanced.
en.editorNote: Great for a grad-photo caption or a text to a friend who just entered the workforce — it pops the 'degree equals money' illusion, and the broker you are the harder it lands
variations: The day I got my diploma I realized it's the most expensive piece of paper I own that I can never cash in.
Congrats on graduating! You've officially leveled up to a highly educated broke person.

items.sourceNote: C.S. Lewis 'far better things ahead than any we leave behind', rewritten as original message

zh-tw.content:
會捨不得，是因為這段日子真的很好。

但別忘了——
你要去的地方，
藏著比你留下的更好的東西。

往前走，它們在等你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給對畢業感到不捨、害怕未來的朋友，一邊承認回憶的珍貴，一邊給出往前走的勇氣，比單純叫人「加油」更有力量
variations: 你留下的每一段美好，都不是終點，而是為了讓你認得出——前方那些更好的東西。
捨不得是真的，值得期待也是真的，這兩件事可以同時成立。

en.content:
It's hard to leave, because these years were genuinely good.

But don't forget —
the place you're headed
is holding something even better than what you're leaving.

Keep walking. It's waiting for you.
en.editorNote: For a friend who feels the ache of graduating and fears what's next — it honors the memories while handing them the courage to move on, which hits harder than a plain 'you got this'
variations: Every good thing you're leaving behind isn't the finish line — it's what teaches you to recognize the better things up ahead.
Being sad to go and being excited for what's next can both be true at the same time.

items.sourceNote: funny caption 'graduated, still buffering', rewritten

zh-tw.content:
畢業了。

腦袋：已滿。
計畫：載入中……
人生：緩衝中，請稍候。

（進度條卡在99%）
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當IG畢業caption，或傳給還沒想好下一步的同學，用「網路緩衝」的比喻把畢業後的空白期講得又好笑又真實
variations: 畢業證書到手，人生規劃還在轉圈圈的那個載入中圖示。
腦容量：使用100%。未來方向：檔案未找到。

en.content:
Graduated.

Brain: full.
Plans: loading…
Life: buffering, please wait.

(progress bar stuck at 99%)
en.editorNote: Perfect as a grad Instagram caption or a text to a classmate who hasn't figured out their next move — the buffering metaphor makes the post-grad blank period both funny and painfully accurate
variations: Diploma: acquired. Life plan: still that little spinning loading wheel.
Brain storage: 100% used. Future direction: file not found.

items.sourceNote: Maria Robinson 'anyone can start today and make a new ending', rewritten as original quote

zh-tw.content:
沒有人能回到過去，重新來一場開頭。

但任何人都可以從今天開始，
給故事寫一個不一樣的結局。

畢業，就是那個「今天」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給覺得大學過得不夠好、有點遺憾的畢業生，把焦點從「無法重來的過去」轉向「還能改寫的未來」，溫柔又有力量
variations: 過去的章節不能重寫，但接下來每一頁，筆都在你手上。
你改不了故事怎麼開始，但你完全有權決定它怎麼結束。

en.content:
Nobody gets to go back and start over from the beginning.

But anyone can start today
and write the story a different ending.

Graduation is that 'today.'
en.editorNote: For a graduate who feels their college years fell short and carries some regret — it shifts the focus from the past they can't redo to the future they can still rewrite, gentle but strong
variations: You can't rewrite the chapters already written, but every page from here — the pen is in your hand.
You don't get to choose how the story started, but you fully get to decide how it ends.

items.sourceNote: Lao Tzu 'journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step', rewritten as original message

zh-tw.content:
再長的路，
都是從第一步開始的。

今天你踏出去的這一步，
看起來很小，
卻是往後所有故事的開頭。

慢慢走，別怕走得晚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片或傳給對未來感到焦慮、覺得自己起步太慢的朋友，把「遠大目標」拆解成「先跨出這一步」，減輕壓力也給出方向
variations: 你不用一次看清整條路，看清腳下這一步就夠了。
起步晚沒關係，只要你今天願意踏出去，時間就開始站在你這邊。

en.content:
Even the longest road
starts with a single step.

The step you take today
looks small,
but it's the opening line of every story that comes next.

Go slow. Don't fear starting late.
en.editorNote: For a graduation card or a friend anxious about the future who feels they're starting too slow — it breaks a huge goal down to 'just take this one step,' easing the pressure while pointing the way
variations: You don't have to see the whole road at once — seeing the step under your feet is enough.
Starting late is fine. The moment you're willing to step out today, time starts working for you.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這條路我披荊斬棘。

主要是被學貸的荊棘。
以及被期末考的棘。

總之，我畢業了，
而且成功欠了一屁股債。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搭配畢業照發IG限動，用自嘲的方式講學貸現實，比正經祝福更能引起同世代共鳴，留言區保證一片「同款」
variations: 畢業證書到手，附贈一份要還二十年的分期付款。
他們說知識是無價的，然後寄了帳單來提醒我它其實有價，還挺貴。

en.content:
I fought through thorns to get here.

Mostly student-loan thorns.
With a few final-exam thorns.

Anyway, I graduated —
and successfully went into debt doing it.
en.editorNote: Pair with a graduation photo for an Instagram Story; the self-deprecating take on student debt lands harder than a sincere caption and the comments will fill up with 'same'
variations: Diploma acquired. Comes bundled with a payment plan I'll finish in twenty years.
They said knowledge is priceless, then mailed me a bill to clarify it actually has a price, and it's steep.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
未來屬於那些
還願意相信自己夢想的人。

不是相信它一定會成，
是就算沒人看好，
你還是願意為它多撐一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片或送給正在追夢、卻常被潑冷水的朋友，把「相信夢想」重新定義成「願意再撐一天」，比空泛的加油更有力量
variations: 夢想不會因為你相信就實現，但會因為你放棄就消失。
沒人看好的時候，你自己的相信就是唯一的資金。

en.content:
The future belongs to the ones
still willing to believe in their dreams.

Not believing it's guaranteed —
believing in it enough
to hold on one more day when nobody else does.
en.editorNote: For a graduation card or a friend chasing a dream who keeps getting doubted; it reframes 'believe in your dreams' as 'be willing to last one more day,' which carries more weight than a generic pep talk
variations: A dream won't come true just because you believe in it — but it will die the moment you quit.
When no one's betting on you, your own belief is the only funding you've got.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們說我可以成為任何我想成為的人。

所以我選擇成為
一個很累的人。

目標達成，提前畢業於快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合發IG或傳給剛進職場、每天喊累的朋友，用黑色幽默講出畢業後的真實狀態，笑完之後其實很有共鳴
variations: 小時候的夢想是當太空人，長大後的成就是準時下班，兩個都還沒達成。
畢業快樂，接下來換公司決定你快不快樂。

en.content:
They told me I could be anything I wanted.

So I chose to be
extremely tired.

Goal reached. Graduated early from happiness.
en.editorNote: Post on Instagram or send to a friend fresh in their first job who complains about being tired daily; the dark humor nails the real post-graduation mood and hits home after the laugh
variations: As a kid I dreamed of being an astronaut. As an adult my big win is leaving work on time. Still working on both.
Congrats on graduating. From here on, a company gets to decide whether you're happy.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
長話短說，
我做到了。

但這句「我」，
其實一直都是「我們」。

那些在圖書館陪我熬夜的、
在我想放棄時多問一句的，
這張證書有你們的名字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業典禮後傳給一路互相扶持的同學好友，或貼在合照下方，把個人的成就歸功於身邊的人，比獨自炫耀更溫暖也更動人
variations: 所謂畢業，就是把一群人的陪伴，收進一段誰也拿不走的回憶。
我拿到的是一個人的證書，走過的卻是一群人的路。

en.content:
Long story short,
I made it.

But that 'I'
was always really a 'we.'

The ones who stayed up in the library with me,
who asked one more time when I wanted to quit —
your names are on this diploma too.
en.editorNote: For friends who carried each other through, sent after the ceremony or posted under a group photo; crediting the people around you instead of celebrating alone reads warmer and lands deeper
variations: Graduation is just tucking a whole crew's company into a memory no one can take from you.
The diploma has one name on it. The road it took had a whole group walking it.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
預測未來最好的方法，
不是猜，
是動手把它做出來。

畢業之後沒有標準答案，
因為這一次，
出題的人是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片或送給對前路茫然、習慣了考試有標準答案的畢業生，把焦慮轉化成主動權，提醒他們人生從此由自己出題
variations: 以前是考卷決定你的分數，以後是你決定要考哪張考卷。
未來沒有參考解答，但這正代表你怎麼寫都不算錯。

en.content:
The best way to predict the future
isn't to guess it —
it's to build it with your own hands.

There's no answer key after graduation,
because this time,
you're the one writing the questions.
en.editorNote: For a graduation card or a graduate feeling lost, used to exams having a correct answer; it turns the anxiety into agency, reminding them that from here on they set their own questions
variations: Grades used to be decided by the test. Now you decide which test to even take.
The future comes with no answer key — which is exactly why no way you write it counts as wrong.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
終於畢業了。

現在我手上這張，
大概是全世界最貴的一張紙。

它不能拿去付房租，
但至少可以裱起來，
每天提醒我曾經繳過那麼多錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在IG畢業照下面，或傳給一起背學貸的同學；把「拿到證書」的驕傲跟「花了一堆錢」的現實放在一起，比單純炫耀更好笑也更有共鳴
variations: 有人問這張紙值多少，我看了看帳單說：比你想的多，比它看起來的少。
四年下來我學到最貴的一課，就是這張紙有多貴。

en.content:
Finally graduated.

And now I'm holding
probably the most expensive piece of paper on earth.

It won't cover rent,
but at least I can frame it
to remind myself daily how much I paid for it.
en.editorNote: Perfect under an Instagram grad photo or sent to a classmate carrying the same student loans; pairing the pride of the diploma with the reality of the price tag lands funnier and more relatable than a straight flex
variations: Someone asked what this paper is worth. I looked at the bill and said: more than you think, less than it looks.
The most expensive lesson of four years was learning exactly how expensive this paper is.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人問我大學到底學到什麼，

我說：學會怎麼在期限前一天，
把一整個學期的東西讀完。

這技能出社會之後，
其實比課本有用多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給準備進職場的同學，或貼在畢業限動；自嘲熬夜趕死線的黑歷史，同時偷偷點出這其實是最實用的生存技能，超好轉發
variations: 我沒有拿到好成績，但我拿到了在最後一刻絕地逆轉的博士學位。
課本教的我都忘了，但「一夜之間學會一切」這招我會用一輩子。

en.content:
People ask what I actually learned in college.

I say: how to cram an entire semester
into the night before the deadline.

Turns out that skill
is way more useful out here than any textbook.
en.editorNote: Send to a classmate about to start work, or drop it on your grad story; roasting your all-nighter habit while quietly admitting it's the real survival skill makes it endlessly shareable
variations: I didn't graduate with honors. I graduated with a PhD in last-minute comebacks.
Forgot everything the textbooks taught me. But 'learn it all overnight'? That one's for life.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們都說「以後要常聯絡」。

但其實心裡都清楚，
以後不會再有「每天」了。

所以那句常聯絡，
不是承諾，
是我捨不得說再見的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在畢業典禮後傳給最好的朋友，或配畢業合照貼IG；戳破「常聯絡」這句客套話背後的真心，比直接說捨不得更讓人鼻酸
variations: 「有空約」是我們這種人，最溫柔也最無力的道別。
不是我們變了，是那個能天天見面的時代，先走了。

en.content:
We all say "let's keep in touch."

But deep down we both know
there won't be an "every day" anymore.

So that "keep in touch"
isn't a promise —
it's just my way of not saying goodbye.
en.editorNote: For your closest friend after the ceremony, or under a grad group photo; naming the quiet truth behind the throwaway line 'keep in touch' hits harder than simply saying you'll miss them
variations: "We should hang out sometime" is the gentlest, most helpless goodbye people like us know how to give.
It's not that we changed. It's that the era where we saw each other every day quietly left first.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業後最可怕的問題是：
「你接下來要幹嘛？」

而最誠實的答案，
往往是「我不知道」。

但別怕，
所謂不知道，
只是還沒發生的可能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給對前路迷惘的畢業生，或寫進畢業卡片；把「我不知道要幹嘛」這種焦慮翻轉成「充滿可能」，安慰那些還沒找到答案卻覺得自己落後的人
variations: 不知道方向，不代表迷路，有時候只是這條路還沒被你走出來。
答不出「以後要幹嘛」沒關係，人生本來就不是一場面試。

en.content:
The scariest question after graduation is:
"So what's next for you?"

And the most honest answer
is usually "I don't know."

But don't be afraid —
"I don't know"
is just possibility that hasn't happened yet.
en.editorNote: For a graduate feeling lost about the road ahead, or written inside a grad card; flipping the anxiety of 'I have no idea' into 'full of possibility' comforts the ones who feel behind for not having an answer yet
variations: Not knowing the direction doesn't mean you're lost — sometimes the road just hasn't been walked into existence yet.
It's okay if you can't answer 'what's next.' Life was never meant to be a job interview.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們把這天叫做「畢業典禮」，
但英文其實叫 commencement——
意思是「開始」。

原來這一天從來不是終點，
而是他們終於放手，
讓你自己上路的那一刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫進畢業致詞或卡片，也很適合配畢業照發限動；用畢業典禮英文原意「開始」點題，把離別的傷感轉成出發的勇氣，格局瞬間打開
variations: 畢業不是把門關上，是有人終於為你把下一扇門推開。
終點線之所以叫終點線，是因為越過它的那一刻，另一場比賽才正要開始。

en.content:
They call today "graduation,"
but the real word for it is commencement —
which means a beginning.

Turns out this day was never the finish line.
It's the moment they finally let go
and let you walk the road yourself.
en.editorNote: Great for a graduation speech or card, and just as good under a grad photo on your story; leaning on the literal meaning of 'commencement' turns the sadness of an ending into the courage of a start, and instantly widens the frame
variations: Graduation isn't a door closing. It's someone finally pushing the next one open for you.
A finish line is only called that because the moment you cross it, a whole new race is about to begin.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這張畢業證書，
證明的不是你多會考試，
而是你有本事把一件難事，
從頭撐到尾。

現在，換一件更難的去撐吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫畢業卡片或致詞開場，也很適合配畢業照發限動；把「文憑」重新定義成「完成困難事的能力證明」，比空泛的祝福更有力量，也更能鼓勵剛畢業、對未來沒把握的人
variations: 文憑證明你能把一件事做完，接下來換你證明自己能重新開始。
你不是撐到畢業而已，你是練成了一種把難事做完的本事。

en.content:
This diploma isn't proof
that you're good at tests.
It's proof you can take something hard
and see it all the way through.

Now go find something harder.
en.editorNote: Works as an opening line for a card or a speech, and just as well under a grad photo; reframing the diploma as evidence you can finish hard things hits deeper than a generic 'congrats', and it lands especially well for new grads who feel unsure about what's next
