variations: The diploma proves you can finish. Now go prove you can begin.
You didn't just survive to graduation. You built the muscle for finishing hard things.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感性語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們一起翹過早八、
熬過考試、笑到肚子痛，
也在半夜互相說過喪氣話。

說再見那麼難，
是因為我們真的把彼此，
活成了青春的一部分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫給同學的畢業留言或畢業紀念冊，也很適合配合照發限動；用具體的共同回憶取代空泛的「友誼長存」，讀起來像在說你們自己的故事，情緒更真也更打動人
variations: 捨不得說再見，是因為你早就成了我青春裡拿不掉的一塊。
我們沒有走散，只是把同一段青春，帶去了不同的地方。

en.content:
We skipped the 8 a.m. classes together,
survived finals together,
laughed till it hurt,
and talked each other off the ledge at 2 a.m.

Goodbye is this hard
because we became a part of
each other's youth.
en.editorNote: Great for a message to a classmate, a yearbook note, or a caption on a group photo; swapping the tired 'friends forever' for specific shared memories makes it read like your actual story, so the emotion feels earned instead of scripted
variations: Saying goodbye is hard because you were never just a friend. You were a piece of my youth.
We're not losing each other. We're just taking the same years to different places.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
學校教你怎麼準備好，
但出社會後你會發現——
沒有一件重要的事，
是等你準備好才發生的。

所以別再等了，
先出發，路上再學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合鼓勵準備踏入職場或還在迷惘的畢業生，也很適合放進致詞；點破「等準備好」其實是一種拖延，把出發的門檻降到最低，讀起來務實又有推力，不會太雞湯
variations: 沒有人是準備好才出發的，大家都是邊走邊把自己補齊。
與其等到萬事俱備，不如先跨出去，剩下的路上再想辦法。

en.content:
School teaches you how to get ready.
Then you graduate and realize
none of the things that matter
wait until you're ready.

So stop waiting.
Start moving, and learn on the way.
en.editorNote: Good for encouraging grads heading into their first job or still figuring things out, and it works in a speech too; calling out 'waiting until you're ready' as just procrastination lowers the bar to start, so it reads practical and motivating rather than sappy
variations: Nobody starts ready. Everybody figures it out on the way.
Waiting to feel ready is just a nicer word for waiting. Go anyway.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
反正做討厭的事也可能會失敗，
那你不如去賭一把你真正愛的。

至少輸了，
你還喜歡自己選的路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給正在為未來方向猶豫、被現實勸退的畢業生，也很適合寫進卡片；用「反正都可能失敗」這個角度鬆開對安穩的執念，讀起來灑脫又帶點膽識，很容易被收藏轉發
variations: 既然穩定的路也沒保證，那就把賭注押在讓你心動的那條上。
選你愛的吧，就算跌倒，你也是為自己想要的東西跌的。

en.content:
You can fail at the thing you hate,
so you might as well take the shot
on the thing you love.

At least if it doesn't work out,
you'll still like the road you picked.
en.editorNote: Meant for grads stuck between what's safe and what they actually want, and it fits nicely in a card too; framing it as 'you can fail either way' loosens the grip of playing it safe, so it reads bold and a little freeing, the kind of line people screenshot
variations: The safe path isn't guaranteed either, so bet on the one that makes your heart move.
Choose what you love. Even if you fall, at least you fell for something that was yours.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你以為畢業是拿到一張證書，
其實你拿到的是一整個
沒有標準答案的世界。

別怕，
你早就練成了自己找答案的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當致詞收尾或畢業卡片的結語，也很適合配畢業合照發文；點出「出社會沒有標準答案」的焦慮，再用一句肯定把它接住，先共鳴再打氣，情緒起伏收得剛好
variations: 從今天起沒有標準答案了，但你本來就很會自己想辦法。
畢業給你的不是答案，是一輩子自己找答案的膽量。

en.content:
You thought graduation
meant getting a certificate.
What you actually got
was a whole world with no answer key.

Don't panic.
You already became someone
who finds their own answers.
en.editorNote: Great as a closing line for a speech or a card, and it works over a grad photo too; it names the fear that the real world has no answer key, then catches it with reassurance, so you get the resonance first and the encouragement second, with the emotional arc landing just right
variations: There's no answer key from here on out, but you were always good at figuring it out yourself.
Graduation didn't hand you the answers. It handed you the nerve to find your own.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自2026畢業IG短caption合集（tassel/plot twist系列），改寫為台灣口語短句版本

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：我居然畢業了。

流下的是汗，還是淚，
連我自己也分不清楚，
反正證書到手就對了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業合照的IG短caption，走「連自己都不敢相信」的反差感，比正經祝賀更適合貼在限動配丟帽照
variations: 帽穗甩過去的那一秒，之前受的苦突然全都值得了。
成績單叫我謙虛一點，我說先讓我畢業再說。

en.content:
Plot twist: I actually graduated.

Couldn't tell you
if that was sweat or tears,
but the diploma's in my hand
so we're calling it a win.
en.editorNote: A short Instagram caption for your grad photo, leaning into the I-can't-believe-it-either angle — better for a story with a cap-toss shot than a formal congrats
variations: The second that tassel flipped, every rough night suddenly felt worth it.
My GPA said stay humble. I said let me graduate first.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「I came. I napped. I graduated somehow.」系列搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本

zh-tw.content:
我來了。
我睡了。
我不知道為什麼就畢業了。

三件事之間有沒有因果關係，
目前查無實據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給一起翹課、一起補眠、一起低空飛過的同學，廢話文學風把「勉強畢業」寫得又爽又好笑
variations: 全部撐過來了。雖然是低空飛過，但低空也是飛過。
我到底怎麼畢業的，這題連我教授都答不出來。

en.content:
I showed up.
I took naps.
Somehow I graduated.

Whether those three things
are related
remains under investigation.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend you skipped class, caught up on sleep, and barely passed with — a deadpan take that makes squeaking by sound like a flex
variations: Survived all of it. Barely. But barely still counts as surviving.
How I actually graduated is a question even my professor can't answer.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「未來屬於相信夢想之美的人」名句，改寫為台灣口語勵志版本

zh-tw.content:
未來不會發給那些等在原地的人，
它只留給
還願意相信自己想像的人。

你腦袋裡那個「有一天」，
從今天開始，
可以慢慢變成「正在」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當畢業卡片或致詞裡打氣的一段，把「相信夢想」寫得不空泛——重點放在「把想像動手做成現在進行式」，比純喊口號更有力
variations: 未來是留給還敢想的人的，剩下的只是把它一步一步做出來。
你心裡那個「總有一天」，其實可以從今天開始倒數。

en.content:
The future doesn't go
to the ones waiting around.
It goes to the ones
still willing to trust
what they can imagine.

That "someday" in your head—
starting today,
it can slowly turn into "right now."
en.editorNote: Works as an uplifting line in a card or a speech; it grounds the trust-your-dreams idea by turning imagination into a present-tense verb, so it hits harder than a slogan
variations: The future belongs to the ones still brave enough to dream. The rest is just building it, step by step.
That "someday" you keep talking about? You can start the countdown today.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「友誼不會畢業，記得常聯絡」祝福短句，改寫為台灣口語感人版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業的是我們，
不是我們之間。

以後可能很久才見一次面，
但那種一開口就接得上話的默契，
不會因為沒同班就過期。

記得回訊息，就這樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業後傳給要各奔東西的死黨，或貼在合照下面；把「友情不會畢業」寫得具體——重點是那種「一開口就接得上」的默契，不落俗套
variations: 校服會收進衣櫃，但我們之間那種話說一半就懂的默契，不用收。
我們散在不同城市，但只要一句『欸最近怎樣』，就又坐回原本那張桌子。

en.content:
We're the ones graduating.
Not the thing between us.

We might go a long time between hangouts,
but that pick-up-mid-sentence kind of ease
doesn't expire
just because we're not classmates anymore.

Just text back. That's all.
en.editorNote: Send to the best friend heading a different direction after graduation, or drop it under your photo together; it makes friendship-doesn't-graduate concrete by naming that finish-each-other's-sentences ease
variations: The uniform goes in the closet, but the way we get each other with half a sentence — that stays out.
We'll be scattered across cities, but one "hey, how've you been" and we're right back at that same table.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「別誤以為畢業就是終點，這才是起點」祝福語，改寫為台灣口語勵志版本

zh-tw.content:
別把畢業當成終點線衝過去，
以為過了就結束。

它比較像
把你一直待著的房間門打開，
外面很大，也很吵，
但那條路，
終於輪到你自己選要往哪走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當致詞或畢業卡片的開場或收尾，翻轉「畢業=結束」的既定印象，用「開門」的畫面把不安轉成期待，情緒收得溫暖不說教
variations: 畢業不是把一本書闔上，是翻到那頁空白的，等你自己寫。
你不是走到路的盡頭，你是終於走到那個可以自己選路口的地方。

en.content:
Don't sprint through graduation
like it's a finish line,
like crossing it means it's over.

It's more like
someone opening the door
to the room you've been in this whole time.
Out there is big, and loud,
but for once,
which way you go
is finally yours to pick.
en.editorNote: Good as an opener or closer for a speech or card; it flips the graduation-equals-ending assumption, using the image of an opening door to turn unease into anticipation without lecturing
variations: Graduation isn't closing a book. It's turning to the blank page that's waiting for you to write it.
You didn't reach the end of the road. You reached the part where you finally get to choose the turn.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「They said I could be anything, so I became tired」搞笑畢業梗，改寫為台灣口語版

zh-tw.content:
大人從小跟我說：
只要努力，你想成為什麼都可以。

所以我很努力，
最後成功變成了一個
非常疲憊的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業季貼IG限動自嘲，或傳給同樣累到不行的同學；比正經的祝福更能戳中剛出社會前的迷惘
variations: 他們說我可以成為任何人，於是我選擇成為需要睡飽的人。
夢想清單第一項：先把覺補回來。

en.content:
Growing up, everyone told me
I could become anything I wanted.

So I worked really hard,
and I'm proud to say
I successfully became
extremely tired.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a self-deprecating grad-season Instagram Story, or to send a classmate who's equally exhausted; lands harder than a polished blessing for anyone dreading the real world
variations: They said I could be anyone, so I became someone who needs a nap.
Item one on the dream list: catch up on sleep.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「感謝維基百科、複製貼上」的搞笑畢業致謝梗，更新為AI時代版本並改寫

zh-tw.content:
畢業致詞，
我想先感謝幾位一路陪我走到今天的貴人：

Google、維基百科、
熬夜的咖啡、
還有發明「複製貼上」的那位天才。

沒有你們，就沒有現在的我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業影片、班級群組或IG貼文用來搞笑；把嚴肅的致謝翻轉成大家心照不宣的真實，越唸越有共鳴
variations: 特別感謝深夜的便利商店，收留過每一個報告寫不完的我。
得獎感言：這座學位，我要獻給複製、貼上、還有 Ctrl+Z。

en.content:
For my graduation speech,
I'd like to thank the people who truly got me here:

Google, Wikipedia,
the coffee that kept me up all night,
and whoever invented copy and paste.

I couldn't have done it without you.
en.editorNote: Great for a grad video, class group chat, or a joke caption; it flips the earnest thank-you into the truth everyone quietly knows, and gets funnier the longer it goes
variations: Special thanks to the 24-hour convenience store that took me in every time a paper was due.
Acceptance speech: I dedicate this degree to copy, paste, and Ctrl+Z.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Lin-Manuel Miranda「你正要進入人生最不確定也最刺激的階段」與「學會與不確定共舞」，改寫為溫暖語錄

zh-tw.content:
親愛的畢業生，
我知道你有點怕。

因為接下來這段路，
是你人生中最不確定、
卻也最令人心跳加速的一段。

別急著把答案想清楚，
先學會跟不確定一起往前走——
那才是你真正長大的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當畢業致詞或卡片裡的一段，安撫那些對未來焦慮的畢業生；把「害怕」正常化，再溫柔地把它翻成期待
variations: 不確定不是敵人，它只是還沒被你走成路的地方。
沒有人一開始就知道要去哪，你只是比較誠實地承認了而已。

en.content:
Dear graduate,
I know you're a little scared.

Because what comes next
is the most uncertain,
and the most heart-racing,
stretch of your whole life.

Don't rush to have every answer figured out.
Learn to keep walking alongside the uncertainty —
that's what growing up actually looks like.
en.editorNote: Works as a passage in a commencement speech or card to steady graduates who are anxious about the future; it normalizes the fear, then gently turns it into anticipation
variations: Uncertainty isn't the enemy. It's just ground you haven't walked into a path yet.
Nobody starts out knowing where they're going. You were just honest enough to admit it.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Arthur Ashe「從你所在的地方開始，用你擁有的，做你能做的」，改寫為畢業起步語錄

zh-tw.content:
不用等一切都準備好，
不用等你變成理想中的自己。

就從你現在站的地方開始，
用你手上已經有的東西，
做你今天能做到的那一點點。

路，是這樣一步一步走出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給覺得「還沒準備好出社會」的畢業生，破除完美主義的拖延；短句有力，也很適合當IG畢業貼文文案
variations: 別等萬事俱備，那一天永遠不會到。今天能做的，就先做。
起點不是等來的，是你決定站起來的那一刻。

en.content:
You don't have to wait
until everything is ready,
or until you've become the person you imagine.

Start from exactly where you're standing,
with exactly what's already in your hands,
and do the small thing you can do today.

That's how a road gets walked — one step at a time.
en.editorNote: For graduates who feel they're not ready for the real world; it dismantles perfectionist procrastination, and the punchy lines make a great grad Instagram caption too
variations: Don't wait until everything's in place — that day never comes. Do what you can today.
A starting line isn't something you wait for. It's the moment you decide to stand up.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「載你回過去的是回憶，載你去未來的是夢想，讓你完成的是現在」畢業祝福，改寫為溫暖版

zh-tw.content:
其實每個人都有一台時光機。

載你回到過去的，叫回憶；
帶你奔向未來的，叫夢想；
而真正能把這兩件事接起來的，
只有你現在的每一天。

畢業快樂，記得好好活在現在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片結尾或當致詞收尾，把懷舊、期待與當下三個時間點串成一句祝福，溫暖又不落俗套
variations: 回憶收好，夢想帶著，剩下的交給每一個認真過的今天。
過去謝謝你，未來等等我，現在，我先好好走這一步。

en.editorNote: Perfect to close a graduation card or a speech; it weaves nostalgia, hope, and the present into one blessing — warm without being a cliché
variations: Keep the memories, carry the dreams, and leave the rest to every day you show up for.
Thank you, past. Wait for me, future. For now, I'll just walk this one step well.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「The tassel was worth the hassle」畢業搞笑金句，改寫成中文順口版

zh-tw.content:
熬夜、報告、被當、重修、
跟教授裝熟、跟死線賽跑——

這一切的終點，
就是把帽子上那條穗子
從右邊撥到左邊。

值得嗎？值得。
划算嗎？完全不划算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當畢業典禮撥穗那張照片的IG文案，自嘲又不失喜感；把四年的辛苦濃縮成一個小動作，反差感就是笑點
variations: 四年的努力，換來一秒鐘的撥穗。這CP值，我給負分，但我甘願。
別人問我大學學到什麼，我說：學到那條穗子要往哪邊撥。

en.content:
All-nighters, group projects, that one class you retook,
pretending to know the professor, racing every deadline —

and the grand finale of it all
is moving a little string on your hat
from the right side to the left.

Worth it? Yeah. Efficient? Absolutely not.
en.editorNote: Perfect caption for the tassel-turning photo — self-deprecating but warm; compressing four years into one tiny gesture is the whole joke
variations: Four years of effort for one second of turning a tassel. Terrible ROI. Would do it again.
People ask what I learned in college. I learned which side the tassel goes on.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「free to be broke in a more educated way」畢業自嘲金句，改寫

zh-tw.content:
恭喜畢業。

從今天起，
你正式從「沒錢的學生」
升級成「有學歷但還是沒錢的大人」。

進步的地方是：
現在你窮得比較有文化了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給剛畢業還在找工作、或第一份薪水很微薄的朋友，用自嘲的方式化解畢業後的經濟焦慮，貼IG也很有共鳴
variations: 畢業不會讓你變有錢，只會讓你欠學貸欠得更理直氣壯。
以前是窮學生，現在是有證書的窮人。人生就是不斷解鎖新頭銜。

en.content:
Congratulations on graduating.

As of today,
you've officially upgraded
from 'broke student'
to 'broke adult with a diploma.'

The improvement is:
you're now broke in a much more educated way.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still job-hunting or surviving on a starter salary — it defuses post-grad money anxiety with a laugh, and reads great on Instagram too
variations: Graduation doesn't make you rich. It just makes your student loans feel more justified.
Used to be a broke student. Now I'm broke with credentials. Life is just unlocking new titles.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「為你已達成的里程碑感到自豪，對你將前往的遠方抱持信心，別忘了享受旅程」畢業祝福，改寫為溫暖版

zh-tw.content:
為你已經走到的這裡，我替你驕傲。
對你即將前往的遠方，我替你有信心。

但最想跟你說的是：
別急著趕路，
記得抬頭看看沿途的風景。

終點會等你，
但風景不會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在畢業卡片、或當致詞的溫暖收尾，把「肯定過去、祝福未來、珍惜當下」三層意思疊在一起，不落俗套又有重量
variations: 驕傲你走過的，期待你要去的，也捨不得你錯過現在的。
願你有奔向遠方的勇氣，也有停下來看風景的從容。

en.content:
I'm proud of how far you've already come.
I have faith in the distance you're about to go.

But what I most want to say is this:
don't rush the road —
remember to look up at the view along the way.

The finish line will wait for you.
The scenery won't.
en.editorNote: Ideal for a graduation card or the warm close of a speech — it layers pride in the past, faith in the future, and a nudge to savor the present, without slipping into cliché
variations: Proud of where you've been, excited for where you're headed, and rooting for you to notice where you are.
May you have the courage to chase the horizon, and the ease to stop and enjoy the view.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自畢業帽標語「I did it for the WiFi / Thanks Mom & Dad's wallet」，改寫成中文畢業帽金句組合

zh-tw.content:
畢業帽上寫什麼，決定了你是哪種人：

「我是為了WiFi才來的。」
「感謝爸媽的錢包。」
「終於可以合法睡到自然醒。」
「學貸：故事未完待續。」

選一句，戴上去，畢業快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給正在煩惱畢業帽要寫什麼的人一份靈感清單，每句都能直接抄；貼IG當畢業帽DIY靈感也很好用
variations: 畢業帽的字數有限，但你的厭世可以無限。
帽子上寫什麼不重要，重要的是你真的畢業了（可能吧）。

en.content:
What you write on your grad cap says everything:

"I only came for the WiFi."
"Thanks, Mom & Dad's wallet."
"Finally legal to sleep in."
"Student loans: to be continued."

Pick one, wear it proudly, happy graduation.
en.editorNote: A ready-to-steal idea list for anyone stuck on what to put on their grad cap — great as Instagram inspiration for DIY cap decorating too
variations: Your grad cap has a word limit. Your existential dread does not.
Doesn't matter what the cap says. What matters is you actually graduated (probably).

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「輕輕的我們相聚一起，無限歡聲笑語；輕輕的我們又要分離」畢業離別語，改寫為現代口語版

zh-tw.content:
我們是輕輕地相遇的。

一起笑、一起熬夜、一起在群組裡吵著要不要翹課。
然後有一天，
也要輕輕地分開了。

沒有轟轟烈烈，
但那些一起浪費過的時間，
後來都變成了最捨不得的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合畢業季傳給朝夕相處的同學或室友，或搭配合照貼IG；用「輕輕」這個詞把離別寫得溫柔，不煽情卻很戳心
variations: 我們沒有轟轟烈烈的告別，只有一句「那……先這樣囉」，然後各自走進不一樣的人生。
最珍貴的從來不是那些大事，是我們一起浪費掉的每一個平凡下午。

en.content:
We came together quietly.

Laughed together, pulled all-nighters together,
argued in the group chat about whether to skip class.
And then one day,
we'll part just as quietly.

Nothing dramatic.
But all that time we wasted together
became the thing we can't bear to let go of.
en.editorNote: For the classmates or roommates you saw every single day — send it at graduation or pair it with a group photo; the softness of a quiet goodbye lands harder than anything loud
variations: We never had a dramatic goodbye — just a 'well… guess this is it,' and then two different lives.
The most precious things were never the big moments. They were the ordinary afternoons we wasted together.
